We had a wonderful experience at El Cielo! The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming — they speak several languages, which made everything so easy and comfortable. We ordered the beef, the burger, and the ceviche — and honestly, everything was amazing. Each dish was full of flavor and beautifully presented. I highly recommend trying all of them!

A great spot for anyone looking for delicious food, warm service, and a relaxed atmosphere in Stockholm.

Absolute gem! I came back twice in the same week because the tapas were so good. The meat platter is excellent, and the staff makes the whole experience even better. Don’t miss this place if you’re in Stockholm!

Nice place with a good selection of tapas — everything was fresh and tasty. It does feel a bit overpriced, but you can book through The Fork to get a significant discount. The sangria was very good too!  I also really loved the beautiful and aesthetic tableware — it made the experience even nicer.

Delicious food, everything fresh and very good. I recommend everyone to give it a try. The place has a warm, family atmosphere and the staff are very friendly. One of the best Thai experiences.

Greetings from Poland — we’d love to come back someday!

Loved our meal at the Elephant Thai.

Delicious, tasty food, served to order.  And a friendly welcome on a chilly night.

I loved the Crispy pork belly with dew cabbage and the Pad Thai didn’t disappoint.

Thank you!

Friendly lady who makes you feel cozy and homey and just like an aunt you know for a long time 😆
(not sure if she is the owner (?))
So many dishes I wanna try after reading the reviews here and ended up going for pad Thai. Yummy!
Overall love it and I wish I could have more time in Stockholm so I’d pay another visit
The Thai food here is super authentic! I totally love the river prawn tom yum goong. There's a ton of variety too, you really should check it out yourself.

The food was really good, and tasted like authentic Thai food. The place has a small and local feeling, which is nice. The service was good, and we felt very welcomed. Also my Thai girlfriend approved and had a very nice chat with the staff! We really enjoyed this restaurant and highly recommend for people who want to eat authentic Thai food!

we came here for brunch and the experience was very disappointed. it is not really a brunch as there were just several specific things prepared: no cheese, no bread. On the other hand avocado on every creation and you need to pay extra for individual coffee. last but not least, all people arrive at the same time which makes it feel overcrowded and not very comfty. The brunch lives of tiktok, unfortunately it is not much more, even the idea is cool and well deserved.

AMAZING!! There Açaí bowls were amazing and I recommend trying the banana bread! Service was great,
Everyone was kind and made you feel welcome + it was so cute that some sparrows flew in and visited 🥹🩷
I visited as a tourist and wasn’t very familiar with how to order, staff were all so kind, friendly, and helpful in guiding me. The food was clean, fresh, and healthy 🫶

Super cute and cozy place in hammarby. They have their weekend brunch famous. It is pretty full on weekend, must book before u go. There were a bunch of options: sandwich, hummus, scrambled eggs etc. If you wanna go for alacarte, then options are very limited for weekends. Green smoothie was awesome.

Very cozy interior! Had the brunch bufe. The food tastes good, but the majority are quite carb heavy, many sandwiches and croissants. But again, they taste great!
For me it is too pricey for what it's worth. For 360 kr I'd like to see more options and maybe a bit more complex dishes. But that is my very personal opinion...

You can also book the venue for private events.

They also serve alcohol.

The weekend brunch is quite popular, so better to book in advance!

Sometimes I come there untill my kid finish the class from Kulturskolan(next door)!, I really like their pommes( French fries) are so delicious! But sadly they not have much choices for beeers, mostly Swedish beers, they don’t match me!, (normally they gives me little kinda allergic “hard to breathe to the next day, or gives me bumpy or sticky eyes for the next morning! 🤷🏻‍♀️
great local place!! super friendly staff and pretty good food. also they've upgraded the bathroom!

Loved the pepper steak.
Comes in 2 different sizes 200g and 300g. Only wish the veggies were slightly bigger portion but besides that it is really tasty food.

Too bad!!

It's a nice place and they seem to be trying to make it better. But avoid eating the overpriced sallad. It's a 140 kr for a few green sallad leaves and not much else. Embarrassing and disappointing to be honest.

They don't seem to understanding when it comes to suggestions either.

Could be a nice place, but they need to check their prices, their menu and dishes, and perhaps their motivation as well.
Super good, we had a perfect service. I recommend you the grilled chicken sándwich, it's awesome. Also good prices 10/10

name: Engelen

googleReviews.text:
Engelen was an awesome bar to visit in Stockholm, situated near the waterfront in Gamla Stan, the exterior is very inviting with pretty lights and great vibe.

The bar is quite large, with the entrance being situated on the side of the building that leads you to the first room which is in the cellar. This comes with its own bar and dancefloor.

Going up the stairs leads you to the main room where live performances take place. The bar consists of dark brown wood and looks very aesthetic.

The staff were friendly, the alcohol was great and the performances were also good. A great place to visit to relax and enjoy!

Very friendly pub style restaurant 😁 There is a stage in the front for live music and seating throughout the back. The server was super friendly and even made a really good joke at the end of our meal lol
The food was delicious 🤌🤌The atmosphere was closer to a tavern style and not a restaurant which was a nice change of pace. Unfortunately we didn't catch the live music that was happening later on in the evening, so next time we are in town it will have to be a stop for my partner and I 🤘
Plus they have really cool hat lights 😏😏

They played really well!
Super professional and perfectly in sync!
This is by far one of my favorite bars from my 20's.  Great food and music as well.  The Plankstek is a must 😋
Food is good, but service slow and a bit hustle. Sometimes it was hard to find waiter.

googleEditorialSummary: Lively, rustic tavern offering traditional Swedish dishes, plus live cover bands & cellar nightclub.

googleReviews.text: Excellent food and great ambience. Clear demonstration that care is taken at every step. Ingredients well balanced, sauces made from scratch and balanced food. Great value food and would strongly recommend!
Loved the food and service. I can recommend the "skaldjur soppa" / sea food soup with salmon, it was delicious as shown in the picture.
Great food and the best staff one could think of 🫶🏼

Delicious food, great service, and really comfortable and clean spot to have lunch or dinner with friends. Loved the Skagen, specialty cocktails are yummy, and the place is never too busy. Really worth checking it out if you want something really good away from the busy parts of Stockholm.
Great restaurant and bar, with outdoor seating, so nice to have something new in the area 👌

Super nice place to visit with good weather. The food is fresh and delicious. The ambient family and pet friendly in a nice quiet area.
Had the spicy tuna sandwich that comes with a little salad, and the caprese salad. I highly recommend this place.

Very welcoming place with deliciuos food and drink alternatives. The personnel are great! They have seating options in the little red house also serving outside while its not snowing or raining. Keep in mind that during lunch time you may wait for few minutes to find a table .

Enskedeparkens bageri is a super nice bakery and cafe serving delicious pastries, grilled sandwiches and soups. It’s location is amazing with beautiful surroundings like a park, flower garden and Enskede Gård. You may enjoy your food inside or outside. The staff is very friendly and helpful. Parking is available just a few meters away. I highly recommend a visit to this cafe.
Super cute cozy cafe in Enskede. It’s usually crowded and serve nice sandwiches, coffee, hot chocolate etc. They have sitting both inside and outside for summers. Loved it that it small and cute

Truly nice bakery. Quality bread and buns. Tasty grilled sandwiches and soup lunches. In summer there are tables and chairs outside so then it's always possible to find a seat. In winter however it can be very crowded - especially at lunch our and for the Friday pizzas. It's a bit pricey - like all Swedish bakeries.
Our friendly neighborhood pizzeria. Reasonable prices, friendly staff and tasty pizzas.
Your typical pizza place, but nicer.
Tasty food! Sat outside, had a pasta curry with chicken and a black and white plate with French fries and Bearnaisesause. Good taste on both and very large portions.

Ordered kebab pizza and altonno. Only thing I can say here is that kebab pizza won't come with sallad on top, for that you need to order the pizza called devran if I am not mistaken!
Otherwise, great staff, great pizza
Amazing pan pizza 👌👌

I just attended a two-day conference at Ersta Konferens and had the pleasure of dining at Ersta Terrass, the on-site restaurant. Situated atop Erstaklippan in Södermalm, the restaurant offers breathtaking views over Stockholm’s inlet, including sights of Gamla Stan, Skeppsholmen, and Gröna Lund . ￼ ￼

Ersta Terrass is part of Ersta Diakoni, a non-profit organization that encompasses a hospital, church, hotel, and various social services . The restaurant serves a lunch buffet on weekdays from 11:00 to 14:00, featuring classic dishes with global influences, prepared with care and high-quality ingredients . The spacious outdoor terrace provides a serene atmosphere, making it a nice spot for a meal or coffee break (the coffee actually gets only 3 stars)

Despite its central location, the prices are really reasonable, especially considering the quality of food and the stunning views. The restaurant is easily accessible, with bus 53 stopping just a four-minute walk away, providing convenient transport to the central station.

Overall, Ersta Terrass offers a unique dining experience that combines good food, a peaceful setting, and a meaningful connection to the community through its affiliation with Ersta Diakoni.

I truly enjoyed this terrace! This place has a wonderful view of Djurgården, Old town and other islands. I was there a Thursday late afternoon and had no problem finding a free spot. The staff were all very kind and sweet, especially the young girl behind the counter.
They do t have too many options in terms of what to drink, however they have all the classic options and a few drinks/cocktails.
I would certainly recommend going here if you’d like to enjoy the sunset, great staff and a glass of wine/beer.

Nice place and view. Bad service at the bar. I askes for som non-alcoholic drink and they only had water and Coca-Cola to offer. I don’t know if the lady at the bar didn’t bother to go and bring fanta or lemonade or whatever juice that I asked for or it was really nothing left at the whole restaurant and bar!!!
As a result I sat at the terrass with 10 other people with a glass of water to drink.

Amazing hidden oasis with a view to die for. Beautiful historic building. Excellent food, the lunch buffet is SEK100 (Summer 2019) with two mains to choose from, including delicious home baked cookies and coffee. Last time we ate we got some perfectly steamed fresh fish. Also the raspberry pie was delicious, you can have just coffee here if you don’t want to eat. Can’t be beat, seriously. And maybe most importantly SUPER nice staff, very friendly!

One of the best places to enjoy beautiful sunset or just Stockholm view! The terrace is cute and cozy, and there are different food trucks throughout the summer — so you can explore other cuisines.
In the evening, most of the tables are taken, but you can still find a place for yourself.
Good classic cocktail options, although not so many of them, reasonable pricing and friendly staff!

name: Esperia

googleReviews.text:
My poor planning and a rumbling stomach made me pick the closest place I could find. Unfortunately, it was February 14th, so it was fully booked, but the manager called someone who had reserved a table and didn’t show up, and managed to squeeze us in.
Honestly, it was a great coincidence that we ended up eating there. The service was excellent, the food was delicious, and my medium-rare steak was perfect. The potatoes were also amazing.
I really like this place.

Food is for sure some of the best greek I've tried in Stockholm. The restaurant is more fine dining than towards the classic greek taverna style.
Not only is food splendid, both service and atmosphere of the restaurant is great, all the waiters are super nice and gladly share a joke quickly!

We will come back many times in the future!

The food is still really good quality – just like it was four years ago when we first visited this place during our trip to Stockholm. However, things felt a bit unorganised when we arrived this time. We had a booking, and upon arrival, we were asked if we had one and gave our name. The waiter went to check, came back but didn’t say anything to us, and instead seated the couple in front of us. Then another waiter came and asked again if we had a booking. It was a bit strange and confusing, but once we were seated, everything went smoothly and the food was as delicious as we remembered.

Had the chicken skewers (majskyckling) which were made out of very high quality, juicy chicken and came with delicious tzatziki and crunchy fried potatoes. The restaurant was cozy and the service was friendly!

Hands down the best Greek restaurant I’ve ever been to! I appreciated the vegetarian options since I often don’t get something proper at other Greek restaurants. I absolutely loved the food. The staff was super nice, great service. Would definitely recommend, only a bit pricey ;)

googleEditorialSummary: A contemporary, rustic-chic restaurant for traditional Greek cuisine like souvlaki, moussaka & meze.

googleReviews.text: Really lovely and friendly staff, and coffee was really good! Food was also a good standard, and liked that the muffin was warmed up. We waited a while for our order but there was only one member of staff working, so we didn’t mind! Wished we’d discovered this place earlier in our trip
The have the best Chai Latte in the country
Spent my time here for a breakfast. The meal was decent and the staff was nice. However, the space feels a bit cramped and there was a fly infestation that made the experience uncomfortable.

Amazing Swedish pastries and a waitress is really nice to advice you. The americano is not excellent but good for the price ! This pastry was amazing but I couldn’t remember the name, it was filled with almond that’s the only thing I remember ah ah !
Nice staff and large coffee cups! Loved every single thing I ever bought here!

I had an hot chocolate and I had to ask for another one because the first drink was not done fully and had only the Coffe grains (hot). This did burn my tongue but I lucky for another one. This one was better and tastier. The service was alright. The atmosphere is like all other expressions houses they are well designed

A great place to spend an afternoon after a hectic day of work. Extra cozy when you get to sit in the sofa. Otherwise, take a seat near a window. The view towards the street is beautiful especially when it's cloudy and/or raining. For these reasons, this espresso house stands out from the others for me.
Good strong coffee and a pretty raspberry cheesecake.  Cozy atmosphere, lots of wooden surfaces.  Don't nitpick - it's just a cafe for coffee with friends, it's not the Ritz 😀
Wasn’t expecting the cappuccino to taste this good from a coffee shop chain with so many shops. Pleasantly surprised, would recommend
Great coffee and pastry. In my opinion it's better than starbucks

Industrial design, quiet and cozy. A lot of options for breakfast - a variety of sandwiches, croissants and sweets. They have matcha latte and all kinds of coffee drinks. On the main square right next to the central station in Stockholm - easy to find and open early. Win- win!
Best cafe house with amazing hot chocolate. Service is really nice with lot of information provided. You can also find vegan options available here.

This is the best Espresso in Stockholm I'd recommend for most especially busy people and travellers since it's just right in the corner next to the train station with amazing view of Stockholm city center. However, they don't have caramel cheese cake which is sad. They used to have it before but not anymore. In fact, I can't find my favorite caramel cheese cake anywhere in most of Espresso Houses!

This is a great spot for a coffee break, often referred to as FIKA. Located in the pedestrian zone, this cafe offers a warm atmosphere and comfortable seating. You can choose from a variety of coffee options and enjoy sandwiches and cakes. It's a perfect place to chat with friends, unwind alone, or simply read a book.
Ordered vanilla latte and chai latte as well as pastries. All were delicious! Great service and beautiful environment.

Food is normal in all Expresso Houses, I like the different seasons of coffee. But the reason we always end up coming to this particular store it’s because everyone in the service is so nice to us, and thank god they speak English (thank you) but honestly everyone so nice it’s a pleasure to come here (even if there is only one toilet 😂)

I have always had a wonderful experience with espresso house in finland and one of my favourite places to be, however this one, not at all living up to standards of espresso house. The tables and utensils were dirty, needed deep cleaning. We informed the staff at the counter, and they said they don't have staff. Okay, understood.

We ordered latte, espresso and cookie, they said they don't have medium size available, and we need to take the larger one, we took that, it was so diluted and cold. Terrible experience. Moving further we saw many other espresso house, regretted visiting this one, total waste of money and terrible service.

One of my favorite locations while in the city and somewhere between Gamla Stan and Central station. Cozy dark interior, huge windows open during summer. Big variety of pastries and drinks, love the boosters. Prices typical for Stockholm I’d say.
Hot choc for SEK61. Though the hot choc is so so, i want to compliment the lady at the counter. She was fantastic and friendly.

It is a cozy cafe. But I would like to warn you that keep your eyes on your personal stuff, especially your bag pack. Mine has been stolen within 5mins (literally just put it on the sofa and turn back to reply a message and it’s gone)

The coffee beans are really premium and quality of service
I used to drink my coffee from this place while heading to my work

A nice place for a coffee break with the family.
Nice sitting area and cozy environment.
Espresso House has one of the best coffee house loyalty programs. Price are on par with other coffee houses in Stockholm.
The café was super good,I like the personal they was very kind.Very big shoes av deserts and sandwich.The atmosphere chill and cute like in dream.I would come next time!

Large and well decorated espresso house. Staff was welcoming and friendly. Food was not bad, pretty standard for the chain.

The seating area was cozy, but needed a bit of cleaning and tidying here and there. Not sure if that means more staff or requiring staff to be more proactive in making cleaning rounds.

Nothing special with food in Espresso House because they all have the same food. Hence the 3 stars.
This particular place have a clean montern, the food look clean and beautiful. The sitting places are clean and in a good order. The working space also look clean and in an order.
It such a sad tho, that Espresso House still don't want to put money to hire more staff.  the staff work alone with plenty customers lines up during rush hour, a lunch time.

The passion power smoothie is to die for. Good place to sit and just enjoy your life really! I was so happy they opened here so I don't have to go so far to get a good smoothie. Of course a bit expensive which is sad but I think its worth it!

Espresso House is a Swedish coffee shop chain that is currently the largest in the Nordic region with over 500 coffee shops in Sweden, Norway, Finland, Denmark and Germany. Espresso House is visited by approximately 100,000 guests daily.

Espresso House Sweden AB is owned by JAB Holding Company, which is a German-owned family business registered in Luxembourg.
Like other espresso house outlets, people are very nice and friendly. Good location near metro and shopping area
Good coffee shop.  Super cappuccino and delicious muffins.  Recommend

It's not the fanciest place, but I love their cappuccino. Their app often has great deals, and with branches all over Sweden, collecting fika points is easy and useful. The pastries are decent, though occasionally not very fresh. For a meal, I usually go for their BBQ Chicken Bagel.

The takoyaki were really amazing and I loved them so much. The rice bowl was a good mix. The quality of the fish was fine. I really liked the decorations inside the restaurant. The view outside is fantastic and it is definitely enjoyable during the summer. There are outdoor seats available.

I was thrilled to discover a diverse array of Korean dishes like Tteokbokki, Japchae, Kimchi, Korean BBQ, and various flavors of Soju, alongside exceptional sushi and chicken.
The service was excellent, and the relaxing atmosphere, with its scenic sea view, added to the experience.♥️

While the prices were normal, I found the portions of Tteokbokki and Japchae to be a little small in my opinion. Nonetheless, I highly recommend a visit to this place for anyone seeking delicious food and a welcoming environment.

I will visit this place often to explore more of they delicious food they have to offer 🫰😋♥️🥰

We had some really nice Sushi Rolls here, sitting by the beach and enjoying the sunshine. The rolls were really good and quite unique with the Mayo and we're just lightly fried. The Gyoze are super yummy too. You'll mostly find local recurring people here
Was really good bawl of ramen!! I’ve never been so full!! Beware of seagulls, they think it’s a open buffet

A huuuge plus for having korean food on menu!!! ("secret menu" not on their official homepage) They have some traditional menus, but have koreanised chinease food that can come up as craving. I hope this place remains for those cravings. Black bean noodles is pretty bland, but the spicy seafood noodle is to recommend! Huge portions! The price feels randomly set compared to how much food you get. Atmosphere and service is standard.

Truthfully, one of the best sushi places I have ever been to. The quality of the ingredients is outstanding, and the presentation is meticulously crafted, every detail is carefully taken care of. Each bite reflects the freshness and authenticity of the cuisine.

A nice touch is that they also offer miso soup as a side dish, which complements the sushi perfectly. However, one important thing to note is that the waiters do not come to take your order. Instead, you need to go to the cashier yourself to place your order.

I’m really sad to write this review. This used to be my favorite spot in the area. They were closed for a while, so I hadn’t been back for a few months. I finally returned today, but it’s not the same anymore, the quality has gone down, and the portions are now less than half of what they used to be. Such a shame.

Really extraordinary sushi.  I never imagined that such a small place could hide such a gem.  we had a craving for sushi, and staying at solna centrum was the closest to walk to.  if you want to go there you have to wait too long at 9pm to close, on a Sunday evening we arrived there at 8.30pm and did not sit us down because they were cleaning everything.  but the second time we were able to taste some truly formidable delicacies.  very small place with few seats, but I think it's also the reason why they manage to create such fabulous dishes here.  less people to serve offers the opportunity to concentrate on the dishes.  I recommend it if you love the genre.  value for money more than excellent.  the portions are really generous.
One of our favorite sushi places in the area. We’ve been here many times over the past three years, but I have to admit that we were not as satisfied during our last visits.

I tried the salmon sushi.
The sushi was served cold, not properly grilled and amount of rice was minimal compared to the rest of the sushi restaurants, I had discomfort after eating and my husband felt ill.
The place and seating looks nice and cozy though.
The place was also so small to get a baby stroller and they had only one baby chair (not child friendly).

This was my first time trying Borscht soup with smetana! Although I’m not a fan of beets, I wanted to step out of my comfort zone and try something new. I’m so glad I did because the soup was absolutely delicious! The owner, Elena, shared the fascinating history of Borscht and explained how she ferments the beets herself. I also enjoyed homemade Potato Vareniki (dumplings) — what an amazing dinner! Спасибо.

If you're reading this, you're probably in the process of deciding whether or not to eat here. We hope this review can help you make this decision well. First, by stating that the decision should unequivocally be in the affirmative: assuming you like great food and a wonderful atmosphere, you should definitely visit Café Eurobar!

That being settled, we can move to our second bit of decision support, consisting of some advice on how to deal with its consequences. Because you will end up with a lasting craving for honey cake (and all of the other dishes you'll find on the menu and really ought to try), and you will learn that, to quote Eurobar's owner Elena, "life is better with smetana". This is what happened to us during our holiday, after we were lucky enough to visit Café Eurobar during a food tour and still more after we inadvertently found ourselves back there again for dinner some days later. We were quite unfamiliar with Eastern European cuisine, and are so glad we could try out so many amazing dishes during our visits. Especially in such a wonderful atmosphere and given Elena's knowledge of it and the joy with which she opens up the world of Eastern European food to others! If we lived in or near Stockholm, we would definitely become regulars. But alas, we don't, and you might live further away as well. So, what to do about the cravings for honey cake and borscht then?

Here's what we've come up with so far: look for Eastern European restaurants in your area, and, since they're quite a rare treat, opt for some DIY: look for recipes, try to find an Eastern European grocery store in your neighborhood so you can always sooth your soul with some smetana, and maybe, just maybe, there will be collaborations between beekeepers and Eastern European bakers in your area so you can obtain honey cake, too. Most importantly: create perspective by deciding that you will visit Stockholm again, knowing that when you do, you'll get to pay this great place another visit!

The food here is very tasty😊 We finished everything on our table! We ordered Boscht soup, Ukha, Pelmeni, 🍄‍🟫 vareniki 🥟, salmon blini, and medovik cake 🍰! Omg… we will definitely come back! So yummy!❤️
The food was as good as always! I loved the meat dish with the sour onion and butter, I think. The potatoes with cheese were fabulous, and the Russian crepes were fantastic!

This Eastern European restaurant is truly a gem. The food is flavorful and sophisticated, served in a simple, cozy setting. You might just walk by, as from the outside it looks like a coffee shop, but don’t miss this opportunity to try dishes from Ukraine, Belarus and Russia. I was fortunate to discover Eurobar as a guide with Food Tours Stockholm. The owner, Elena, is a wealth of knowledge, so be sure to ask her if you are interested to know more about the food and culture. The borscht and honey cake are to die for!

name: Falloumi

googleReviews.text:
Sten-very nice, but carrots 🥕 could loose some cinnamon.
Free coffee is great, very helpful service from behind the counter girl.
Baklava is probably the best I have tried so far…

Dear old Falloumi. You have been around now for some years. At the beginning every trendsetter had to run there and describe how good it was. Now it has settled down and the restaurant is still around with the same concept. Really good falafels in a spartan eating area right next to Skanstull. Still worth going there from time to time because the falafels still dance among the very best.

Amazing falafel with a beautiful interior.
This place serves falafel you’re guaranteed to enjoy. You can choose between a roll, pita, or bowl and you really can’t go wrong with any of them. I especially enjoyed the roll version since it was much easier to eat and felt really well balanced.

I wasn’t the biggest fan of the fries since I prefer mine extra crispy, and these were a bit soft on the inside despite being nicely done on the outside. Still decent, just not my personal favorite.

The interior is warm and inviting, with an interesting design that I really liked.

Fantastic food and highly recommended. Tried Fellow pita and it was very delicious. The falafel was very unique taste and instantly became of fan of it while tasting. It was quite a good treat! This place is vegan and vegetarian friendly place and must visit if you are one! Staff was very kind, informative, helpful and friendly. Located very close to Skanstull metro. I recommend this pace😊 Have a great time with your family and friends! Bon appetite 🧆🥙

I’ve been eating out a lot in Stockholm lately but this place immediately went into my top 5 restaurants! The food was amazing, flavourful, fresh, unique. The fries were homemade and delicious. Service was lovely and quick. Coming back here asap

googleEditorialSummary: Low-key, colorful restaurant serving falafel & other Middle Eastern offerings, plus vegan options.

googleReviews.text: This is my favorite dumplings ever! This place is a must if you are in Stockholm. It feels like you’re walking into your grandmas kitchen. The staff are so friendly and nice. I recommend strongly!

Oh, where do I even start?..
I've read actual Chinese reviews that warned me how BAD this place was, but for some reason I wanted to test it myself. It was such a MISTAKE!
The food was simply terrible, I've never eaten anything that bad at any Asian restaurant. It tasted mediocre, the dumplings had too much dough in them, were not closed properly, the contents were far from done, the amount of salt in the soup was too much. This has nothing to do with authentic food!
The place is not very clean, all chopsticks are laying together in a large box.
Besides, the staff actually tried to scam me and forced me to pay 5 SEK for tap water without even mentioning anything.
PLEASE DON'T GO HERE. There are many good Chinese restaurants in Stockholm and even on Södermalm you will find some dumpling restaurants that are authentic and tasty.
I would give it 0 stars if I could. The only ok thing was the chilli oil.

This restaurant shines in Stockholm like a beacon in a storm. If I have a hard day, I go here. If I have a great day, I go here. I will forever recommend this restaurant to people looking for dumplings. On some days, there is little crowd, and it is very peaceful, and you are warmly greeted by the wonderful woman that runs the restaurant. On other days, it’s packed, full of people enjoying their small but elegant menu. To those reviews that say the dumplings are doughy, I say you have no culinarily experience and should expand your view on what is appreciated. Every bite of the food here makes me thankful to have tastebuds. You must try this restaurant!!! Thank you Fang Yuan Shi Wu for feeding me, I will see you tomorrow!

Super tasty spot in Sodermalm! Awesome homemade dumplings served with a smile and come out quickly and made fresh! Staff is super nice and welcoming and the restaurant is low key but super cozy. 100% recommend and will be back!

Insta
🥩🥦🫛🧄🧅🥟🍜🥣

📸  Sichuan Beef and Veggies
📸  Chicken & Beef Dumplings
📸  Chicken Noodles
📸  Dumpling Soup

@FangYuanShiWu
Exquisite handmade dumplings

📍Stockholm 🇸🇪

Just a very average place. Not classy. Simple home kinda setup with tables, silverware etc. Ladies who run it are super cute though. Dumplings were good but pretty normal, it doesn’t come with any dip or anything. I would have preferred a dip on the side. Noodles were also good but I felt I am eating at home. Price was very nominal though. So if u want just a casual food truck kinda feeling eating by the road, then it’s good. But not for a fancy restaurant kinda feeling

name: Fem små hus

googleReviews.text:
Lovely food. Therefore I give 4 starts. We went there with our 3 children. The table was pre-booked and we expected a faster service. But we had to wait for more than 45 minutes to get our starters !!! That was too long to wait !!!
However the rest of the courses followed well &  in time & all were happy!

We just had our NYE dinner here.
The NYE menu existed out of 5 courses for 1095 sek. If you wanted the accompanying wines it was 695 sek extra.

The atmosphere is great, old classy tavern vibes.

The food was okay to good. The taste of each ingredient on your plate fitted with the rest.
However, I’m not complete convinced that it justifies the price. But as it was New Year’s Eve, I guess we also paid for this special night.

I’m not familiar with Swedish hospitality as we’re visiting here, but it seemed the staff wasn’t specifically happy to be working on NYE.

All combined a 4/5. Nice food and a nice setting for Stockholm’s oldest restaurant.

A very atmospheric restaurant in the heart of Old Town. Great food, great service, and wonderful atmosphere. Like all places in Old Town, the restaurant is expensive, but that is to be expected given the location. They have a great lunch and dinner menu which focuses on traditional Swedish meals. I can warmly recommend this place based on my experience there.

Awesome place of 17th century Tavern one of the 10 world’s oldest restaurants! Speaks of the history and food is amazing as well the service!!! We just loved the fish and vegetarian menu paired with best of french and white wine…! Just loved the candle light setup on a cozy swedish winter…!

Very deep in character. Most of guests were in large groups or families- got very loud for a "cave"  with curved ceilings :). We were given table in a walkway, somewhat separated, which was nice with time, as main rooms got louder. Food was nicely presented, was heavy and oversalted though. Main dishes are different meats and different ways made potatoes. I liked my desert!

googleEditorialSummary: Intimate & ornate 17th-century cellar restaurant for upscale Swedish/French cuisine by candlelight.

googleReviews.text: Very nice and cozy place with good food and coffee. Everything is natural, the products good and the interior design very nice looking. The service was great too and we could enjoy two nice breakfasts. I highly recommend

We had chocolate pancakes and a breakfast bowl, they were quite enjoyable. Thank you for not using plain Nutella in the pancakes. The only thing I didn’t like is coconut whipped cream in the pancakes – I think the milk cream is much tastier but the restaurant seems to be fully vegan so I guess this complaint doesn’t count.

Enjoyed this little vegan cafe! Got the green smoothie, custard tart, and the tofu sandwich.

Loved the smoothie and sandwich. Don’t be turned off by them being in the glass case, they taste very fresh and the sandwich was lightly toasted before it was served to my table. Sandwich was like an egg-salad-style tofu topped with greens, tomato slice, and pickled onion slice; was very flavorful. The custard combined with the tart crust was too dry when together; I’ve seen similar custards served in vegan cafes in other cities and this seems best executed as a custard in a jar.

Girl at the front counter was very friendly and helpful! Wish I took photos of the food, but I ended up devouring them pretty quickly 😂

When I was in Stockholm with a group we were looking around for a breakfast stop in the city and stumbled upon this cute cafe

Love that it's a veggie place and the workers were lovely as well, even providing our dog with a water bowl which was great against the heat!

The people working at this place are very friendly, kind and they also seem to put a lot of effort in it but I see a lot of potential in improving this place.
Because of the good reviews I had high expectations for this place. Especially because I find it a bit hard to find fully vegan places in Stockholm. So we decided to come for breakfast.
The best thing we tried was the scrambled tofu sandwich. It was well balanced and tasty.
The Tofu wrap was bad. The spinach was fresh but the tomatoes tasted quite acidic and they also looked a bit old. The tomatoes were on the bottom of the wrap which made the whole thing very wet and soggy. It fell apart while eating.
The avocado bread was okay but also the garnishing looked not fresh. For a basic dish also not good.
To drink we had coconut cappuccino but they must have burned the milk frothing it so also the taste suffered a lot.

Really great service and food. We came here for lunch on a Thursday and we're able to get seated right away. The server gave good recommendations and we very much enjoyed the Reindeer dish. It had a nice sweet flavour to it. We also enjoyed the swedish meatballs as it was well seasoned. I highly recommend dining at this restaurant.

We were disappointed with our visit to this restaurant. We came in hoping to eat something quickly, without a reservation. Even though i saw free table, the lady at the bar who spoke with us, although polite, did not allow us to sit down, claiming that all tables were reserved. When we asked if we could wait until a table became available, she allowed us to wait at the bar.
We decided to wait for a table to free up. During this time, we ordered hot wine, which first of all was served in a very small quantity, was very sweet, and also very expensive. We waited for 15 minutes, sipping the wine, and the tables that was free when we arrived was still free when we left.
We decided to leave the place, and nearby we easily found another restaurant where the waiters were very friendly and the food was excellent. There, unlike in this restaurant, we felt welcome. Here, however, I had the impression that they were not interested in us spending our money on a meal.
Personally, I will never return to this restaurant, as I felt that I would not feel comfortable there. I like cheerful, smiling, and positive people.

Lovely place for a traditional meal. The girls serving were very sweet and helpful. We weren’t massively hungry, so we got the shrimp toast and the herring platter — both really good. We also tried the meatballs, which were delicious! Slightly on the pricier side, but overall great and worth it.

A delightful dining experience with delicious, freshly prepared food and a warm, cozy atmosphere. The service was exceptional — the staff were genuinely friendly, attentive, and highly professional. Their kindness and politeness made the visit even more enjoyable. Highly recommended.

This was honestly the best meal I’ve had since arriving in this city. I came on my own, and the Swedish meatballs were absolutely delicious 🤤 perfectly cooked, full of flavor, and very satisfying.

A special thank you to Melanie and Amanda. They were both incredibly kind, attentive, and their positive energy truly added something special to the atmosphere of the place.

Highly recommended. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
The pizza selection is quite decent, with many vegetarian toppings. All the ones I've tried have been delicious. A great plus is that they serve affordable wine.
We always visit when passing through Stockholm from Arlanda Airport
My favorite Pizza place, can be a little crowded but it’s absolutely worth a visit. Kebab is also very good.
Really good value for money! Pizzas is great and the sallads come with a 20cm newly baked loaf of bread!
Great place. Friendly staff, good atmosphere, even better food! Would recommend to anyone in the solna/filmstaden area!!!
This is the highest point in Stockholm.   As such, it serves as a wonderful viewpoint for the city skylines.   A good place to visit for pictures.

One of the best viewpoints in Stockholm. The street goes high above the water banks so you can see a lot of beautiful places - in both far away river bank or on the Fjallgaten street itself (narrow streets, old houses, small parks and cafes). Very nice place for a date or a casual walk.
Great spot to take amazing pictures of the islands.
Very nice place, beautiful view of the city skyline, it's also worth seeing the sunset from this spot, it looks stunning. 😍💜

breath-taking view over the city of Stockholm (although not really over the old part of the city). From this terrace you can see the leasure park, the Djurgarden and the eastern part of the city. It's worth a visit and just a 20-minute-walk from the city center.

Great Italian pizza experience!
We ordered the pizza buffet and it was really good. Fresh, delicious pizzas, a crisp and tasty salad, and good bread. Everything felt fresh and well prepared.
Cozy atmosphere that made the whole experience even better. Definitely worth a visit.

Firstly, very cool space and, for the summer months, what looked like a fine outside terrace with excellent views over the city. Secondly, and perhaps more importantly, great flatbreads, a.k.a. pizza incl. good vegan options. Add to that a good beer selection and friendly service and you're on to a winner. Will return.

Amazing lunch place and offers a wide array of pizzas in the lunch. Pizzas are baked in a clay oven and kept fresh, a good concept. Very friendly staff to guide you around.
The ambience is amazing, a great location on the hill and in the middle of nature.

It is a stylish restaurant in an old water tower, on top of a rocky hill. There is also a very nice terrace at the tower entrance where you can sit.
They serve delicious pizza and a phew other really tasty things.Must mention they have one of the best brownies I have ever eaten in my life, included in the lunch pizza buffet.
Lunch is really one of the best price-quality ratio.
Also, lunch includes some assorted salads, tea, coffee, water and some other fancy things.
They really keep the quality standards up in my opinion.

Yeah tommorow you can forget the words I told you
Tommorow some other day
The help project Three stooges join
Teams with only others
Good night amen
Holliua
Hope you remember to wake up
On A later now an then
An definintely 11 o clock
Goodbye see you later
An good riddens
Why it matters cause a sleep pattern
You should remember
Replay it
A,g,a,i,n and a,g,a,i,n

Such a cozy little restaurant, excellent food and service!

We came in with no reservation on a Friday evening and they made space for us, first at the bar and then later moving us to a table. The skrei and tartar we had were fantastic. The staff seemed to be enjoying themselves and having fun, which created a super nice atmosphere. We'll be coming back!
I wish I remembered more of the food taste! Thanks Kurt ❤️

Misguided by SVD !!!!  visited  the restaurant after reading SvD krogguiden , neither the tacos , octopus or the crudo made an impact on taste or presentation , maybe the gnocchi had certain level of creativity and flavor,  weak service.
Food and vibe was excellent. Especially pleased with the Ragu. I never tip. But this time I feel bad about not doing so.
Food and atmosphere was exquisite, service was very nice but a little slow with a single person.

Nice place for a beverage,  and maybe some food. Plenty of beer types but some at too high price.
Separate room downstairs, with limited beverage selection, suitable for larger groups and overflow from upstairs.
Nice service!

Rude owner! He insulted us. We left without finishing our drinks.
DONT GO HERE! I have never had such a traumatic experience.

It was the most insane experience I ever had. From the time me and my co-workers walked in to grab drinks it felt like they couldn't wait for us to go. The server was antagonizing and rude, mocking my colleague's lack of fluent Swedish. It almost felt as though they were being targeted for being immigrants. I left there feeling disheartened. All I can say is never again.

Owner was extremely rude, throwing insults even though we ordered drinks. Don't go here if you want a pleasant experience. You're better off going to a library

Response to owner:
It's pretty evident you try to gaslight your guests. Look at the other reviews and you will see a common pattern, your aggressive behavior.
Amazing buregers, highlights recommnended.
I love this place and recommend it to everyone the only bad thing about this pizzeria is that they are too good, too tasty😭 That is why they have been rising the price but trust me it's worth it
Good thin crust pizza. Has a selection with "Italian ingredients", which gives a more premium feeling. I would also recommend their calzone. Average price for neighborhood pizzerias.
The place is nice and intimate with lots of pizza menus 😜 except no alcohol.
My local pizza place. Pizzas are smaller than competing Il Postino, but well prepared with friendly staff. Great of picking up and bringing home, but too sterile for eating on location.
Pretty chill pizza place, the food tastes bomb but a bigger portion size wouldn't go amiss.

A nice buffet-style lunch restaurant at a resonable price (when compared to for example Eatery). Offer some "traditional" Swedish meals like ärtsoppa and pancakes on Thursday.

They always offer at least one meat, fish or vegetarian options.

I found the staff friendly.

1st experience here and it was good. Only 1 meat option on this particular day. Lots of veggie options! Food was flavorful and presentation lovely. Food could have been a bit warmer temp for my preference and another meat option. I would definitely go back again.
Nice place for weekday lunch buffet. 3 main course options and plenty salad. 5min walk from Stadshagen tbana. Coffee and bread is included.

This is the best place I know to eat at. It has insanely good food and every Thursday there's the best pancakes ever.
I wish I could make you, the reader, come here, but a review is all I can do.
Really recommend!
Great lunch place in Stadshagen. 3 different alternatives each lunch. Typically 1 vegetarian, 1 fish and 1 meat option.

Went there for birthday dinner. We wanted to have Asian food and this was the perfect spot that served us delicious Chinese food. The staff is welcoming and warm. Food was delicious, well presented. The food quantity is also good. Everything was freshly cooked. Ambience was great. Restaurant is also quite big located in the heart of Gamalastan. There are both vegetarian and non-vegetarian options available.

Excellent restaurant – everything was fresh, beautifully presented, and very tasty. We enjoyed every part of the meal: from the small starters, through the main courses (both fish and vegetarian), to the outstanding ice cream at the end. A well-balanced and carefully crafted dining experience. Highly recommended!
We went to the lunch buffet for 155 sek. The Selektion was alright and the food good. I wish there would have been more information about what is vegetarian /vegan.

Ordered hotpot, felt the food portions not enough  for two people for the money. Ended up ordering a to go at Max Burger afterwards. Otherwise the was food was good. Will definitely go there to try other dishes but not hotpot.

Very recommended. I don't know why people wrote opposite experiences. However, we were 5 people with very mix backgrounds from Germany, Bangladesh, and Asia. We were very surprised and extremely satisfied with the foods that we ordered! Unfortunately we didn't think to make pictures before eating. Almost always forget it when the foods are so tasty.. :D

If you are around and wanted to have asian foods for lunch or dinner, you should give it a try!

Coffee like this should not be served to customers. We expected the same good quality we had last time, but this was really disappointing and not worth the price. Not only did it look poorly made, but it also tasted bad. Unfortunately, if you’re looking for a well-made, consistent coffee, you’re better off going somewhere else.

A little slice of Paris by the Baltic! ☕ 🥐 We popped in as a family and fell in love with the flaky-butter croissants, silky cappuccinos, and jewel-like pastries that look almost too pretty to eat (almost). 😋 Staff greet kids with big smiles, high-chairs appear without fuss, and the bright, airy room invites you to linger over that second macaron. A must-stop for anyone who believes great memories start with good coffee and perfect pastry. 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦👍
Very good dessert and coffee, hot chocolate is just so so, has some weird flavor.

Now this was a surprise. I did not expect much but i was wrong. I tried a chicken truffle sandwich which was phenomenal! My wife got the day's soup and we tried a salad.

Everything was perfect, tasty and cozy.

You can also try their desserts. We tried macarons and I can say that they are nearly as good as those we tried in France.
Excellent products in a beautiful location. The shrimp salad, the reuben sandwich, the ice cream and all tge deserts are fabulous. A must try in Stockholm.

Genuinely one of the best meals I’ve had in a while.  Like 10/10 excellent.  Beautifully cooked fish with roasted breadcrumbs, baby potatoes and dill sauce.  Side salad with vinegarette dressing.  Truly perfect flavour balance.  The bread had a nice lentil/ roasted tomato spread.  Dessert (carrot cake) also tasted amazing.  Wasn’t too sweet and cake/ frosting were delicious!

I ate outside in the sunshine on the patio.  The atmosphere was perfect.  After I thanked the server and the chef and they were both so kind so kind/ appreciative.

Great place. Fantastic food. I highly recommend the meatballs. Plenty of space to sit and comfortable sofas. I would give it 5 stars, but my friend got two more meatballs than me. That was the only negative. Tip: add some cheese to the meatballs, it was really delicious.

Such a delicious food there! The soup with coconut milk, pasta bolognaise and the salad dressing (the gravy one). The people who work here are very friendly, (the cashier guy and the young girl at the food line)

It is a nice place considering you are in the museum. By the entrance you can see the menu in order to know in advance what you will take by the time you get to the cashier. Important to mention there is a salad bar, which is part of the price you pay, and the vegetables were fresh.
There was a long line and the service was pretty fast considering it was lunch time (therefore busy).

Great place, staff was polite and food was decent. Also I see people have complained about prices but to be fair, 100kr for a main dish + drink + salads is very cheap for a restaurant in Stockholm. These are student prices

We got the chance to try the second Frankys in Stockholm. It’s a bigger place. I had the Umami burger this time, it didn’t not live up to the same level as the Texas. But the burgers at this place are amazing. Really worth a visit.

Lovely place. Super quick service, friendly staff and good seating options (outdoor area as well, very nice in the summer).
The burger was perfect, and the truffle dip was absolutely insane.
Wide selection of beers as well.
Would surely come back :)

The best burger we had in a long time, after we were done we could have another one. Not because we were hungry but the juiciness and how tasty it was.

Quick and friendly service on top of that. This was the day after we finished our Hyrox competition and we were in need for some energy, and this for sure gave us what we needed.

A must to come back once we're back

Delicious burgers (cheers chef/(s)), only 5-10 min waiting time, chill atmosphere, maybe a liiittle bit loud, but there was a blonde waitress that seemed almost offended by our mere presence there, hope she is not always like that, maybe she was having a rough day. Otherwise, nice place to go! 🙂

Everything was fantastic! The burgers were super warm and perfectly crispy, just how I like them. The service was excellent—quick, attentive, and they always double-checked to make sure we were enjoying our food. Our table was well-stocked with salt, pepper, ketchup, and hot sauce, which was a nice touch. Drinks came with ice in a glass, which made it feel extra refreshing. Overall, a top-notch experience from start to finish—I’ll definitely be coming back!

Before a gig had a meal of a lifetime their venison sirloin was perfectly cooked. When entering Everything WIll Flow by Suede was playing. What a perfect start for the night and after some walking hours. Cosy and crowded.

Delectable food presented well! Cooked well with fresh ingredients. The staff is knowledgeable and very helpful. A bit expensive. But, you do get what you pay for, delicious food, good service and a warm ambience. The place is in a blink-and-you-miss-it niche at the corner of Sehlstedtsgatan. Reservations recommended.

Funkalistic is a fantastic Bar & Bistro with excellent Food,Drinks and very enjoyable Music and a MUST when visiting Stockholm,,Kitchen and Service are outstanding,we'll come definetly back,the farest way is worth it!!!

Ordered steak here! Gluten-free crisp bread provided as well. I’d say their steaks are amazing because my mate ordered something else and he didn’t love it as much as I did. But I’m still gonna give this a 5 star because their service is great and it’s just a very nice experience. Try medium-well 😍

The place was good but extremely crowded during rush hours. You won't think it's that much food if you are used to large portions. I had fish "Torsk" with potatoes ...didn't like it that much because of the butter around it! It actually looked like some kind of sauce but it wasn't. The price was around 120 kr. Sallad, bread, apple pie and coffee included.

Nice viewpoint close to the center of Stockholm. Haven't been insode the Restaurant, but going up for some pictures is worth ist tho :) you will have a good view over the river/harbour etc. btw, toilets are locked...thats something usual in that area tbh.

Really beautiful restaurant with amazing view, fit for big parties and weddings,  but the food was a disappointment! But the other guests seemed to like it, so I guess it was not my or my husbands cop of tea🤷‍♀️
Great value for the Julbord! Nice view and decent food. Our waitress is very patient with us as we are a big crowd and it took time to order and pay.

Great place to enjoy the stunning view over Stockholm!
Even if the restaurant/cafe is closed you can always come here just to enjoy the view 24/7.
Not sure if it really worths a visit if you are coming from Stockholm central area. The view is good, food is ordinary, the area around is a bit shabby.

During my solo city trip in Stockholm I visited this place and really liked it.

The food, service and atmosphere were nice.
Great food, meatballs best we had during our stay in Stockholm. We came back a second time, even though we only stayed in Stockholm for 4 days.

They have refurbished and are now open as a bar/cafe.

Excellent selection of very well priced drinks.

Staff are very friendly. Great atmosphere too.

Would definitely recommend a visit here.

Friendly service - the girl at the counter didn’t seem to mind explaining their entire salad menu for me since it wasn’t written in English. Cozy seats and atmosphere. My salad came out to $13 but that’s Sweden for you.
Loved it! Very friendly staff, an electric outlet to charge devices and a refill of the delicious coffee! Cozy interior with comfy chairs (fåtöljen!). Couldn't be happier with the visit 💕

We decided to head out of the center city a bit specifically to try the meatballs at Gamla Enskede Matbod and they didn’t disappoint. They were, by far, some of the best Swedish meatballs we’ve had on our various trips to Sweden. They even tasted better than a bonafide “best Swedish meatballs” restaurant we visited in Gamla Stan a few days later (and paid much more for). This restaurant is very easy to get to by train and is walking distance from the station. It’s also beautiful.

Overall: Not worth it. I really looked forward to it but went home pretty disappointed.

Pros:
- Good flavors
- best apple sauce
- cozy atmosphere
- Glögg guy was welcoming

Cons:
- we waited 8 mins to be seated (had a reservation) despite numerous staff members passing by us and greeting us during the 8 mins but nobody seemed to care. We had to ask to be seated!
- Waiter couldn't speak English and asked his colleague to help but she went ahead with Swedish anyway
- The staff were very inattentive e.g. the water never got refilled (I had to find someone and ask), I also went to look for coffee and had to ask a staff for it, dirty plates not always removed.
- The warm food was not warm when we got there as some had been sitting there a while probably from the previous seating.
- Several dishes ran out but were not refilled. I asked for several to be refilled incl. Julskinka, köttbullar, inlagd gurka, beetsalad, apple sauce. This is something the staff should always be mindful of and not be asked to do!!!!
- we were seated around 8pm. At 9pm we were asked if they could remove the sill. A bit later we were asked if they could remove the cold cuts. It's supposed to be a buffet (?!).

Got invited from a friend that recently visited this place. He was right. Place is very nice and the food is great. I had some gnocchi with pumpkin (I highly recommend it if still in the menu) and my friend ordered some pork dish which he said was awesome too. Tasty coffee and good service
Cosy restaurant, high quality, old fashioned Swedish food. Quick service and personable staff.

Our company hosted a client event in the restaurant's new private dining space. The managers were wonderful to work with - very responsive and accommodating. The group of 20+ visiting Americans had an absolute best time. Our guests could not stop talking about the charming venue and fantastic food. One of them mentioned the dinner being the most authentic Swedish food he's ever had. Thank you for such a wonderful experience!

Old Orangery Café in Stockholm is a lovely place where you can enjoy a meal or fika, sitting inside or outside. You can also walk around and enjoy the beautiful surroundings. In spring and summer, it’s a perfect spot to gather with friends!
I took a stroll around the many gardens and ended up in this café. The ambience is really calming and beautiful, but unfortunately my food was served with a bee swimming in the sauce.

The food here was surprisingly good given that it is a botanic gardens cafe. The tomato risotto with mussels was excellent. Our kid loved the goat cheese salad and my husband enjoyed the salmon.  We sat outside in the autumn sun and enjoyed the ambience.  The princess cake wasn’t great nor was the cinnamon bun but the main food was delicious.  We’d eat here again.

The fresh pasta was delicious and the chive pesto complimented it perfectly. It really was excellent.

One of my worst food photos, but this lunch basically set the bench mark for my trip in Sweden.

Sometimes, I get really lucky as a Google guide!
I have been visiting so many cafes and restaurants through my own work as a consultant in food & beverage and in my spacetime !
Today, I visited this gem!
Everything matches together in its own seamless simplicity and organic look and feel !
A must if you are in Stockholm
This place deserves a bit more publicity
I have been there several times. Its very delicious pita gyros and other greek recipes. I  recommend it.

As a Greek, I must say that I finally found my favourite place for gyros and souvlaki in Stockholm! Very tasty, reminds me of my country! They are very polite and kind! Already went there 2 times in 3 days... definitely will go again and again!
Maybe the most authentic Greek fast food in town. Friendly environment tasty food and fast service!
The place it was a little tight. But still  well  arranged and about the food it was special. It gives  you the Mediterranean  feeling.  For sure will go again.
A very good personnel. Can give 5 stars about the cleaning . And the food reminds us the Mediterranean . Where I come from. I highly recommend this place.

name: Gast

googleReviews.text: Fun branding, trendy setting and good tunes. Busy but not without empty seats.  Good service and food, special shout out to the american pancakes with blood orange and vanilla mascarpone. Little ghost is very cute.

The cafe interior looks nice, and overall experience is okay, but I was highly disappointed with their Benedict eggs, which is one of my fave brunch options. Here, it was the most expensive and the least tasty I've ever had. It was ok, but definitely not for their price. The bread wasn't toasted, and hollandaise sauce was mediocre. My bf took spicy tuna sandwich, which was cheaper and much better. Overall experience was fine, it was pretty busy, and our table wasn't stable, which was a bit annoying, but I wouldn't say it's the reason to never back. To sum up, don't order their Benedict eggs :)

Gast Cafe is a spacious coffee - quite full during lunch break. For a start, their menu boasts both sweet (pastry and pancakes) and savoury options, which is great. I went originally for the Cesar salad but they didn’t have it anymore on the menu, so I opted for scramble eggs on Parmesan bread with avocado and bacon.
Parmesan bread was very original, and the chilli oil on top made the whole favours more interesting. The bacon was sadly very hard - barely able to cut it.
Food was good, and the portion was filling.
Sadly, the coffee was disappointing. A flat white, which should have 2 shots of espresso, didn’t taste as intense as I’d expected. Sadly to me it felt a bit like a watered latte, which made me regret not having tried a simple espresso.
I knew this place was not for speciality coffee, but somehow, due to its popularity, I thought it was more resembling a stronger aroma. Still, this is a good place to chat with a friend (and even work with your laptop, but mind their timetable!).

Really cozy place and a nice presentation of the eggs benedict with avocado that I ordered. However, there was barely any flavor apart from the chili, the bun was bland, and the eggs were inedible - watery liquid ran out as soon as I cut into them🫣

I’m a big fan of specialty coffee, and unfortunately, this café didn’t quite meet my expectations in that regard. I ordered a cappuccino with oat milk (56kr), which tasted more like warm milk than coffee and took a bit longer than expected to arrive. I didn’t try any of the food, so I can’t comment on that aspect.

The service felt somewhat impersonal — the young staff seemed busy and focused on delivering orders, but there wasn’t much interaction or warmth. The space itself was crowded and quite noisy, which may appeal to those who enjoy a lively atmosphere, but it wasn’t really for me.

Overall, it’s probably a better spot for people looking for a bustling café environment rather than a specialty coffee experience.

googleEditorialSummary: Specialty drinks & savory brunch fare, plus pancakes & porridge, in a chic, modern cafe with plants.

googleReviews.text: Decent French type coffee place, but a bit on the expensive side. One medium latte and one carrot cake will set you back 100 kr. But the carrot cake was very good. There are some places to sit inside and some to sit outside, but not a lot. They have decaf as well.

This pistachio cinnamon bun was DIVINE indeed 😭… everyone who’s wondering about the famous swedish bread, go for here’s cinnamon bun…🥺 the staff was very nice and spoke english!
Thank you for making my day sooo delicious 😽

A small local Gateau cafe with lovely personell.
Good coffee, good pastries, the Orange Juice is not locally pressed, but ok.
Breakfast baguette was a bit chewy ... But tasty if you made it through.
Nice outside seats in the shade of the trees on a quiet street in a great neighborhood.
The coffee and carrot cake I ordered were good, however, the cleanliness at the place was appalling. Deducting 1 star for cleanliness, 1 more for the prices and 1 more for the staff to ignore it.
Really good coffee with good price. The atmosphere was also nice and very enjoyable. I would come back to try the pastries, they looked very nice.

Super cute café the food and drinks were amazing !! As a French tourist I was pleased to find out the barista who was there, Roda, spoke French pretty fluently ! Great staff, would definitely recommend, if you’re French ask for Roda
Loved this place. Cozy, great food, great coffee, compact. Will visit again.

Very friendly
Great coffee and pastries

We visited for a quick afternoon Fika and found this place via Google Maps. Turned out to be a rather pleasant experience. Nice staff and delicious pastries. What more can you wish for? 😀 The place itself is quite small, but we still got a table and were able to enjoy the Fika.
Stopped for lunch on our walk around Edsviken. Beef on soda bread roll was lovely, as was the cardamom bun and Americano coffee. Would recommend.
Great spot if you fancy something different to Swedish food. Great beers, goid music amd tasty food!

German Bear Hall offers a happy and inviting atmosphere that instantly makes you feel welcome. The service is excellent, with staff who ensure you have everything you need. The drink options are impressive, with a wide variety of beers, cocktails, and shots to choose from. You can also order food or snacks to pair perfectly with your drink.

The decoration is charming and adds to the overall cozy vibe of the place. It’s an ideal spot for after-work gatherings or simply enjoying a glass of beer with a friend.

After my visit, I can definitely see myself becoming a regular at German Bear Hall—it’s a gem worth returning to!

IF you go on a Saturday you better be prepared to party! However, the food is better than expected and veggie options are available.

The portions are large. The service is vey friendly and the staff bring energy into everything they do.

The pretzel was still hot from the oven and fresh. There’s a large selection of beers both German and Swedish. The sausage was amazing and inexpensive. There’s a kids menu with pancakes and chicken nuggets and the schnitzel was delicious too. Service got a little overwhelmed on Friday evening at rush hour but was still friendly.

Sausage basket better than expected and the same with the cheese hot dog. No problem to get a table for 4. Service exceed expectations since wife was offered to try a couple of beer types since she normally prefers wine. Chef Artur was were attentive to the guests and took the time to chat with guests. Shared the desert between us 4 and it was liked by everyone.
We will be back

I had lunch at German Beer Hall in Liljeholmen and tried the schnitzel. It was… so-so. Not terrible, but definitely not great either — a solid 2.5 out of 5. The place itself feels very similar to what was here before, and overall it’s not that impressive.

It’s clear they’re going for a fun German beer hall vibe, but they don’t quite pull it off. Instead of an authentic atmosphere, they show Oktoberfest clips from YouTube on screens and have staff wearing “traditional” outfits that look like they were ordered from Temu. The result feels more like a themed costume party than a real German experience.

That said, it’s not a bad place — just not one I’d choose for lunch again. I would recommend it for an afterwork beer though, as that seems to be where the concept fits better. But if you’re looking for good food, I’d eat elsewhere.

Update Nov 2025:
There have improved a lot. Waiters are friendly and there when you need them, food arrived quickly, the loud booming ompa ompa music is gone.

Original review:
Me and my better half were there on the opening day, hadn't booked a table but got one anyway.
We ordered a beer and a main each, we got the beers after 5 minutes. After that we didn't see a waiter for about and hour until one came over and said we had to leave as our table was booked. Guess they forgot to tell us that? We informed her we were still waiting for our food so she let us keep the table and went to the next table over and informed them their table was reserved and they had to leave. 😄
10 minutes late we finally saw our waiter again (after having sat at an empty table for 45 minutes). We ordered another 2 beers and asked when we might get our food. He said he'd tell the kitchen to hurry up.
10 minutes later we got a beer.. and 2 minutes later we got the 2 beers we had actually ordered. 🤷🏼‍♂️
At this stage we started to make bets on if it would take more or less than 2 hours from the time we ordered the food until it arrived at the table. I bet on more than 2 hours and lost the bet as it "only" tool 1 hour and 55 minutes for the food to arrive.

Atmosphere; Music was way to loud. They've kept most of the interior from the previous pizza/pasta place which didn't quite for work the German theme they are aiming for.
Service; Our waiter was very friendly (but absent for most of the evening)
Food; Great! The currywürst was as good as the ones I've had in Berlin, if not better.

I know it's not fair to give them a low score after one visit on the opening nigh so I will visit again soon in update the score.
German BEER hall. I went in for a beer (or two)! Don’t understand other reviewers complaining - awesome atmosphere and really great talkative staff 💯 I’ll be back :)
Food quality was terrible.. I felt like had expired beef dish.. food was not warm at all and zero fresh.. even the potatoes and beetroot tastes bad

You honestly shouldn't judge any place based on opening night, which many here has, when most things are just bound to go wrong. Murphys law and all that.
I was here for lunch today, which was excellent! Food was great (Swedish raggmunk), beer came quick and the waitress was nice and polite. I really have nothing to complain about, except perhaps the decorations of the venue. It doesn't feel like a German beer hall, but perhaps that will improve over the coming weeks. Let's see. I've booked a table for dinner a couple of weeks ahead, might update this review after that.

Nice atmosphere, especially in the basement.  Decor is very tex-mex biker.

Vegan options if that's your jam.

Food was good but under seasoned.  Happy to see they had Mexican street corn (elote) but it wasn't quite classic, missing the Tajin (used paprika instead).

They have some nice tequila for margaritas or shots.

The entrance to Geronimo's can be a little deceiving as you enter an upstairs bar area, but keep walking through to the stairs at the back and it opens up into a cavernous restaurant and bar, that looks like it also hosts music nights. We had not booked but took a chance and the staff were super accommodating. We had a drink upstairs whilst they waited for a table to finish and boy, am I glad we waited. We obviously ordered way too much, but then again, it was impossible to choose. We had tacos, burritos and quesadillas, plus corn and dirty fries. The tastes were outstanding and it was a fabulous, lively atmosphere. Highly recommended if you are in Stockholm.

The ambiance was warm and inviting, perfect for a casual night out or a special celebration. The menu featured a fantastic variety of Mexican dishes, and everything we tried was bursting with flavor. Do try their Margherita. They also have vegan options for all their dishes, and the staff was really helpful in creating a delicious non-alcoholic drink for me.

Geronimo's offers a fantastic dining experience in its basement atmosphere. The staff is impressively kind and attentive. The food is not only delicious but also served in generous portions. What's especially great is the extensive selection of vegan options, catering to diverse preferences. To top it off, their margaritas are truly exceptional, making it a must-visit for both flavor and hospitality enthusiasts.

Simple menu.  Tacos were fresh, hot, crammed with meat and fresh salsa, bit of heat.  Tried el pastore and chicken.  Tacos don't come with sides so order tortilla chips with salsa and guacamole - crispy, crunchy, salty perfection.  1 order enough to share for 2-3.  Tacos order 1, 2 or 3 - price per taco goes down as # goes up.  3 is just the right amount.
Margarita!  Best I've had.  Sweet, tart, salty.
Love the 70's tunes filling the cave-like room.  Loud enough to enjoy but still able to converse without screaming.
Recommended.

A lively pub that serves Croatian cuisine, which is rare to find in Stockholm. The minced beef was juicy and the grilled vegetables were flavorful. The sides were a combination of creamy yogurt and aromatic minced bell peppers, which neutralized the heaviness of the dish. The potatoes were a bit under seasoned.
The drinks here were well priced for a pub and there were quite a few options. It was a bit too noisy inside because we need to increase our volume a lot to be heard.

What lovely staff. Despite the balkan atmosphere, they know their shit when it comes to dietary preferances and vegan food. Amazing vibes and plate sizes didnt dissapoint.
The only loss of point was that it was not very spice-ful, yet tasted great. High prices but very worth it.
Check out their schedule as they seem to have food theemed weekends often!

The oxfilé steak was a solid 10/10 – highly recommend it. The fish and chips came in a generous portion and were tasty, but a bit too greasy for our liking, so I’d give it a 7/10. I’ll definitely come back for the steak. The staff were friendly, and I really liked the atmosphere and decor as well.
Pleasently surprised of the food at they joint. They had the regular pub menu but also a kroatian menu, both were nice. Highly recommend

It's so hard to find traditional Swedish food, but here it was! The vibe was really nice and we got the mozzarella sticks for free after they forgot our order. Excellent customer service from the brunette lady behind the bar!

name: Glashuset

googleReviews.text:
We enjoyed by the river nice food and drinks. I took fish and was delicious .
Reservation is recommended
Ambience sitting out is perfect for nice evening. For us weather for perfect but in general they also have heaters outside. Right near center
Beef was also fine

This restaurant was very good. It is a cute little place along the water. I’m sure the view was very nice however we ate after the sun set so couldn’t take advantage of the view.

The food was delicious. I had the chicken and my friend had the steak.

The service was very good as well.

We came here for dinner on our second night in Stockholm and were not disappointed. The food was very good, it has a nice atmosphere, and the server even came to check on us and make sure we had everything we need — which, if you are a traveler from the US to Europe, you know is not a common thing. Overall, we had a great experience and would definitely go back again.

I came to this place family members and the location was nice and by the water so there was a nice view.  The atmosphere was a bit too loud from the people trying to talk over the loud 80s music. The waiter was nice but barely saw him.

The family had ordered the red prawns dish which came out a bit too salty. It had two half sliced bread and some garlic mayo. The mayo I found was delicious. Next item ordered was the poke bowl. This was bland and hardly any salmon. And they didn't have any hot sauce, chili oil or even wasabi which could've given it a nice kick. The rice was mushy as well.  Next is the char dish and at first couldn't find the char and mistaken the croqette for the char. The char tasted good as well as the chanterelles. Only gripe was why couldn't the chef chop up the cauliflower and not stick a hunk of it on the plate as if the chef had given up. And finally I got the crispy fried eggplants which I thought was good but also came with chunk of cauliflower. I only took a photo of my dish where you can see the hunk of cauliflower.

Overall it was ok. I mean I think there was only my two waiters and ours was also mixing the drinks which is why we might not have seen him often, but he was pleasant enough when we did. And some of the food could've been better.

We were lured into the waterside Glashuset this afternoon by their sense of humor (see pics of signs), and we left happy to have enjoyed their appetizers and beer/wine. The fried artichokes, burrata & tomato, and duck croquettes were delicious. Our waitress gets a thumbs up for trying to teach me how to say "artichoke" in swedish (kronartskockor).

googleEditorialSummary: International, farm-to-table dishes offered in a refined, waterfront eatery with occasional DJ sets.

googleReviews.text:
I recently visited Thai & Sushi Globen and tried a couple of their dishes, including Tom Kha Gai (coconut chicken soup) and Pad Thai. Overall, my experience was mixed, with both positives and some disappointments.

**Tom Kha Gai**
The presentation was nice, served with a side of rice and fresh herbs on top. However, the soup contained an excessive amount of inedible ingredients like large pieces of galangal, lemongrass, and other fibrous elements that weren’t properly strained. While these ingredients add flavor, they shouldn't take up half the bowl or be mistaken for edible parts. This made eating the dish quite frustrating, as I had to pick out a lot of them manually. The coconut broth itself was creamy and mild, but the balance of flavors could have been better.

**Pad Thai**
The Pad Thai was visually appealing and had a good mix of vegetables, bean sprouts, and lime for freshness. The portion size was reasonable, and the dish looked flavorful. However, I would have liked to see a better balance of sauce and seasoning to really bring out the classic sweet-savory Pad Thai taste.

**Service & Overall Experience**
The plating was nice, and the portions were decent, but the quality of execution in the Tom Kha Gai left much to be desired. It felt like more attention was put into aesthetics rather than making the dishes fully enjoyable.

I would consider returning for other menu items, but I hope they improve the preparation of the soup to make it more enjoyable.

⭐ **Rating: 3/5** (Presentation was nice, but the Tom Kha Gai was difficult to eat)

Would I recommend it? If you love Thai food and don’t mind some preparation issues, it’s worth a try—but be prepared to do some extra work picking things out of your soup.

Very welcoming staff and yummy food.
Cozy place with soft sofas🙏
Took Teriyaki Chicken, Ba Mie Pad with chicken and Fried chicken from the kids’ menu!
Did not eat everything, so asked for takeout and had been given 2 nice boxes to take with!
