So If they improve the food quality, it could be a great go to place.

Food is great.. ambience is very good.. nice view in the outside seating..price is bit more costly... Food came to table within 5 mins which was a surprise..But there is one problem with this place... The person who takes the order was restless and was in some hurry and acted little rude while asking for food options and ingredients of a menu item..even my friend experienced the same..looks like they treat Indians that way😨 and others in a pleasant way...salad was very less and plates served was very small to place Naan bread on that.. need to hold ur bread and have it😮.. but otherwise everything was ok.. in a nutshell you can try it once..good luck with the order..cheers
The food used to be really good and it used to be my favourite restaurant but since November the food doesn't have much or even no taste, no spices at all.

This place used to be great and I ate here all the time. Despite the fact that the name, menu and sign remains the same, it would appear that this place has new owners (it is no longer listed on the Indian Garden website and the big photo of the owner has been removed). Sadly it has new owners that hires chefs that are not as good at cooking as the old ones and hires serving staff that obviously don't care very much about anything. The staff I could abide if the food was still as good as before. Sadly, it is not.

Been there twice for lunch and dinner during our visit to Stockholm last month. Every time had superb experience. The foods were very delicious, plentiful for us and the service was warm too! There is a Bangladeshi boy working for the dinner shift, whose service is beyond the normal - excellent in single word.

Would love to be there again.
I have been there with my colleague, became so disappointed. Food was ok but their service is very poor. Staff behaviour should be more polite with their customer
Amazing food. The service is a bit bare, but this is understandable at lunch time!
One of the better Indian restaurants in town.  Prices are a little higher than normal hence the 4 stars.  I’ve eaten takeout here a myriad of times - first time in person.

Visited Indian Kingdom Restaurant today and had a really lovely experience! We tried the Dhaka Biryani (spicy +), alu chop, garlic naan and mango shake. The biryani was amazing full of flavor and the quantity is perfect for one person. The mango shake was refreshing and delicious. I actually wanted another glass but was already too full 😅. They were super friendly and welcoming, which made the visit more worthy. Definitely a recommended place if you’re looking for good test and great service! We will visit again.
Food 10/10, service 10/10. Everything was very delicious.

Loved it! The food was amazing. Service was awesome as well. I’m gluten free and I double checked with him that the mixed grill option I got was gluten free. They have a lot of vegetarian options, too. I loved the lentil soup they gave us as a starter. Would definitely go again!

Absolutely delicious! Ordered the palak paneer, the lime tandoori chicken and garlic naan. The chicken was super tender and the sauce was so flavour full and perfectly spiced. The garlic naan was fluffy and crispy in all the right places. The staff was super nice and attentive. Don't miss out on the lunch options where you get complementary, free mango lassi, juice, water, tea, coffee and soup. Prices were also super reasonable. Highly recommend!

Indian Kingdom in Stockholm is a hidden gem! Fantastic dinner with great service. The food is tasty, and the portions are generous. The atmosphere is nice, quiet, and peaceful with attentive staff. Highly recommend for an authentic Indian dining experience1!

I absolutely loved my visit to Indigo Bar in Stockholm! Tucked away on Götgatan, it has this cozy, artsy vibe that makes you feel right at home the moment you step inside. The lighting is cool and colorful, the decor quirky in the best way, and the music sets the perfect mood—relaxed but with just enough energy to keep the night going.

The cocktails? Amazing. I had a rhubarb fizz that was hands down one of the best drinks I’ve had in the city. The bartenders were not only skilled but super friendly—they even offered to mix something custom based on what I liked. You can tell they care about the experience, not just the drinks.

It got busier later in the evening, but even then it didn’t feel overwhelming—just lively and fun. The crowd was friendly and a mix of locals and visitors, which made it feel really welcoming.

If you’re looking for a laid-back but stylish spot to kick back with great drinks and even better vibes, Indigo is the place. I’ll definitely be back next time I’m in Stockholm!

I had a really disappointing experience here today. I ordered a Cola Zero and was given a regular Coke instead. When I pointed it out, the employee refused to fix the mistake and was rude and dismissive to me and my friends. This happened right when the place opened, which made the poor attitude even more surprising.
Very unprofessional service and a bad first impression. The guy had a shaved haircut.
great cocktails, loved the espresso martini style drink that had a nice sweet cream top. the rhubarb fizz was also nice, not too sweet. staff was friendly

Well, I am not even able to comment on drinks or anything else, because of the service girl that is at the bar today (14/12 since 8pm or sth around that). My mom wanted to go out to the bar and we're on holiday together, so even though I don't drink I went out with her as a company. My mom has sight defect and also her English isn't the best so I was helping her to choose something to drink for herself but told her that she should order for herself because I didn't even take my ID with me, because I don't drink, as I said. When my mom wanted to order, problems started, this girl told us that she needs to see my ID and she won't sell us anything (probably because my mom wanted to order 2 or 3 things to try them out, but I didn't see any sign that doing so is forbidden). I understand that alcohol selling laws in Sweden are strict but the place is small so it would be rather easy to observe that I really don't drink and I checked that the place itself is not marked as 18+ to enter and sit (I'm sorry if it's written in the law or sth - still she didn't tell us anything like that). She started to say that she doesn't believe us, implying that we want to lie to her, which is pathetic to say at least. Finally, we found my passport so she could see that I'm over 18 (nearly 20 in fact), but we showed it to her only to prove her wrong, because I wouldn't want someone like this to prepare my drink or even be close to me.
Maybe she just likes to suspect and valify customers 🤷🏼‍♀️.
I don't want to give the place 1 star in overall, because it looked nice and cozy, but this girl definitely deserves 1 star or less and is underreporting this place.
Great bar really funny staff

Wow! This will be our new sushi spot! The quality seemed to be so so good. I really enjoyed the taste and flavors. They have a wide range of rolls to chose from. I especially liked the location and the diverse range of options with salmon. The prices were also very reasonable. I really hope the quality and service remains as we would hopefully come here for the foreseeable future. I loved it. Also the staff are so so kind.

It doesn’t matter how much you are good in Sushi, it is matter how you host your guests that they feel comfortable. We didn’t see it that way. They were not welcome us and after I asked them if I can sit and order they wanted me to order first. The sushi was normal. The miso soup didn’t have seaweed, and after I asked they only said no we don’t have! They forgot to bring vasaby for my sushi!

I ATE MY SUSHI BUT WITHOUT TRUST!

And all is coming from how you respect your guests. It was my last time visit that place and I don’t recommend it! There are much more better sushi’s restaurants in the city!
Had the lite fried salmon roll with kimchi here yesterday. Absolutely amazing! The flavours were spot on and even the open-serve soup was surprisingly delicious. Great place, definitely coming back!

Was the only customer eating in. Read the reviews before going and it wa definitely as good as the reviews mentioned.

I did have to wait a while s the staff qere busy preparing food for delivery. Besides the wait everything else was fantastic.

The food was prepared with care. The plate looked beautiful and the taste ws great. Out of all the restaurantexperience in stockholm, this one made me feel like I got value for money. (Stockholm is expensive for me, so it's a subjective opinion)

The restaurant was a short walk from out hostel, so we decided to give it a try. And we couldn’t have chosen a better spot. Free miso soup, calm ambiance, polite staff, fresh and delicious sushi. Just get you translate app ready if you don’t understand Swedish, that’s the only language in the menu.

Just been declined entry because I was wearing an Adidas sweater - and a good one at that! We were three gay guys having our first beer of the evening and were not allowed to order because of a dress code? Very modern! Very insane… get better staff, I beg!

International Bar is a gem in the heart of Kungsholmen. Here you'll find selections of great international brews.

The service is outstanding, with friendly and knowledgeable staff who are happy to offer recommendations.

One of the highlights is the great outdoor seating area. It provides a relaxed atmosphere with comfortable seating on a lively street, perfect for enjoying a beer.

International Bar combines great beer, excellent service, and a pleasant environment, making it a must-visit in Kungsholmen.

I wore a shirt with a rainbow on it and the security guard wouldn't let me enter because I was "too drunk".  I had 0 drinks prior to that and had used no substances or anything like that.  Apparently discrimination is real here

Cozy atmosphere. Really service minded staff. Food tasty and reasonable prices.
Kungsholmen bar is coziest of them all.
Fries portion was cheap, hearty and good, the beer was cheaper than average, service was quick and nice. Atmosphere was friendly and relaxed. I would definitely go back.

name: Internationella Pressklubben

googleReviews.text:
Pretty good lunch with three main options, plus a nice salad bar and fresh bread.
Even though there wasn’t a vegan option listed on the menu, they were able to offer a vegan meal, which was great. The food was tasty overall — but unfortunately, one of the dishes had pieces of eggshell in it, which was a bit disappointing :(

Fantastic experience at this restaurant! The atmosphere was really pleasant and cozy. The dishes were delicious, and the waiter was very friendly and attentive. Special mention for the beer menu: an enormous variety of Belgian beers, and despite our knowledge on the subject, he managed to help us discover some hidden gems. Highly recommend this place for a great time!

It is a cozy and nice place , it is a heaven for beer lovers. The food was delicious, but I think the portion size was a bit small for the price. However, the staff were friendly and very attentive. If you decide to try it, I have no doubt you'll have a lovely dining experience. Thank you for the pleasant experience!

Lovely place with tones of atmosphere, the selection of beers is out of this world Im guessing over 50 but I can be corrected, they also had the odd Cider and wine for those not wanting beer. The food was very good, I had mussels to start which had fabulous flavour's. Burger for a main which was great but I was stuffed from eating too much bread with the mussels sopping up all the source!

To commence with- the quantity could be bit less(pertaining to food only)compared to what one would have expected, but they excel in quality, authenticity and service. They were out of mussels and monkfish which was a pity, however the appetiser with bone marrow was exquisite(well first time for me, so I was tad bit confused on which portion to eat and how :) and the Belgian truffle dessert was tiny, but delicious. In the beer department however, there were plethora of options and the few I tried all were awesome.

googleEditorialSummary: Sophisticated restaurant offering a range of refined European dishes & 750+ kinds of Belgian beers.

googleReviews.text: This was a perfect dining experience. The food was superb! Fresh and made with care. The drinks were just as good. We were taken excellent care of from beginning to end, even with it being a fully booked dining hall! Daniel was our waiter and if you’re lucky enough to have him taking care of you, you’re in for a great evening! He is so personal and professional, he spent time with every guest while managing to keep a steady flow. Nothing was rushed, he delivered everything with a smile! We will be returning for sure!

First time here at Invence restaurant the pasta was cooked perfectly, rich of flavor, fresh ingredient fresh seafood and absolutely amazing service. Courtesy of ( MR. KARLOS ) Thank you so much appreciated i'll definitely be back here soon 👍🏻

Recommended for reservation on the weekday

We came here after not being able to eat at pelikan due no seating when we went around 6pm. Invece was great second choice with great food and really great service. For five people sitting on two tiny table was hard but we made it work.

We had gotten the fried calamari thinking we were going to get a bunch but it was literally only five pieces which I guess was just enough for the five of us but I wanted more. They were bigger than normal but still, more than five would've been nice. They had it with a aioli sauce not marinara which was also good airline. Three of us ordered pasta and two ordered meat. There was the seafood linquine with shrimp, mussel and squid. I heard everyone raving over it. I thought the linguine was a bit thicker than normal but the taste was nice. Another ordered the spaghetti with clams and also everyone raving about it and the clams. Next is the tortellini which i heard was delicious as well. Finally the meat dish which were the ribeye orderd medium and was nice and soft. Then the chicken with the truffle arancini was also moist and delicious.

Mainly the service was great. Our waiter Daniel was really nice to nice and super helpful. When we originally went in there was a huge group of female  teenagers that were really loud and kinda rude but they left maybe 15 mins in. Daniel was really nice to us and even gave us a few tiramisu for one of our birthday boy which we didn't expect. He had said the group of girls expected their desserts to be free and we laughed with him saying why and said we have a birthday boy as well. We def didn't expect him to give us anything and was joking around with him so it was nice o.f him. Also the food was great overall so it was a great night.

It was my first time in Stockholm and I was trying so hard to find something nice for Christmas dinner.
I will not say a lot, because I’m getting in tears.
The way everything tasted , looked , smelled.. MY GOD…
Inside this place ,is hidden a treasure. Please whoever reads my comment…trust me!
This man at the service and the chef..will definitely make your night!
Thank you people. Thank you for making us happy…

Really enjoyed my visit to this restaurant. The outdoor seating area is lovely and adds a nice touch to the overall atmosphere, which was relaxed and inviting. The food was delicious, I had pasta for the main course and absolutely loved it.

A moderate authentic Thai restaurant with above average food quality.
The pork belly was juicy in sauce but the texture of the skin was a bit too hard to chew on and the texture of the meat was not tender enough. The odor of the pork was properly removed, which was to my liking, and the sauce was flavorful. The duck and fish dishes I ordered were unfortunately the same in seasonings, I should have asked beforehand. They were both paired with coriander and red onions, together with lemongrass and spring onions, the combination of aromas reminds you of a Thai vacation. The fish was throughly cooked and even the tiny bones were deep fried until edible. The duck meat was a bit dry, but the seasoning went deep into the meat so it was still not bad. I would like to try more fish dishes from them next time.

Quite nice Thai place - nice deco inside the restaurant, very service oriented waiter and waitress, and very tasty food. If one just takes the main course, it might be on a relatively small side. With a starter, things work out nicely!

We went here based on reviews and we did not regret it. We ordered plenty of food, a party of four, and myself had the pad Thai chicken. Everything tasted really authentic and the Namtok, isaan style, tasted great and brought back good memories from Thailand. This place we highly recommend, and if we have come back to Sundbyberg we'll come back here for sure.

Authentic Thai food!

There is wide variety of dishes to choose from. I got to try some different ones: papaya salad, fried rice, pork belly and crispy rice. They all were very tasty, spicy and flavorful. I've been to Thailand before and I can tell the food here was pretty authentic. They will also let you choose the spicy level.
One of the best Thai restaurants in Sundbyberg. You can order some insanely spicy authentic Thai food. Didn't like the fried banana dessert though

I took lunch (Poke bowl). Taste was good. Avocado could be served better, like cut in a proper way instead of served whole as we eat with sticks it was inconvenient. Also when I paid, the person standing in the kassa didn’t inform me about what else is included. Like soup for example. Had to figure out. When I asked about the order number she said that she already gave the number to the person who came with me. Food was fresh though. Prices were ok too. Do not expect high level of service. Food is good.

Visited for lunch on a Sunday. Very uncrowded. Excellent staff, friendly and attentive. Ordered bibimap and chicken pokebowl (both recommended by waiter), both were excellent. Place was clean, service quick, food fresh and very tasty. Easy with kids too.

I like sushi and comparing new places with simple menu. I was pleased to enjoy Itamae's sushi 11 pieces. Dining was challenged  by eating it on a bench during sunny Sunday. eveything fit to my larger palms and the soya sauce had perfect shape to dip the sushi in the little bowl. There was unnecessary amount of fried oninon as almost half of it fell down from rolls.
Surprisingly sumptuous burger. Crispy fragrant fries came with a truffle flavoured dip. Salmon chirashi was average but portion was generous.
Great sushi, delicous and fresh! On point! Very friendly staff…

Very close to the Affordable Art Fair location.
As we were too early, we first had drinks on the cozy heated outside seating which comes with plaids. I also discovered a great local gin.
Our waiter, Ilis, did a good job.
We had schnitzel which looked rather “funky” compared to the classic germanic one, but it was good. The burger was also good and came with a huge portion of fries. We did all think that the food was a little too spicy.

A gem of a place with a stunning view of the sea canal and life and buzz upon it.

Great food, especially fish. It takes a bit long to wait in between courses but it’s always worth a wait.
Super nice Resturant , the location is wonderful just by the ferry station, the food was amazing and the staff were lovely!

Very nice place to eat fresh fish and shellfish near the harbour. The dishes were served with the utmost care and our service was excellent, from a very busy but attentive server, and as in most places in Stockholm you never feel rushed.

Very pleasant restaurant, right on the harbour. Food very fresh (as you would expect) if a little on the salty side (Swedes aren't averse to a bit of salt).
Pricy, but realistically for the location and the area it's never going to be cheap.

Service was excellent, from a very busy but attentive server, and as in most places in Stockholm you never feel rushed.

name: Jensen's Bøfhus

googleReviews.text: A very rich buffet with excellent quality. You can eat a wide variety of dishes from the buffet and also order freshly prepared hot meals. Ebba, our waiter, was exceptionally professional and welcoming.

We come to this restaurant once every year. This time I had Oysters, softshell crab and venison. They were all delicious. My wife had monk fish and the kids had kötbullar and cheeseburger. They were all happy. It is very expensive though, but I guess this is what you have to pay in central Stockholm in a cosy restaurant. The service was very good too.

In the middle of Stockholm. Yummy food. Got the traditional meatballs, mashed potatoes and a very yummy berry sauce. The dessert was so good. I think it was the strawberry shortcake. It was the best! The waitress was very helpful with the options. I stayed at a hotel closeby so this place was very accessible with a beautiful atmosphere

We had a business lunch. Reservation was made a week ahead. One of us was vegetarian and we barely found one dish in the menu that was vegetarian and even that we had doubts about as the name of the sauce would suggest that it could be made from fish. There was no separate section called “vegetarian”. Food was tasting fine. At the end we ordered coffee. The waiter came with a bottle of milk not even explaining what kind of milk it was, not giving a chance to ask as the person was in a rush. While pouring the coffee she spilled the coffee to the table and left without cleaning. My colleague was about to touch the spilled coffee with his arm (suit) and I managed to dry the table with the napkin. One of us was lactose intolerant and when we asked for lactose free milk it took eternity to bring it and by the time the milk arrived coffee was finished as we don’t drink it cold. The face expression of the waiter was as if we owned money to her and it was overdue for several years :) feeling uncomfortable every time we needed to talk to her. So I am not considering coming back here. Bye bye.

I was staying a the hotel BUT this not the reason I visited the restaurant. I’d heard good things and my goodness this was accurate. The food was sentimentally good, the wine was equally as good and the service was perfectly paced. Tuna tacks to start were amazing, streak was beautiful and the desert was lovely. Would come back here in a heartbeat x

googleEditorialSummary: A sleek modern hotel restaurant specializing in seafood dishes, including sharing platters.

googleReviews.text:
Jhonny’s is one of my favourite places to go for either lunch or dinner and my favourite dish there is grilled salmon. You also get some starter salad. The fish is cooked in a way that it tastes so delicious 🤤.

They also have good pastas. Haven’t tried their pizza yet.

The staff working there is amazing, no words! They welcome everyone and make sure that you will leave with a smile on your face and may crack some jokes 😄.
My favorite place in Stockholm. Everyone who works there is amazing! The food is fantastic! Try the steak or the calzone, but any other pizza go well, also! s2
The pizzas are slightly better and more fresh than avrage pizzerias. Cozy place to sit and relax and the staff is kind

Best pizzas within a radius of at least 20, 30 km.

While toppings are not of premium quality ingredients, the sauce does taste tomato and the crust is definitely one of the best in town.

The restaurant has an air of typical suburban pizzeria, relaxed and casual. Anyway you come here for the pizza.
Great pizzas for a reasonable price.

I was disappointed with the sushi.
The ramen was ok and the speciale rice was not good. Though the satff were so nice.
I have lived around the area for a few years now and have also ordered here in the past, and I must say that their sushi has improved lately! Always appreciate their smiles every time I go 😊
So far the only place where one can find authentic niu rou mian (beef noodle soup) in Stockholm. And thank goodness it's super yummy!
The sushi is somewhat disappointing. The shrimp tasted... Off, and the rice was poorly seasoned. If you visit here, I recommend getting any of the hot dishes instead

Supercool to find a local sushi/Thai that does homemade Chinese dumplings, definitely recommend! The sushi is okay, haven't tried the Thai dishes and the beef ramen is essentially a niu rou mian (beef noodle soup) which was a pleasant surprise. If you live in the area and need a Chinese food fix, Jintao does the job :)

Repeat Visits (Dine-In)
2 Mamas with 2 Full-sized Strollers
2 Babies (7m & 5m)

Great location and Stroller Friendly with lots of room to move, but there is one step up off the street which is easily manageable. Friendly staff and quick service. Both individual chairs/tables and bench seating. Nice and comfortable Lounge/Couch area in the back. Has a standard baby change area in a large bathroom.
We came here for the first time on the first day of Stockholm, and we loved it! Keep it up! Cheers

Their food and their juices are delicious, but the chain quality went down these days. And they used to be good since the first time I went there several years ago. Last time I visited, the place was very dirty, with flies all over the place.

Unstable quality. Sometimes good, sometimes bad. I usually go for the avo shake at Joe, but today it was watery, mostly vanilla milk and hints of avocado. Unfortunately I was not able to tell them on site, but I really shouldn’t need to..
Go to another Joe nearby.
extremely noizy place. if u dream of a calm morning moment with a cup of coffee - look for another place. and its not because of visitors, no. the personel is loud as a bunch of seagulls.

name: Johan & Nyström

googleReviews.text:
This coffee shop has a really nice vibe. The staff are friendly and always make you feel welcome.

There’s a pleasant aroma of coffee in the air, and the coffee itself is consistently good, probably one of the best you can find around Stockholm.

I come here quite often, and it’s always a reliable spot for a quality cup and a comfortable place to sit. Definitely worth a visit if you’re nearby.

The coffee is absolutely fantastic, the staff is kind and helpful. Every time I come here, I know to expect exceptional quality and sustainable products. I bought coffee beans and had them ground for mocha pot and just had my coffee at home that tastes sublime, the barista helped me select the most suitable option for me. Strongly recommend this spot to coffee lovers.
I have not came across many quality coffee spots like this coffee shop during my visit to Stockholm, regarding its unique design. Beautiful coffee and pastries. Had lovely experience.
Great cafe, will be even better when they discover the revolutionary fact that some cups and mugs also have handles, meaning the customer can actually pick up HOT COFFEE without risk of injury.

lovely matcha ! and nice atmosphere even tho shop is small. would go back again, esp in summer w outdoor seating. pastry nice but dry, i understand not being able to keep baking them fresh but wish they at least had some way of keeping them warm during the day

googleEditorialSummary: Vibrant, neighborhood coffee shop offering espresso, pastries & light fare, plus sidewalk seating.

googleReviews.text:
John Scott's Kungsträdgården: Perfect Pub Vibes by the Park! 🍻🌳

John Scott's in Kungsträdgården is my absolute favorite spot for a relaxed and fun time in the city! Its location right by the beautiful park is unbeatable, making it perfect for both an after-work unwind or a weekend hangout.

They've got a fantastic selection of beers on tap, delicious pub grub that always hits the spot, and a lively, welcoming atmosphere that's just right. Whether you're catching a game, meeting friends, or just enjoying the buzz, the staff are always friendly and keep things running smoothly.

It's truly a reliable go-to for great drinks, good food, and excellent company in a prime Stockholm location. Five stars for consistently delivering a top-notch pub experience!
Friendly staff. Great food. Great atmosphere.  Definitely would return. The vibe and music was awesome.  When your singing while eating that's how you know your having a great time

This was a great pub to enjoy some delicious food and sit outside by the fountains (or inside if you prefer).  We had the fish and chips and my son got the Swedish meatballs. It was all delicious!  The fish was fried beautifully, the fries were outstanding.  The meatballs looked hand made instead of manufactured, the mashed potatoes were creamy and rich, and the lingonberrys were a perfect balance of sweet and sour.  I also really liked the cucumbers in vinegar that came with the meatballs. We came back later for dessert and it was pretty great as well!  The guys had ice cream and I had the brownie that had chocolate ice cream and raspberry sauce. It was a perfect balance of sweet and tangy!  Highly recommend eating here!  They also have a nice drink selection.

Very nice place , near to a water fountain. The food and the service is correct.
They use an automated order when you can order food through a web application, very useful and make the service quick and efficient.
I recommend the shrimp toast , very tasty and the quantity is more than enough.
Well  recommended if you are looking for a nice place to enjoy with friends in Stockholm.

Enjoyed eating here tremendously. The meatballs were prepared perfectly, service was fast and efficient, and the view of the fountain was lovely!
Whatever challenges they had when they first opened, they have figured them out and are much better now.

name: John Scott's

googleReviews.text: Sports bar, bowling. Live sport from tv. Nice service, good food and of course big choice of beer. It is hard to get seat. Live line. Will come back defenately!

It's modelled like a British pub but it's nothing like a British pub as in it's a civilised place full of civilised people having a nice time, I really enjoyed it, more so than any British pub I have ever been to! Prices for a pint are a bit eye watering but we're in Sweden so that has to be expected, still had a great time and the service is really good! It comes with my full recommendation!

It has a bowling alley if you're into that kind of thing and they show ice hockey on the telly which being British has as much interest to me as flower arranging, table tennis or diving off a board but that doesn't detract from the fact I had a nice, peaceful night there - it was a wonderful night.

EDIT: I went back last night and I want to pay tribute to the staff on both floors (because there are two floors and two bowling alleys) they were amazing! If I lived in Stockholm this would be my 'local' whereas in the UK I don't have a local because pub culture in the UK is massively stressful to my sensitive nature! Completely recommend this pub as a place to hang out and chill if you are ever in Stockholm.

Great bar with fantastic staff. We spent the whole day here, yes 11 hours & was looked after by the staff all day. Some great beer prices too, specially in happy hour. Food was also well priced and tasty. Bowling alley at back if that's your game !  Nice outdoor area when the sun is shining! Good DJ on at night too!

We were at John Scotts every single evening for the last seven days. The only evening we weren’t there was because it was closed. That alone says enough. We kept coming back every night because we had such a great time there.

The staff is incredibly friendly, the drinks are good, and the overall vibe is just really nice and easygoing. It quickly became our go-to spot during our stay.

If I’m ever back in Stockholm, this will definitely be one of the places I return to. Thank you to the whole staff of John Scotts for supplying us with the good vibes and drinks every evening all week.

14.01.2023

My wife and one of our friend went there. We had some drinks, and ordered Nachos as well. The pub is huge and a lot of game plau space too (For bowling and other games). The servers, tenders were extremely useful, and it was a great experience.

+ Lot of Space
+ Crowded but still space available during Saturday night.
+ Games available
+ Decent pub prices
+ Good food
+ Huge washroom

googleEditorialSummary: American-inspired menu, plus alcoholic shakes in retro-style diner & bar set within a bowling-alley.

googleReviews.text:
This bar has everything you could ever dream of! Live jazz music, rock, pop. All kinds of pizza, pasta, drinks, and beers. You can buy cool bracelets or attend a quiz night and win prizes. Maybe watch something fun on TV while smoking a cigarette in the smoke room. If you’re feeling social (most Swedes are not), you can sit at the bar and chat with Antoine.

When in Vällingby, don’t ever miss out on Joseph’s Bar.
You can este&Drink good things!!
Lovely staff and great food as usual!
Delicious pizza
God pizza

They have a great selection of different food for breakfast, lunch and brunch.
The drink selection ranged from hot to cold, including some alcoholic beverages like Portuguese wine.
We had a shrimp sandwich, grilled croissant and avocado smash sandwich, all tasty.
The staff was friendly and helpful too.
Super cute and cozy local coffee shop in Solna. Very friendly staff. I wish I could try the sweets but was too full with the avocado smash already. I love the latte. Definitely will come back.

It looked really cozy on the outside and on Google. But, it wasn’t as I hoped for at all.

It’s way over priced for what you get in my opinion.

The macha latte was filled with clumps and not stirred at all.

The avocado smash was the size of an appetizer.

My girlfriend got the Carrot Cake was super sugary.

The burrata was very cold and had the consistency of a mozzarella.

And, it was dirty all over the floor.

But, if this is the only place in the neighbourhood I can understand why people go there.

It's a cute little cafe with a great variety of eating and drinking options. They have lunch, sandwiches, and fika. It's a bit crowded. I just wish cafes in Solna would be open after work or longer on weekends. Unfortunately, the staff is not so friendly, and it is a bit overpriced.

Really nice place for a Sunday brunch! Cute spot with a 'beach' vibe. Although it seems quite small, there are a lot of tables, both inside and outside. Everything we tried was delicious. They have nice breakfast packages as well!

Excellent carbonara and shrimp salad, very nice during summer when you can sit outside (they have beautiful outdoor seating considering it’s central Stockholm). Fast service, very fair price.

Cozy atmosphere with nice staff. Got coffee on the house ✌️

This is where I wanna live my life. I live and die by Café Arte 🫡

The pancakes were stale and microwaved, the man in the kitchen touched them with his bare hands to check if they were done. Very gross kitchen and dining area. Even without a pandemic it seems that this place is a prime location for bacteria and sickness.

0/5 I would rather go hungry
Nice place to visit with family

Hi!
Nice Café with atmosphere in a creative environment.

A good selection of pastries, sandwiches, food and drinks.

A popular hangout, for everyone

251128/Kjell.
Quite an okay place! Not the first option I think of, but the ice cream they had was ok. Had a salad and some pastry too which could have been tastier and fresher.

I would move to Sweden just for this Cafe. The salmon today was close to a Michelin Star, the staff very friendly. Located on a little hill midst Stockholm, this cannot get any better. An excellent choice!
Cozy little cafe next to Stockholm's old observatory. Tried their cookies which were very nice, but they didn't taste very good.

We loved it! It was a pleasant discovery after arriving on the hilltop. The coffee and the cakes were very good, the view of the city is the best we had.
You have to order at the counter. The staff is very friendly.

Note: it was October 9th 2020 in the middle of the corona pandemic.
Inside is quite small for ordering (yo feel easily crammed).

Coffee and water were in self-service, which was nice but not exactly germ-free (in Germany, finding a place like that is currently impossible) - all the reason to enjoy this little freedom with own measures!

Do you feel like having coffee, eating or watching a live band in the open sky, on a mountain, or in Stockholm? Does that sound crazy? It is. Himlavalvet is a cozy spartan cafe housed in the observatory grove's premises up on Stockholm's only mountain peak in central Stockholm. Good food, cozy atmosphere and cool bands playing on the outdoor stage. All this you can get for a pittance, with the Milky Way as your roof and with only a faint murmur of the noise of the city below the mountain. Bring a jacket and cool sunglasses and enjoy!

Very nice cafe and very welcoming people, thank you for everything! The waffle is absolutely crazyyy. Love from Switzerland 🇨🇭♥️

N.B. : THE PEEPER (Melvin will understand)

It is so nice to have such a gem in the neighbourhood. Great service and atmosphere, I love to spend time here. On top of that, great baking, and tasty sandwiches and soups. Everything I have tried has been delicious
The cosiest, loveliest cafe in Stockholm! The owner is super nice, I always practice my Swedish with her! The coffee is great too!

It’s a lovely cafe that has a very calm and nice atmosphere. I do wish there were a few more option for drinks/ food but i understand why a small cafe isn’t able to have like a 100 different options available.
The things you could get were really good, I recommend it if you want a nice and calm place to relax, read or study :)
Damn fine cup of coffee, excellent food, beautiful atmosphere, fantastic place

Beautiful calm little Café. I come by sometimes, drink a coffee and read a book. Best place for that. The soups and pies are very tasty aswell. Seems like a one person business which makes it even more honorable. Highly recommended!

It’s a very good place for breakfast or brunch.  The waffles were amazing and their sambal chicken sandwich was delicious. I love their fresh juice mix, especially the beet, ginger some fresh juice mix.
The interior decoration is lovely as well. There are many outdoor seating.

Good place to meet up professionally or casual meet up. You may want to the busy times since there are few one on one seating spots. If so gett there earlier would be great to get a good spot. Staff are friendly and regular pricing. Meal presentation is great. Quantity is ok - not too light not much either.

For a relaxing atmosphere, visit at the end of the day or at opening time. At other times of the day, this bistro tends to be crowded.

The menu is not very big but here you can find breakfast, sandwiches, some warm dishes, salads and a decent offering of pastries and cakes.

The seating is comfortable - tables, high chairs, booths, window views of Luma Park and a few tables outdoors on the pavement.

It is possible to relax with a beer or wine when the crowd dwindles but this place feels more like a family restaurant / fika / business lunch venue than a drinks & mingle destination.

BEER : Bernard ⭐⭐⭐
Dark golden lager. Bubbly, enjoyable but very bitter. The glass is cool but the beer would be nicer if it was colder.

Good service. Clean and modern.
A great new place in hammarby. This is great for breakfasts n brunch. Their luxury sandwich is too good. Chai latte was to die for, so super yumm with all the right flavors.
My favorite place in the area, everything is fantastic here 👌 first of all the service is the best and everyone’s smiling. Second everything on the menu is REALLY good
Sat outside and ordered inside. I had the chicken sandwich which was very yummy. It did take a little while to come out, but the matcha was great! Recommend for some food and people watching!

Fantastic food, some great vegan options that were full of flavour.  They have quite a big menu with brunch options.

I would recommend the vegan bowl, pictured.

Coffee is good too.
On a busy street.  Food was really good and so was the coffee. Coffee arrived a little spilled. I loved the falafel sandwich which was vegan!
The most delicious sandwiches. Friendly service, it was busy, so it took a while until the food arrived but it was well worth the wait.
I have been in there last week. Everybody so kind and helpfull. Asspecialy brunette lady, tall one. Thanks for her. And tomato soup is perfect also. Taste of it still my mind.

The cafe that we regularly visit. I love their healthy bowls, salads, grilled sandwiches and lentil soup. They serve their latte in huge, elegant glasses. Friendly staff, delicious food! I strongly recommend!

Uppdate: Last week the lentil soup we ate was extremely spicy. We asked for extra cream in order to not to complaint. However, we later got sick.

We have been eating this soup for years, it has never been so.

I was directed to the manager who  sorted the issue out & even encouraged us to inform them in case something is wrong.

I am glad that our constructive feedback will be of help, as this place tries to keep the high standard they have had for many years.

Yet, please be careful about the lentil soup :)

Ok fika. I ordered a decaf cappuccino. Decaf does often taste bad, but here they tasted like standard cappuccino, which was nice.

Cute ourdoor seating with calm sorroundings. Many seatings available.
Nice cafe with friendly staff but do NOT get the Matcha Latte. I love matcha lattes but 75 kr for something that tasted like dishwater, not worth it. Do not recommend, stick to coffee y’all💀
Ordered two matchas — tasty. Only downside: they weren’t brought to the table. Lots of tiny birds around, which was cute. There’s only one toilet shared by men and women.

Good ambience.. Though you have to walk a distance from Skanstull station.. If not you can take the bus 66 to alight about 150-200 metres from this Cafe. Tasty treats await you. The coffee mugs were themselves special large and had a unique touch to them.. Small seating area can seat about 30 to 40 at anytime.. Has washrooms as well.
A coffee shop with soul. The coffee is rich and flavorful, and the French press is exquisite. The ambiance is lovely, with energetic yet not noisy music. The owner is exceptionally friendly.

The staff here are aggressively unwelcoming, I could not understand why I was being treated this way, and judging from other’s similar experiences my conclusion is that those who run this place are suffering mental health issues or personal trauma and letting it out on unassuming café customers. I cannot recall ever having being treated this way before, as if we were dirt, a nuisance, almost less than human. It seems many fellow reviewers are echoing the same sentiment, and that at least gives me some consolation, that my friend and I (black and brown people) were not targeted by the staff solely for our ethnicities. Either way, we cannot support a business that not only fails to provide good service, but actively bullies clients. Engage in some introspection please and reflect on how you can treat people better.

It’s a nice spot but the service is weird, didnt feel good interacting. They do take cards now so no worries lol and the toasts were fine, def good flavor, though I expected more crayfish than what I got (see photo)
It’s pretty peaceful here as well so in short not a terrible spot when other coffee spots are overcrowded.

Kaffe in Stockholm has some strong points, no doubt. Their coffee is excellent, boasting a rich and satisfying flavor. Additionally, their toast offerings are nothing short of delicious – a great accompaniment to a cup of coffee.

However, there's a rather notable "but" to my experience. Upon visiting, I ordered my coffee and toast, settled the bill, and then came the unexpected surprise. I was informed that computer use is not permitted on weekends. This unexpected policy caught me off guard and left me feeling slightly disappointed.

While I understand the desire to maintain a certain ambiance, the restriction on computer use on weekends could be more clearly communicated to customers beforehand. Such policies might impact the overall experience for those who planned to get some work done or enjoy a leisurely browsing session.

In conclusion, Kaffe offers a great coffee and culinary experience, but the weekend computer restriction is something to consider if you had other plans in mind. Clear communication of such policies could enhance the overall customer experience.

A very bad experience. The owner treats tourists poorly and is disgusting and rude. She claimed he didn't have internet, but everyone next door was using Wi-Fi. Thanks to the customers at the next table, I was told it was actually connected. A place full of lies. It seems the owner has a bad temper and is depressed. She needs to see a therapist. Her life is also very difficult. She’s probably struggling financially and taking his unhappiness out on her customers.

A very bad dining experience, because we wanted to take a rest after the meal. After packing up, the waiter asked us if we wanted to order other food. After we refused, he immediately asked us to pay. There was no smile at all and the expression on his face was not good. He kept hinting us to leave quickly.
Btw food is nice.

Excellent experience! The food was absolutely superb. We indulged in some truly tasty Swedish cuisine, particularly the meatballs and skormarkalåda, which were both delightful. Our plates were left squeaky clean, a testament to how much we enjoyed every bite!

The service was equally impressive; the crew was very friendly and accommodating, making us feel right at home. I highly recommend this place to anyone looking to enjoy authentic Swedish flavors!
Dining alone. The staff and owner were very friendly. We had a little chat.😀 I enjoyed my onion soup, perfect for the cold night. Meatballs was authentic and tasty. I had a good dinner time here.

Kaffegillet Restaurang och Café

We had our dinner here. The staff were friendly and allowed us to share the dishes, so we didn’t need to order one per person. They also checked in with us from time to time to make sure everything was okay. The food was delicious. Even though the restaurant was busy, our food was served quickly. They accept both cash and credit card for payment.

Lovely, cosy restaurant with lovely, very attentive staff and delicious food! I had the salmon and vegetables and it was great. Traditional Swedish restaurant definitely worth a visit when in Gamla Stan!
The semla is awesome, it has bits of crunchy almonds and the bread is super fresh and soft. Brygg kaffe is also very good, I think it is Lavazza and it is served hot.
We’re tasty and always fresh pastry. Very recommended.

You walk in and it smells amazing and the food looks amazing. The two ladies behind the counter were both sweet and helpful. I had an amazing blueberry/raspberry muffin and a cheese/ham sandwich. Will be going back regularly for the rest of my stay here.
Cozy, nice atmosphere, delicious smells and, more importantly, delicious food. Child friendly.

Nice location with good food and excellent staff.
You get 15% discount on your review.

Nice spot right by the water with beautiful views and a relaxed atmosphere. Perfect place to sit outside on a sunny day and enjoy the sea breeze. The vibe is modern and cozy.

That said, the prices are on the higher side compared to what you get, and service can be a bit slow at times. Worth a visit for the location and ambience, but not the best option if you’re looking for value for money.

I recently stumbled upon this charming little restaurant during an unplanned walk in Stockholm, and it turned out to be a delightful discovery. After a long, chilly stroll, the inviting interior caught my eye—it's stylish, modern, and exudes a chic atmosphere with lounge music playing in the background.

I initially had different plans but decided to go with the flow and ended up here. The Seether Salad I ordered was delicious, especially the sauce, though I found the individual lettuce leaves a tad too large for my taste. Nonetheless, they provide knives, so that wasn't a major issue. I paired it with a fitting rosé, and overall, I was extremely pleased. Not only did it warm me up, but I also spent quality time in this noteworthy establishment.

I plan to revisit intentionally, perhaps during the summer. It was an unexpected gem, and I look forward to experiencing it again with purpose.

A new favorite restaurant in Nacka Strand. I come here very often to get a drink in the hot sun, coffee when in need it, lunch or dinner from the food trucks or Kajen's restaurant. I come here to relax, read a book, see the sunsets, spot the sea traffic, be close to the water, meet friends, listen to live jazz or salsa music. This place has so much to offer, recommended!

Wonderful! This place reminds me of my life in North America. I love trying out new restaurants but I’ve learned that in Stockholm city, it’s not the best idea to just randomly walk in any restaurant.

We had a vegetarian dish here; it was so delicious. It’s inspired me to try more vegetarian dishes. I would love to come back.

The staff was friendly but slow. The espresso was served cold as well as the food. It was tasty but it was cold, we didn’t want to complain because there was a lot of people waiting for the food and we were hungry.

Nice café by the water. You’ll have a great view while enjoying an icecream or a easy lunch. They especially have morning and midday sun here.

The staff were nice, but not too friendly. The icecream was really good though. They had a selection of “mjukglass”, “kulglass” and GB icecreams. Great place if you wanna grab something to go or go for a lunch.

Nice snackbar in a sunny spot by the water. Sells an ok selection of sausages with a pretty decent amount of different condiments and mashed potatoes. Also you can get a "fika" here with an assortment of ice cream, cookies and coffee.
Bang on location and, for a kiosk anyway, a great selection of vegan and vegetarian options. Service is fast and it's nice to sit there and watch the people and boats go by.

Great latte to go if you want to enjoy the weather. It’s nice to walk along the water and look at the boats. (There’s a free ferry to the other side that the locals use for everyday transport. Many of them seem to buy their light breakfast here.)
Very convenient to jump off the ferry and grab a snack.

The food was fresh and tasty, with a low price. Great for lunch! However, even though the staff was friendly it was a bit chaotic. The setting is not very cosy and the place gets quite packed from 12.00 and on

We ordered food from uber eat, and the moussaka DIDN'T specify that comes with meat. We checked their menu in their website and it doesn't say it comes with meat.
We checked Foodora and Wolt, all of them doesn't say that it has meat.
Honestly.... if you have meat in a dish you MUST add it as a ingredient. It's a MUST.

Ordered food via uber eats. After more than 1 hour waiting I called the restaurant and they said the food was ready but the driver was not available so it would take another 45 minutes. Super annoying that they are on uber eats when they are not equipped to make deliveries. And gross that they will let food sit around for an hour before serving/delivering it to customers!

The staff is really easy going but the food is a bit flat and doesn’t bring back memories from Greece let’s say, but it’s cheap so can’t blame them. The perfect equaliser if you drank way to much the day before, horrible for a first date. Average the two, 3/5. Decent space outside during summer. Kitsch decor. Στην υγειά σας!

If you have allergies stay away from this place. They have signs encouraging people to ask what is in the food if you have allergies but they don’t know what is in their food and they don’t even look at what spicemixes contain. Despite having returned the food after a first mixup from their end I still ended up getting an allergic reaction from eating their food. Never going back there again.

We visited Kanaans Trädgårdscafé and had the Caesar salad, pulled pork tacos, coffee, and carrot cake. Everything tasted really good, and the cozy garden setting made it even better. It’s a lovely spot to enjoy a relaxed meal or fika surrounded by nature.

Great place around the beach to have a good time with friends and family.. The area offers great hiking opportunities overlooking the majestic lake Malaren.. Beautiful Hikes above the rock is a great experience altogether.. The restaurant Cafe has some basic sandwiches and snack options with some cold juices Beer made available.. Outdoors they usually have music bands play in the Summer..

Very nice and cozy place :)
The pesto pasta was served cold, but we asked to warm it up and they easily fixed it ;)

This is an awesome place for a coffee and a snack. We had some sweets, coffee, tuna sandwiches and some more stuff, it was awesome food.

But hey, the team running the bar and the cashier, they literally let the money wait outside the door, and the guest need to endure the cold air draft from open door. To the owner, get your bar/cashier staff fixed, for real, waiting outside with empty tables inside, that's just really annoying, and you loose that business. Your food and ambiente is awesome, this part of the service however: not so much.

Lovely cafe / restaurant in the heartof nature. Rustic charm. We visited for lunch.

🔸️Prime rib stew with couscous, chilli oil, topped with pumpkin seeds and zesty garlic parsley sauce.
(Högrevsgryta serveras med couscous, chiliolja, rostade pumpakärnor och gremolata)
👍👍👍👍
A warm, hearty meat dish that paired well with the lemony couscous. Medium sized dish, satisfying but does not make you too full.

🔸️Saffron flavoured fish stew
(Fiskgryta med saffran, musslor och aioli.)
👍
Didn't like this soup. It was cold and bland. The fish was hard. Not recommended.

🔸️Brewed coffee / Cappuccino
👍👍👍
Dark roast but not bitter. Nice  after meal drink.

🔸️Cakes
We tried a couple of cakes. Only the carrot cake was ok. The others were not enjoyable or too sweet.

We came on a Saturday late morning. The place was already quite full with a queue from the door to the counter. Service was quite fast. When we left about 12pm, the queue was twice as long.

Lots of seating outdoors. Limited seating indoors. Self-service. Only 1 toilet.

Would visit again but only for coffee.

Nice and friendly stuff, sweets and fika food was good but sandwiches are nonsense expensive. 155sek for a tiny toast with a joke salad is actually crazy. Didn’t even feel like a starter. But hey it’s “sourdough” bread ✨
Been 10 years in Sweden so not a tourist feedback.

Amazing authentic family café, delightful and warm service for us and our dog 🐕  inside very cozy rooms and atmosphere,  outside a lot of tables and space for everyone. Beautiful served food, great taste and good portions. A lot of chose for fika and cakes, all freshly made.
5 stars from us and 5 pawgs 🐾 from our dog 🐕
We will come back! ❤️
Thank you!
Really nice cafe with good selection of toasts and desserts. The location anf view also great. The only issue is the customer toilet.

waitress gave us pie touching the spoons and the food with her fingers ...there is no espresso coffee, looks like we travelled 70 years back to history.
I wouldn't come here anymore.  It's very sad and hard for me when I have to write bad about some place but this guys need strong improvement

Wandering what to do out of Stockholm ? Get to Lidingö :) small island just 15 to 20mn away from Stockholm city center, Lidingö will seduce you with its little beaches, forest paths, art space with Miles Garden museum but also sweet little places like Kaptenstugan :)
Near Lidingö's beautiful church and an overview on a small harbour, Kaptensstugan is the sweetest place to experiment swedish way of life ! Super welcoming team (and friendly customers too), swedish dishes and pastries, good coffee and cute decoration...everything is made to let you have a good time.

This place is more touristy than suitable for everyday dining, in my view. The menu is simple and limited, and the prices are fairly high. We also found the place to be too quiet and boring during our dinner, so we had to go to a nearby place for some more drinks.

Wonderful restaurant—great location with plentiful indoor and outdoor seating options. Waitstaff was great, but the host and his exceptionally sweet four-legged companion stole the show! Food was so delicious that we visited both days we were in Stockholm!

A really nice spot on the water, not too far from the Palace. They handled our large crowd with ease and warmth. The food was ranged from creative to classic. I went creative with my cocktail, ordering the Midsummer’s Night Dream, made with Koskenkorva Rhubarb, lemon, and elderflower foam. Delicious. My entree was the classic Swedish meatballs and it was perfect. Some of our group were Lfrom Stockholm and were most impressed, so consider this a recommendation from a visitor and some natives!

What a pleasant surprise. This lovely spot right on the water turned out to have excellent service and first rate food. With an ideal location, we did not expect the extra efforts that Karamell went to. The steak tartare and shrimp appetizers were both excellent starters. A great place for a late lunch or early dinner.
Amazing high quality food and excellent service. I ate mid afternoon and was delighted by how prompt the service was. Has a fab view of waterfront.

I really enjoyed their baked Salmon. It was very fresh. The portion size was also good. They have both indoor and outdoor dining space. I visited in afternoon and it was not very rushy.
I think it is a great place to relax and refresh after visiting the Palace and garden.

My review will be about the cafe near the palace. I really liked the food, everything is fresh, the coffee is delicious, the staff is nice and fast. I highly recommend it. The prices are shown in the photo. For two of us it was 600 kron/about 60€, 2 main courses, 2 cappuccinos and 1 dessert.

Wasn’t expecting much, just wanted something that was close (after the palace and walking around the gardens) but it ended up being surprisingly good! We both got the AVOCADO SMASH- thinking it was going to be small. Pretty good size portion and very tasty!

It was quite expensive, but it was delicious. Sadly, we had three wasps attacking us and the people around us, people panicking in Spanish and Italian, my neck oddly stiff, and the staff saw them and just considered it normal. They would go for people's meat, and follow the staff as they carried the carts with the empty trays, and I can only assume it was not a random occurrence. I should have suspected when I went to order and one "bee" passed by and the worker chuckled. I loved the food, but I had to run to eat it and get away before it got worse.
Good restaurant. Nothing to blow your mind off. Great option to eat before/after visiting the palace.

A super cozy restaurant in a rustic elegant style. The staff is very nice. The restaurant served super delicious food. I tried both chicken and vegetarian options which I can recommend. The restaurant has a nice patio if you prefer to sit outside. The walls are decorated with various nature-inspired paintings which you can also buy. Easy to get into the restaurant which makes it perfect if you want to get in with a wheelchair or stroller. Warmest recommendations for this restaurant.
Delicious cake in a beautiful building with a nice garden and area around.

Went here for SOS (sill ost smör), a traditional dish. The staff were utterly delightful and were only too pleased to help us. The food was very fresh and tasty. The cheerfulness of this place might make it seem frivolous compared to its nextdoor neighbour, Skogskyrkogården, but it's an oasis. They do live music under normal circumstances, evening meals, and you rent the place for private parties.
Love this place. Amazing food, good beer, lovely staff. They even let me pet their two amazing dogs!
Very friendly staff and lovely cozy place to eat good Swedish food. We will come back :)

10/10 – A Must-Visit in Stockholm!

If I could give 10 stars, I absolutely would—for the food, service, atmosphere, and everything in between. This is hands down one of the best restaurants I’ve ever been to in Europe!

I live in NYC and travel often throughout Europe for pleasure, always on the lookout for authentic, local gems—and this place blew me away. I had some of the best Swedish meatballs ever, paired with a delicious glass of wine, all served with exceptionally friendly and attentive service.

The atmosphere is warm, welcoming, and full of character. It’s the kind of place that makes you feel like you’ve truly experienced Stockholm.

Highly recommend to anyone visiting the city—or lucky enough to live there. I only hope they consider opening a location in New York one day!
Highly recommended. Don’t miss out on the amazing starters. Main courses excellent as well although one of the fish dishes were slightly overcooked. Nonetheless, I’ll be back.
Nice and cozy bistro with friendly stuff and perfect city and retro ambient,live it,the vine celer is big and full of nice names☺️

This place was really great. We got there exactly when it opened at 4:30pm and didn't have any reservation. They said they would be able to seat us but we only had to 6 for others with reservations. We ate comfortably without rushing and was out with 10 mins to spare. This is the restaurant I believed bag had the best meatballs from all the meatballs we've eaten. Our waiter was also really the best next to Daniel from Invece.

So on to the food. Their hash brown and chanterelles toast was really good. The hash brown was really nice and crispy and amazing. Also got the 1/2 Swedish lobster which was small but fresh. I got the cod with the potato croquettes and it was soft and moist and the croquettes were delicious as well. The cheese was salty but it went well with it. The meatballs and lingonberry were so flavorful and moist and I just loved it. Next it the wild boat sausage which i heard was amazingly flavorful. Next is the beef tartar but the portion eat huge which ended up becoming too much got the person eating it. Next is the wild boat sausage I was told was juicy and tasted good.

Overall we all ate great food and enjoyed ourselves without feeling rushed even tho they said we had a bit of a time limit. Our waiter was really friendly and nice and explained whenever we had questions

The portions are jokingly small. I paid 445 SEK for a main course and got just three tiny potatoes with the meat. Regardless of how good the food tasted, it’s not a great experience to leave a restaurant still feeling hungry.
I was super hungry after a long flight today. The owner was really kind and the food was perfect. Thank you very much!! I will visit again next time :)
We ordered sushi set 30 pieces today. It's  delicious as always. Staff are nice and food atmosphere. One of my favorite sushi places in Stockholm. ❤
Great Sushi 🍣, both the rice and the fish are perfect👍, the  restaurant owners are friendly and generous 🍻. Worth to try. I will absolutely always come back!

A very cozy place to have some Japanese food. Extremely tidy environment, super nice staffs. Outstanding food.
Highly recommend 👍

we went there twice: first time we ate there and the food was delicious, freshly made and perfect taste. as we are both vegetarian we had the vegetarian sushi plate with 3 sorts of sushi and the vegetarian wok with vegetable and tofu. and because it was so good we even accepted the pot of black tea they served us instead of the green tea.

as we stay at the motel L next to the keyaki and there are not much restaurants in the area with late opening hours, we went back there two days later to get the same menues for take out and eat at our room. and unfortunately this time it was absolutely disappointing. the vegetarian wok came without tofu, less vegetable and a less tastier sauce than the first time and the sushi plate with only two sorts instead of three. as we only figured out when we opened the boxes at our room, we didn't go back there to complain.

as the staff was very friendly and we ate really good on our first visit we would still recommend it to other hotel guests of the motel L.

Good Korean food. Well-spiced, fresh food and good sized portions. Fast service.

You order from a computer on the wall.

Many veggie options.

undeniably tasty food, but pretty expensive. good portion size but most of it is rice, i also wish it came with way more sauce. the kimchi is SO delicious and well spiced and the ideal balance between crunchy and sloppy! love the variety of vegetables.

feels a bit impersonal to order from a screen, it’s also close to the door and feels awkward and confusing. also there’s NO TOILET? HUH?

the guy working there was really nice though and guided us through the dishes!

This place serves several bowls and I tried one of them. I wouldn't say that it is best in the neighborhood but it is acceptable also the soup is included in the meal which is nice.
Apart from the food the service and design is comparable to some of their high quality competitors.

It was my first time to visit Keemchi. It is one of the good spots for Korean food in Stockholm. We can sit in both indoor and outdoor zones. I ordered one set of Bibimbap beef and one set of Ssam beef. Both of them are tasty, but I prefer Bibimbap because it is easier to eat. The price is reasonable with the portion. The restaurant is quite small inside but you have a lot of space outside. The staff was nice.  I waited for my food for just 10 minutes.
I ordered japchae for delivery. The foods was good but size portion small, few pieces of chicken and it came almost cold.

🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
Best gelato spot EVER 🍦🔥

Kenny’s Gelato in Sickla is literally the place to be. The vibes are super chill, and the gelato is unreal. I tried the pistachio and Kinder egg combo — 10/10. So creamy and full of flavor. 😍

They’ve got a bunch of cool flavors that change all the time, and there are vegan ones too which is awesome. The outdoor area has games and places to sit with your friends, plus it's right by the cinema so it’s perfect for a movie night snack.

It gets a bit crowded on sunny days but honestly, it’s worth the wait. Staff were friendly and fast even when it was busy. Kinda pricy, but you get what you pay for — this isn’t your basic ice cream. 🍨✨

Definitely coming back with my friends again. Love this place 💯

Pricey considering the small portion they served us and compared to the other gelato place with similar price points.

The pistachio was really good, but the dark chocolate was more like a milk chocolate.
Convenient and strategic location, hence perhaps it justifies the price.
Delicious gelato. Friendly service. Fun games can be played in the front area. Plenty of seating.

Overall an ok experience. Kennys gelato offers tasty and pricy icecream. Nice outdoor seating area, perfect on a sunny day.
Popular place next to shopping mall and street food restaurants. Straff was a bit disorganized. Free water kettle was empty. Some tables needed cleaning. Recommended for anyone visiting Sickla shopping centre.

Very tasty gelato, the saffron was incredible! They also have some nice games on the patio (cornhole, ping-pong, etc).

Heja Gnaget!

Kina Slottskafé in Stockholm is a nice little spot for a quick break. Great for grabbing an ice cream or some water, with both indoor and outdoor seating. The atmosphere is calm and pretty, but don’t expect a big menu, there aren’t many options.

A really nice cafe, open during summer only. Really good coffee and delicious treats - though can be crowded especially in peak season. The atmosphere and architechture is amazing and lovely.

A nice place to rest for a while while sightseeing the castle, the park and the China pavillion.
Nice deserts, good coffee, large park nearby.
Limited selection. One of our party bought an ice cream for 29SEK and 5 minutes later was  followed out and asked repeatedly if they'd paid for it. Stick to the cafe near the main palace.

Although it said Kina meaning Chinese, there was nothing Chinese in there. I was expecting fried rice or something Asian.

It was all Swedish food

Music is kind of loud. Food portions are pricy for their sizes. Don't be fooled by the low prices. For what you get it's fairly expensive. Staff is very nice and friendly. If the food was at least tasty I would give it a 3* but as it stands 2* is kind of generous.

If you are in sundbyberg there are lots of other good places.

This was already the worst place when it was called "phils burger" and now as "kitchin" they still dont care about the restaurant area of the hotel.

Such a shame as the location is great

Saturday = BRUNCH
Awesome. Very tasty
Great atmosphere and Super staff
A +++++
Excellent food, great service and kind staff. Had a very pleasant dinner with family. Will be back 100%

Love the vibe here. Not sure if I’ll be back for the sushi. I judge every place by the Spicy Tuna sushi 🍣 Tuna was pretty thing and the cucumber 🥒 inside was way too big. Eating it just made it not soo nice unfortunately. Spice is good. But not sure 🤔 about the rest of the food here. But I love the vibe of the place.

Coming from Hawaii. My standards can be high. But there are a few places here in Stockholm that have met those standards.

Best service we had in Stockholm by far! The girls were really nice, always with a smile on their faces, it was really pleasant being served by such people! The coffee/food/sweets were all good, the location is perfect.
Nice lemonade, and sandwich. Only the Dubai cake tasted too healthy it did not really have a taste.
Enjoyed the espresso, hot chocolate and Dubai chocolate cake during this visit. Cozy interior with nice lighting and decors, except it was hard to find available spots.

I have been to Kladdkakan probably a dozen times in the last decade and they are consistently good. Probably the best chicken Caesar salad in Sweden. I always ask for light dressing. It’s pretty expensive but you get lots of chicken and it is in the heart of Gamla Stan.
A small but very nice cafe in the heart of Stockholm! The food is delicious and the staff is super friendly! Not that expensive as well if compared to other places in the city…

The cafe has a great location with a nice view.  The staff is polite and helpful. I was here not for ice cream and not for cocktails, as you can see in the photos, and I can say food is okay, so if you are hungry, and want to have a snack or some cocktail 🍸 with a view, that's a great place.

We had a nice breakfast in the sun here while waiting for the 89 ferry. Good batch brew, delicious toastie with pesto. Cakes were okay The waiter was extremely nice. Very good vibec, pretty flowers everywhere. No bathroom.

Nice spot for a light meal or just a glass of beer or wine. Lovely view of Stockholm city hall and lake Mälaren. The island Södermalm just across the water. We had tomato soup that was very good. There is a public restroom nearby, but I can’t recommend it. Use only in desperate need.
Delicious Irish coffee, wonderful service, magnificent view, what more to ask for?

I arrived here on a sunny afternoon and thought it would be a lovely place to grab a bite to eat.  As I arrived in the line-up a drunken man from a wedding party began hovering over me extremely close.  I moved to the side but was unable to see the posted menu.  When I got to the window the drunk man shoved in front of me.  I said to the man working that I was there first and asked for a water to start.  He said he wasn't going to serve me if I wasn't ordering anything.  I said that I'd like to but I couldn't see the menu.  He rudely said he would serve the drunk man first.  I told him calmly that he was being rude; that the drunk man was blocking the menu.  I asked if he was really going to entertaining such mysogynism.  He said he wasn't going to serve me for calling him rude and challenged me to post a Google review.  Here it is, picture and all.
It's fun to see how it's growing and to look inside the little cozy cottage!
Really good experience, highly recommended!

Best cafe in Stockholm. Prices, food and staff, everything is on high level. Very good filtered coffee (one free refill :D). I strongly recommend homemade focaccia sandwich and anything made with cinnamon for desert. Food is homemade and very delicious. Best regards from Croatia!!

Excellent bakery.
Tried the coffee semla, and kardemummabullar...both Devine!!
Coffee was top notch too.
Atmosphere was cozy and warm, and plenty of space to relax. 60s scandic furniture was a real nice touch inside.

Lovely bakery with tasty buns! It seems quite popular so I was afraid that there would be lots of people, si I went at the opening and there was nobody. It was quiet and warm, and the staff was lovely. Highly recommended!

Best place to start your day in Stockholm. Close to central station just a 7 minutes walk but away from the stressful atmosphere of the railway station. Ideal for breakfast and lunch with tasteful sandwiches and homemade soups. Kids friendly with amazing buns and croissants. Ideal place for coffee lovers.
I've gotten pastries, bread, and sandwiches here multiple times and I've never been disappointed. The staff is always very polite and kind. Highly recommend!

Wonderful konditori where everything is handmade with love! Great pastries, sweets, good coffee, and a lot more affordable than some of the other bakeries nearby. They also had fresh salads and wraps. Staff were lovely. Definitely stop by!

The coffee and the bun was just okej, nothing special. Maybe a little touristy, so i wouldn't go here again. Casue i think there is alot better cafés. But the staff was nice.

I wish you could take your own coffe and milk.

* I changed my starts from 3 to 5 cause after a couple of visits I realized it was not fair to review the place as a cafe rather a bakery. We love the daily fresh bread from this place.

In walking distance from T-Central, there is this little cozy bakery with home made bread and cookies.
More suitable for a take away as only limited seating is available inside.
We had breakfast, simple but affordable. The cheese and hummus sandwiches were tasty.
Really cozy small bakery/ cafe with good coffee and absolutely stunning pastries

Great espresso and coffee. Delicious cakes and extraordinary customer service. A little gem where everything is homemade and the small details is making the difference. Recommend warmly to visit them.

One of my favorite places to go on weekends. Lovely food and coffee, stunning views, and very friendly people who truly care about the bakery. The day’s special pie and the meatballs are a must-try. The desserts are always fresh and delicious 💛

Lovely place to visit on warmer days. The best seatings are those facing to the waters, but the garden is also quite nice.

Many kinds of fika and simple food to choose from. Generallt ok. The päron biskvi was not good though.

This was such a wonderful experience! The food was great and the service was so nice too. Staff was super friendly! The cafe is beautiful and really gives off traditional Swedish atmosphere. I recommend their meatballs! Also, love the table with free coffee and tea. That was such a nice touch and I was so impressed.

Very cosy place and nice choices of cakes, pastries and sandwiches. It gives vibes of old house converted into a cafe. The staff were super excellent and have us a warm welcome.
I tried cinnamon bun which was a little dry and Dubai cheese cake which was the best and worth trying. 🍰

Very cute and lovely konditori, serving a handful variety of decadent cakes and husmanskost. If you could secure a spot on its balcony, you’ll have a lovely fika with amazing view overlooking the water.

The place could be a bit busy on a sunny day with a long queue and the number of toilets is limited—would’ve been nicer if they have more toilets. All in all still a lovely place.

googleReviews.authorName: Mariel Ann Bontigao Mariel Ann Bontigao
googleReviews.text: A big respect to the restaurant especially to all the staffs you guys are doing your job so well. I love the food, drinks and the ambiance of the restaurant. You have a delicious foods and your drinks has amazing design and also I really love the taste. The environment of the restaurant is very clean and you have a comfortable bathroom downstairs and I love it also because you have music since I’m a music lover, you know it helps me to be more relax while eating.. As an Asian tourist I can compare to the other restaurant that your restaurant is amazing, welcoming and very accommodating. I hope that I can come back to bring my friends and family soon to witness everything. Thank you Nō53❤️
Really like it! Great food and service. Currently a bit loud when you sit outside. Somewhat pricey at lunch hour, paid 220 for halloumi burger with alcohol free beer. Will come back for sure!
Lovely spot in Stockholm's old town for pasta and other Italian-style food. The arancini balls are fantastic, and they have a great cocktail menu so make sure you order one.

It was ok tasting food. Nothing special. The waiters were super nice and sweet. We sat outside which was beautiful on the warm day but the tastes were overwhelming rich. And the mozzarella/burrata was actually frozen in the middle. The langos were ok but it’s toast skagen not langos which was a disappointment.

Had booked a month in advance and a week before we were told there was a set menu for New Year’s Eve. Without any options for allergies. We were allowed to cancel our reservation, but a week out from New Year’s Eve there’s not a lot of options.

The menu at the restaurant was also not the same we got shown a week before.

We didn’t get what we ordered food wise nor drink wise, with the paired drink package we didn’t get the last drink, which they at least removed from the bill. Drinks came long after food was served.

Sitting by the kitchen window we saw a lot of food get sent back and a lot of food sitting finished waiting for the previous dish to be done so it can be served. Also a few people got up and left after seeing the menu.

Had to chase after waiters for anything and everything. We got served our food then didn’t hear another word until the next dish.

Superb little cafe located right by the water within easy walking distance of the T bahn station. Had a very good coffee and cinnamon bun. We went there specifically for the cafe and was very pleased to have done so

We waited 45 minutes for one cup of coffee and korv med brod for a kid!!! First they started to serve people who came later then us, then forgot our order! And the cafe wasn’t even full or overcrowded.

★☆☆☆☆ Hostile service and completely illogical rules.

I visited this cafe yesterday with a stroller and had no issues. However, I returned today (a Monday at lunchtime) with my 2-year-old, and the experience was shocking. Despite the cafe being almost entirely empty—there was literally one other customer and zero strollers—the lady staff member felt the need to lecture me. She told me I could keep the stroller inside "for now," but if other strollers arrived, I would have to take mine out.
