The food really shines — the fried chicken bowl is absolutely delicious, and the chicken in red coconut curry sauce is outstanding, rich, and full of flavor. The bao buns also looked amazing if you enjoy duck and salmon. Everything tasted fresh and thoughtfully prepared. Super reasonably priced too.

Definitely a cute, memorable find if you’re visiting the city and want something comforting yet special. Highly recommend stopping by.

Wonderful place in a cool location "on the steps". Inside dining is well laid out, but was a bit warm for us so we ate outside on the steps. Our server, soft spoken fellow, was welcoming, bilingual, and just pleasant to chat with.
The food, well! Incredible. Fresh, tasty and huge portions. Every dish was that way.

We went here for lunch after our arrival and it was amazing, the food was so fresh and tasty. Also great service the waiter was very nice. Absolutely loved the bowls and would totally recommend it if you like fresh asian food.
A little gem close to the most iconic viewpoint of Stockholm. Great food friendly service. We are coming back for more.

Delicious Asian lunch dishes, I usually eat noodles with mushrooms (which are a bit spicy- heads up) and curry chicken with rice. The staff is kind and fast. I came here several times for lunch during work days and I will go again, so I totally recommend it.

It was a very hot day in Stockholm, I enjoyed the frozen smoothie. The desert was delicious but the muesli toppings were dry and really hard to eat. I'd also suggest putting better hints on how much sugar each bowl contains.
In general a nice place for a pit stop!

Really good café with a wide variety of healthy, energy packed food.
The café is spacious, with cozy decor and a warm, welcoming atmosphere, and the option of outdoor seating, which is great on a nice day.

Their smoothies are excellent fresh, flavorful, and perfect for a boost of energy.

A nice touch is that they offer free tampons, which shows attention to guests needs.

The only downside is that it’s a bit pricey, but overall, I really like this place. It’s a great option if you want to stay energized throughout the day without feeling too bloated.
Tried a lot of stuff since we were in a big company. Everything tasted great. Was really impressed with the blueberry "oatmeal" dish, didnt know you could make it that interesting, perfected it imo.
Very tasty vegan food, generous portions and very fair prices. Lots of options for savoury food, we had a hard time deciding - so great to find a place like this. Will be back again soon!

The food was good, service wasn’t the best
Not sure why the order taker and her colleague had to leave the queue and come to check on me if I got a coffee that I didn’t pay for!! Al thought it was paid but I was waiting for the food to arrive to pour my coffee. We also received a wrong order.
Really disappointing!
In the end the cashier has done her best to satisfy us but I guess it was already late.

I’m so glad I found this vegan gem! There are a lot of breakfast, lunch and dessert/snack options, and they all look delicious. I got the strawberry-compote oats with peanut butter and banana, and they were really nice.
The place is very cosy and spacious, really nice to sit in and relax for a bit.

Was looking for vegan places, we were travelers from North Carolina 🇺🇸 and discovered this adorable vegan spot—so may amazing vegan options are available at this place and everything we tried pancake, tofu scramble, protein bowl and noodles and everything was absolutely delicious. The dessert was to die for… totally in love. Highly recommend!
Absolutely loved this place. Had the pancakes and the scrambled tofu on toast and they were both so good. Can't wait to come back to sweden and will definitely be coming back here

The pancakes and baked oats were very good, and the scrambled tofu was excellent! Delicious coffee as well. The terrace was very nice, cozy and quiet and had both shady and sunny areas with plenty of seating for weekend brunchers. Inside also looked quite nice and spacious.

A cafe with a nice atmosphere, simply decorated & plenty of space both indoors & outdoors.
If you are vegan then a visit here is a must - you’ll find a wide range of plant-based dishes for breakfast, brunch or lunch/dinner. It’s a popular place & often crowded.

The menu has a large content, varied & the dishes are served in generous portions. The food is filling, tasty & reasonably priced.
Helpful staff & attentive. Super nice at every visit.

I like their variety of Asian food and that's why I took quite a long time to make a decision. I ordered Wonton Soup, Tenderloin Sichuan Sauce (served with rice) and Chicken Teriyaki (served with rice). All of them are delicious, especially Tenderloin Sichuan Sauce which comes with a big portion. It is simple food that gives you the homemade taste like you eat at home. I came alone and I could take two bowls of rice in the sets so I asked them to take it home. The staff are nice and the atmosphere is cozy. The price is reasonable!

Unprofessional unclean,Be careful here.
The lady was coughing and server had her fingers inside the bottles she was carrying the food the kimchi was spoiled test was garbage the food wasn’t that good the other issue we had  I ordered plate of 30 I only received 27 when I told her she started counting very unprofessional she ignored me and didn’t even count right I received as well 10  vegetarian sushi rolls I didn’t order they were spouse to be 10 makrill fish I paid over 600 kr and I deserve better food not partially made and better customer service so here it’s obvious they don’t care about that as well unclean it’s actually first and last time I always for some reasons I see the Asian customer service and cuisine overseas really bad their mentality is like this I don’t recommend this place especially I had people with me that was so embarrassing.
Nice food but it was very little protein and mostly carbs but it was teasty , people who work there look really tired

Waited for 30 minutes for the waiter to finally take my order. Felt ignored for all that half an hour, even people who came after me got their order taken faster. Unpleasant lady working there. Food came very fast though. The food was good, but too salty.
The best restaurant in city/stockholm very delicious food every day waiters is kind and Sociable the best Asian food ever

name: MamaWolf

googleReviews.text:
This was a good tasting bowl of noodles that was very satisfying with the thick udon noodles paired with a tasty curry soup. The soup is thick with a rich taste of sessame. Rucola blended well with curry taste.
Chicken karaage is pretty damn great, this is crispy, juicy, and intensely flavorful.

Came here and the place was full of people which is a good sign. Had to join the waitlist but it was worth it definitely! The atmosphere is lovely, the staff are very friendly and the food is the star of the show. ⭐️ Portion sizes are perfect and so worth the price, the taste really brought warmth and pleasure to the tastebuds. One of my new favorite places to go for some classic Japanese food!

Japanese street food yes!
Delicious noodles and warm atmosphere!
A bit crowd at some hours but fast service!

🌱 Vegan options as well!

the roast beef don: tasted bland. do not order this dish.

the curry udon with pork: very yummy.

fries: greasy and not crispy enough. we ordered yuzu mayo but we barely got any sauce. not any expectional seasoning going on (maybe not enough seasoning?) just average fries.

tofu rice bowl: yummy!

beef onigiri: super nice flavor but the rice fell apart easily.

spicy-ish chicken kaarage: nice flavor! however we’re kinda dissapointed we only got 3 small pieces, which is not alot value for the money.

One of the best Japanese restaurant in Stockholm. I’ve been there for several, and always take the tantan noodles which is probably the best in Stockholm (with Sin Ramen).
They have several vegan options which is great too.
The place is quite small and often full, which is maybe the only negative part. But not sure what they can do to solve this anyway…

googleEditorialSummary: Trendy Japanese restaurant with a terrace offering locally sourced dishes, sake & a take-out option.

googleReviews.text:
Don’t ever get their Kyckling Sesame. Poor in quality. They use really old, not fresh meat. Plus there was more deep-fried flour than meat. Refer to photo.

They used to be so good. I’m a frequent customer but probably not anymore. Bring back the quality and I will change my review.

The food is great! I highly recommend the dish Mapo Tofu .. really delicious .. friendly staff feels like welcoming home

The price is good, not expensive.. if you live in Stockholm , this is average price ..

I have been there at lunch time. They have buffet option. 99 kr.
Varieof sushi, Chinese food👍🏻
The most good paer is that they always feel up the plates.
Good service and wide variety of dishes. Lunch buffet 👍

Best bubble tea in Stockholm! I come in for a bubble tea every time I'm in the area. The owners are the sweetest, and I like their bubble tea much more than any of the chains' in Stockholm. I'm so happy this place exists!!!

Good food and beer but Very slow. We order when the sky is still bright and the food comes when the outside is dark. Other people order pasta later than us get food much earlier and we do not know why. waiting for a pasta is 1 hour.
The restaurant give priority to online customers and refuse to serve pizza for clients at the table, which is ridiculous. Waited 1,5 hours for the main course and got the wrong food.
The most authentic pizza in NDS. After trying the many pizza restaurants in the area, I can comfortably say that Mare Blu's pizza is as authentic as it can get.
Great pizza, great pasta - Italy level good, super lovely hosts, nothing more to ask.
Very nice personnel and atmosphere on top of wonderful Italian food! 5/5 will recommend and come back

Mariedals Trädgårdscafé is a lovely place with a great ambiance and friendly staff.
We ordered the köttbullar, Caesar salad, and lemonade, and everything was very tasty! There is plenty of space to sit outside, relax, and enjoy the sun, making it a perfect spot for a sunny day.
Overall, it was a great experience, and we would definitely come back again!
Comfy outdoor seating, fun atmosphere with kids and dogs. Lilacs and flowers, really add nicely. Food is good. Great place to soak in an afternoon.

Pretty fantastic place! A hidden gem. Food is at a different level. Incredibly delicious and original recipes with great wine selection. Service is also top notch. Great vibes surrounded with flowers, water and good music. Strongly recommended both for casual visits and events.

We had our wedding celebration party here. Professionalism of Lars and his team made everything perfect. Fantastic food, great atmosphere, smooth service. Strongly recommended for all sorts of events.
Very nice place to stop during a walk to drink something and rest while enjoy the sun. Very nice service and accommodations. The attendants are kind. Toilet is available and clean.

A good restaurant for Indian food in stockholm.  Nice hospitality, very friendly. If you want Indian spicy tell during ordering your food. Food was tasty. Prices are bit higher range compared to some other good Indian restaurants around the Stockholm city. But you will not regret.

Quite a nice place with pleasant and fast service. The curry (butter chicken) was good, but the naan was too dry and crispy. Some staples of Indian food like pilau rice and paratha were unavailable. Not a bad experience, though.

We came here for for dinner because it was recommended by some friends . We started with some whiskey and wine with Mixed Pakoda . For main course, we had had Palak Paneer and Mixed Veg Curry with Naans. The food was quite good. The service was very good. The ambience was very ordinary.

Great authentic Indian restaurant.  Great and smiling service,  complimentary water,  great beer ( the local one -  as a Czech i am a little biased against Bernard so I went for the local IPA). Dishes come with rice included ( even those that do not mention it on the menu). Overall Great experience.
Smallish portions so order a Naan. The food was good for the price. However, the food was lacking spice but still quite nice. Service was fast and staff were polite. I would go again.

name: Matbaren

googleReviews.text: Lovely and lively lunch, we had the set menu with wine pairing. Great food, and some very interesting wine choices. Will definitely come again next time we're in Stockholm.

Was visiting Stockholm for the first time for 4 nights and ate here twice! The people working were extremely kind and attentive. We enjoyed every dish we ordered - the pork bun is insane! happy with our experience thank you :)

Dined here for an early dinner in October (early flight the following day).  Beautiful restaurant with impeccably professional staff.  In fine dining, service can teeter on either side of what I would characterize as stiff/formal (like a crisp, starched shirt) or natural/relaxed (like cashmere or merino).  Matbaren’s dinner service is the former.  Unfortunately, this rigidity lead to some awkwardness on timing (we explained at the top of the evening that one of us would likely have three courses and the other only two.  this lead to appetizers and mains being misaligned and a long wait for one of us between courses, as the team seemed unable or unwilling to pivot).  The “pickles” dish left me underwhelmed and a little flummoxed.  It consisted of pickled vegetables, but the only taste was vinegar.  I expected seasoning – caraway, chili, dill, coriander…something else to make it interesting.  It wasn’t bad, just bland.  The cod was beautifully cooked – just barely set.  The Bolivian chocolate, clearly a popular dessert, did not disappoint.  Overall solid, but if I were to visit again, it would likely be for the lunch service.  Perhaps the vibe is a little more relaxed.

This place was recommended for me to go to during my trip to Stockholm. I was also excited to see that it was a Bib Gourmand restaurant… but unfortunately this place did not live up to the expectations.
I reserved a seating as a solo diner. When I arrived I was seated at the bar, but it was a part of the bar where it was basically just me. There was one couple on one side of me, separated by an empty seat and on the other side there were four empty seats. Which would have been fine, except the service was terrible. It took at least 10 minutes before the bartender even acknowledged my existence and talked to me about the menu (that’s on this wooden board). She mentioned an additional wine menu and I asked for that. Another 15 minutes goes by before she came back. In the meantime there is another person behind the bar offering other people bread, but she stops before she gets to me and just smiles. I was confused why I wasn’t offered bread and asked when the bartender finally got back to me. She said that you only get bread after you order (mind you they put butter in front of you in the first interaction, before you order). She handed me bread after I ordered, about 25 minutes after I sat down. She also forgot to hand me the additional wine list, and I had to ask for it again. It was such a weird experience to have as a solo diner who’s sitting on the side of the bar with very few other people. Last issue with the service was that I was give a wet napkin with one of the first dishes that came out. I used it and the ripped wrapper stayed next to me the entire meal (usually that would be picked up after the dish is finished, but not with this restaurant).
Then there’s the food. It was fine, but not at the level I expected. There was only one dish, of the four that I ordered, that rose to the occasion… it was the Swedish pork skewers.
All in all, I wouldn’t recommend this place, especially if you’re a solo diner. You can get better food and service at other places in Stockholm.
Amazing food! nice setting, friendly staff and the food is an experience one must try! I am vegetarian and they were very good at replacing some of the dishes for me.  Loved it!

googleEditorialSummary: Creative, contemporary cooking in the smart restaurant of a plush hotel, plus a funky bistro.

googleReviews.text: I've come by several times when it says it's supposed to be open, only for it to be closed. Better come by no later than 18:30 if you want to eat here, I guess.
Nice location, quality food, friendly staff...little short of space but that is compensated by good service

This is not a fancy sushi place. It's a neat tiny restaurant you visit because you're hungry and you crave something truly delicious.

The bits are far from bite-sized. The generous slices of fish are fresh, juicy and tasty. The sweet-ish dressings go surprisingly well with the sushi. The rice is just right.

Your meal comes beautifully served and very reasonably priced. You can help yourself to miso soup, green tea and water. I've been to Matsu Sushi more times than I can count and loved it every single time. They are just really great at making sushi.

I will definitely go back to this restaurant!
The staff was kind and environment inside of the restaurant was clean. There is an option to sit outside during summers and miso soup available for the customers.

The dish was delicious. I specifically loved the lightly grilled salmon sushi with roasted onions on top and chili mayo on the side to dip the sushi in.

The best sushi in stockholm.
The portion is very big . The taste is outstanding.
The staff are really nice.

While walking around Stockholm, we happened upon this restaurant & bar and glad we stopped in for lunch.  Once we figured out how the place operates we ordered our meal and relaxed.  We had the Langkokt Oxkind and Kyckling Pasta and some salad.  I enjoyed the oxkind and my wife said the pasta a bit on the salty side for her, but we both enjoyed our short time here.  Their outdoor seating area was full, so we sat inside the sunroom area, which at the time had lots of open tables, but started to fill up as we sat and had our meal.  I do recommend giving it a try.
We ordered fries and juice and were talking. Because we didn’t ordered anything else suddenly the waiter came and asked us to pay the bill even though the place was almost empty
Rude behavior! IStay away from here. Strongly recommended not to sit there.

The place was not full, the waiter gave thus the check while we were waiting after we finished our drinks in a very rude way in a sign for us to leave, he could’ve said we needed to order more or at least spoke instead of just putting the Visa machine on the table with no words.

Took me 20 minutes to get the attention of the waiter. Ordered a glass of white wine. Nothing happened. Another 10 minutes later, the waiter walks by, looks at me, groans, and goes to pick up the wine that he forgot. It’s not like the place was full or anything… massive disappointment…

This restaurant is simply amazing. If you don't speak English, don't worry. They have an English menu and the waiters are so lovely to help you in case you need suggestions for your meal.

Many thanks to Johan who gave me great suggestions for a superb glass of wine, an exquisite fish dish as well as an insanely good dessert. He also helped me to combine each dish.

The fish dish I had was a true explosion of tastes, with hints of sour and slightly spicy coming together in each bite. It was so delicate and well cooked.

If you want to taste fishy flavours, that's the right place to come. If you're looking for something fine for the palate, that's also the place to be.

The restaurant itself looks super chic and can enjoy a couple of hours with a cool playlist.
Highly recommended.

Tack för allt och vi ses snart!
good dishes, the Skagen salad has too much sauce for me, but the atmosphere is good

Old review, got food poisoned from most likely the oysters here. Got reminded today as I heard someone else got listeria from the oysters here. Would recommend the staff to consider rigorous sanitation and hygene check, as well as that the cooling is properly working and oversee the handling with their supplier.
Great seafood 🦞, beefsteak tasted amazing. For 240€ for lunch, I wish a better atmosphere.
Nothing special. Dessert is superb, but not that wow.

The sushi was ready in 5 minutes. It didn't look fresh. The rice was bad. It was like they ran out of the right kind and used regular rice. The sushi was falling apart and tasted bad. I don't recommend this place. The service was terrible too.

Top 5 sushi places in Stockholm. This is a genuine Japanese establishment. Skilled sushi chefs with a lot of exciting items on the menu. Me and my wife prefer to go to this place even if there are a lot of alternatives in our neighborhood. The trip is worth the great taste.

Such a disappointment. We’ve just ordered family mix. The sushi was old or badly done. The rice was not sticky and falling apart. It was also not cooked well and still hard. If they can’t cook the rice properly, what is the point. Never order takeaway.
One of the best Sushi places in Stockholm, the fried tuna rolls and the seshami are our absolute favorite

I heard good things about this place, but unfortunately very mediocre and expensive sushi. You can get much better sushi for this price in Stockholm. They were also rude to my girlfriend when she picked it up.
10 out of 10, the best sushi i have ever eaten!

+ quality and taste
+ Atmosphere

-unfriendly boy in the counter
- Bit expensive

Cool inside and good food.
The food lacked something though, can't put the finger on it but it did not give the typical sushi vibe. Also a bit cheap on the sushi with the garnish
A bit pricey, but best sushi that I have eaten in Stockholm.
Not many sushi places on the islands. Good one of them is pretty decent
Delicious! Great food and friendly service

This was pretty nice especially because we did not expect too much... They had great pizza and they were super friendly and service minded. Some nice 80s synth music was playing.

This is NOT fine dining but it's a good place to take have a pizza and beer.

Extremely rcist bar. This is supposedly a bar, and we were just having fun, but because we were Black and POC people the owner, not other patrons, asked us to keep it quiet. We weren't drunk, just laughing and having fun. Never going to this place again.
They did a fantastic job accommodating some of our dietary needs (cow's milk free pizza). And the food was very good with great service to top off a nice night out with the family.
We call it “The best bar in the Solar System” among friends. Dalai Lama was there, there's his picture. All the best public from the area. Who doesn't understand is not from the Solar System.

name: Mikkeller Södermalm

googleReviews.text:
Every time when in Stockholm, I try to get here for something new and interesting. Like a lot that bartenders are always so friendly and ready for advice. Today had like 3 different types of beer, and each one was exactly matching the mood and wishes. Lovely!
Just bear in mind, coming along on Saturday evening - you might miss like I missed last time, the queue had its tail far in the street :)
Nice place!

Cozy bar, with a lot of room for hanging out with your friends while enjoying a pint from a vast selection of craft beer and some of the best Neapolitan-style pizza in Stockholm. Speaking of, and jumping to specific menu recommendations, I found both "svamp" (mushroom) and "chèvre" delicious, while "grönsak" (vegetable) was quite underwhelming.

I had a great time at Mikkeller Bar in Stockholm! The place is beautifully designed, with a modern and artsy vibe that makes it really stand out. The artistic details throughout the space give it a creative and relaxed atmosphere.

The beer selection is excellent, with a wide variety of high-quality craft beers to choose from. Whether you're into hoppy IPAs or something more experimental, there's something for everyone.

Definitely a great spot to enjoy good beer in a cool and inspiring environment. I'll gladly come back!

Favorite tap room in Södermalm! Friendly staff. You will find a beer to your liking. They also have non-Mikeller beer. Wine and liquor, too! Mango margaritas are 😋.  While there are only a few pizza options, everyone will find one of their spectacular pizzas options to suit them.

The location is super close the train station, only 5 minutes walk.

They have plenty of craft beers you could try and enjoy.

The atmosphere is very friendly and not loud, good for hanging out with friends or just chilling alone is fine too.

They also have pizza and non-alcoholic options.

googleEditorialSummary: Intimate bar & bistro decorated with antique tools, with a casual vibe & a menu of steaks & seafood.

googleReviews.text:
Great place. The food was tasty and of good quality. Having an italian background, it felt great to enjoy the flavours of the raw ingredients they used that you do not find in many places.
My children enjoyed it as well. The tiramisú was the real one!
The closest thing to an actual Italian pizza I’ve had in Sweden so far. Not very cheap, but 100% worth the price. An easy 5 stars.

New restaurant with potential but some kinks to sort out.
. Almost full on a Monday so had to wait for service. But as a new restaurant one can overlook that. Food was good but not special. They need to work on the details a bit .
Also is it a pizza place or a fine Italian.  They need to decide !
Great potential so will wisit again soon and hopefully bump ut the stars ! We'll see !
Waitress was super helpful. We tried lamb racks, ragu, tiramisu and carpaccio. All of them were good, I’d definitely recommend tiramisu and lamb racks
Great food, Antipasti, Primi and wine just work together. Friendly stuff and nice place

Candles were lit up during dinner time. It transforms the environment into a more modern and mysterious atmosphere. The starter(prawns with garlic source) was really delicious. It is more impressive than my main course, which is Swedish meat with mushrooms sauce. Recommend for business meetings or friends meetings with no more than 4 people)

I went to Milles and ate a beef tartar and ended up being food poisoned. I left the restaurant feeling queezy and not long after I got sicker and had to go home. The same goes for my dinerpartner. When we emailed the restaturant and told them about this they simply replied it couldnt have been there food since no one else reported food poisining from that day. That is a ridicoulus claim and I find it to be disapoiting. I will surely never come back.
I had dinner there in August 2024. Excellent location, lovely atmosphere on the terrace in summer, and the food was good.

It was a rainy Friday night and hard to find a spot for dinner when we did not book ahead. Happened on this restaurant and the food was really delicious. I had pasta with Burrata and artichokes and my husband had pasta with mussels and shrimp and clams. Both dishes were delicious. Pasta was perfectly cooked. Our appetizers were shrimp and garlic bread and he had Iberico in a croquet. The wait staff was attentive and very friendly. We were lucky to find this place for dinner.

Very rude waiter, refused to turn the heaters on outside because he got my order wrong and proceeded to treat me and my group with ultimate disrespect. Food was not nice and extremely overpriced for what it was. Pasta wasn’t cooked and sauce was too sweet. There are much better restaurants around Stockholm.

Restaurant/coffeeshop located inside the museum. We ordered the chicken salad & spaghetti bolognese. Both dishes were big & tasty.
Very nice scenery.
We order at the counter & the stuff brought our meal.
Amazing sculptures in a stunning setting. We couldn't get over how productive Carl Milles was. The displays up on the pillars against the sky made everything so majestic. Well worth a visit.
Lovely place with good coffee and deserts. Sugar free option available. Super fast service as well.
A beautiful cafe with nice lunch options and delicious sweets. Cozy places inside and outside with direct access to the museum (you don’t need to visit the museum to visit the cafe).
Nice terrace to relax after enjoying the sculpture garden. Very friendly young team.

We went to this Greek restaurant for lunch and loved it. The service was quick and friendly, and the food was just amazing.It really felt like a little taste of Greece. The dishes were simply plated but still elegant, and the whole place was very clean and inviting. It’s perfect for families, couple,groups. The music was soft and relaxing, not too loud. Definitely a spot we’ll be coming back to!

We visited Milos Restaurant today and ordered the lamb rack, lamb fillet, and souvlaki—everything was amazing! The ambience was superb, and the service was excellent. Jawad served us and was incredibly friendly, always smiling and making us feel welcome.

We had a wonderful experience at Milos! Everything from start to finish was excellent. The beef Angus was cooked perfectly — juicy, flavorful, and beautifully presented. We also tried a few other dishes, and each one was fresh, well-seasoned, and full of taste. The atmosphere was warm and elegant, with a relaxed vibe that made our evening feel special. The staff were amazing — friendly, professional, and attentive without being intrusive. They made us feel truly welcome and took great care of us throughout the meal. It’s rare to find a place that combines great food, service, and ambiance so perfectly. We’ll definitely be coming back to Milos and highly recommend it to anyone visiting!

I visited this Greek restaurant with my friends, and I must say the food was outstanding. I have worked as a cook in Germany, but I have rarely found food with such balanced and authentic flavors.
The atmosphere is very peaceful, and you always receive VIP-level service here.

As a cook, I highly recommend this restaurant. A must-try.

Best Greek food in Stockholm. During our recent visit from Miami to Stockholm we had the pleasure to visit Milo’s restaurant and everything was top notch! The food, the service & the ambience was second to none. Highly recommend it.

Food was decent when I ate there. But got same 2 meals to go which tasted atleast 3 times spicier than what they served in the restaurant. So much so that couldn't eat even when paired with Yoghurt, even when I like spicy food. They just dumped red chilli into it. Had to throw away all of it.
Not sure why they did it.
The food colouring went straight through the chicken breast that had the smell of defrosted chicken. I was quite skeptical eating there, in terms of Indian food the quality is poor.

This place is one of the absolute best Indian Restaurants in entire Stockholm metro area, it’s a true hidden gem that we stumbled over when in the area and we immediately became regulars. We’ve tried a number of the best Indian/Bangladesh restaurants in Stockholm and Miss Goa rates at least top-3. Highly recommended 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟.

This is not Indian taste .. Bangladeshi food .. Definitely not for South Indians. . Ordered briyani and received spicy/sweet  tomato rice with pomegranate inside — how can pomegranate be part of briyani rice ?????
OK. Few foods are cold inside. In mixed sizzler plater, lamb was very stiff. Prawn was good and tasty, both in sizzler as well as in separate dish.

Amazing Indian Veg food served with a smile. I turned up late for dinner and they still made a nice veg thali for me even during the closing hour rush. Very warm and welcoming people and the food very tasty. Highly recommended place

Nice place to have lunch and dinner.
Very cheerful staff. We visited Miss puri for lunch. We had both vegetarian and non vegetarian taalis. It's very reasonable price and good quality food.
I enjoyed it a lot and will definitely recommend to anyone who loves India food.

Nice Indian restaurant with a good ambiance and pleasant service. My wife loved the food while I enjoyed it (but have had better in Stockholm). The Naan was very good. Can recommend a visit if you are in the area.

Decent Indian restaurant in Stockholm. The vegetarian thali has good variety. The yogurt and raita are sweetened as seems to be popular in Scandinavian countries Indian restaurants. The mango chutney that comes with Papad is quite good. The restaurant owner was quite accommodating to our requests.

Situated on a peaceful lane beside a crowded Fridhemsplan shopping street, this place boasts of being an authentic Punjabi restaurant in Stockholm, perhaps the only one to claim such. Having been at multiple Indian restaurants before, I wasn’t expecting too much from it. But the food surprised me to the core. It was by far the most Indian food I had in Europe. I visit here with my spouse and ordered Vegetarian Punjabi Mushroom masala and Black Dal, a tandoori roti and a naan. I just say the gravy was cooked nicely, vegetables were crisp, and mushrooms and Paneer chunks were smoked nicely. Breads were nice and just how they should be. Only downside perhaps could be the vegetarian samosa which were not felt to be in-house prepared, perhaps they were bought as frozen food and fried afterwards.

Overall we were happy with the food and would like to visit here again.

Awesome sushi, specially deluxe ones.
Super delicious and perfect preparation. You never disappointed on them not even one time.
After I try this place from 1 year ago I can not eat sushi anywhere else.
Strongly recommended 👍
Thank you for your great service

We ordered the MGL deluxe special. It was fried outer rice layer.. it was very tasteful. We also ordered the salmon deluxe rolls.. the freshest sushi I have tasted.. I prefer the MGL  more.. the meso soup and the garnishes inside were very plentiful. The restaurant was very clean. Customer service was good I would rate it a 4.4
my favourite sushi place in stockholm, get the salmon and ask for it to be flambéed 10/10 every time

Awesome sushi by Koreans. A proper miso (finally), normally it doesn't taste much but this time it rated a lot. Kinda cozy place and many fun maki variations. Sushi was very good, rice perfectly balanced and tempura roll was excellent. You get a lot of food, we were very full after the meal.

Tried this mgl after eating multiple times in their shop in liljeholmen. We ate here during the lunch and was pleasantly suprised that this sushi was actually much better than the one in liljeholmen (could be that we always eat takeaway from there). Generous slices of fish, rice was not too mushy and not pressed too hard. Good variation, wouldn't mind some grilled slices though. Staff friendly, atmosphere great, a small and cozy restaurant with aquarium occupying a a large portion of the restaurant.

Little cozy place. Had a really wonderful experience. Tucked away from the crazy hustle & bustle of the main tourist area. Lady at the counter was super pleasant & helped us with our food selection. All the food is fresh and made in front of you. Sushi rice was perfect!
Thank you!
Small but wonderful Japanese restaurant. I am Hong Kong people live in UK. For around £50 (Sek 650) I have the amazing value delicious food which is up to Tokyo standard.
Really delicious and fresh sushi!! Totally recommend

Fantastic food, mix platter gives you a taste of everything they have to offer. Very friendly staff. The place itself is quite small, around three tables but I can't say enough as to how good the sushi was.
I found the place on google maps and glad I visited, miso soup and the set of rolls and nigiri was delicious, and the staff is very friendly, definitely recommend!

I have to say the atmosphere and energy of this place is something else. The waiters are very nice and the drinks they make are amazing! Whether you want a beer or a cocktail you will not be disappointed 😍 10 out of 10!!

Don't know what happened to this place, used to be a good spot. Now the atmosphere is uncomfortable and the staff are really rude and dismissive with no understanding of how to treat people. Will not return, there are many better places close by.

I just walked into this pub to grab a drink before my pre-planned party and my God,  this place was good.  It's small, cozy and filled with good vibes.  The bartender was all pumped up and all energetic and I had my all time fav guiness n one haffe and I just sat at he bar counter and enjoyed the drinks.  Nice place to chill with some beer n Irish music 🎶

A standard simple pub, popular by locals, with mostly Swedish beers on draft.
The interior nowadays needs a lot of TLC, and toiletts are not as fresh as at other bars.

Very cozy and friendly atmosphere!  Great burgers with high-quality meat! Perfect music selection.  If you are a fan of oldies, they play a golden selection every evening! There are also a couple of LCDs showing important football games occasionally but it's definitely not a sports pub. The location is also very convenient. Right by the huge church in downtown Stockholm.

Really awesome, believe me I have Indian tastebud and rarely get something that dance on tastebud. But this was really nice spicy I ordered 3 level spicy fried chicken and it is worth the price .

Highly recommended.

As per my recommendation, dine in . But just you order take away and food gets soggy then just oven it for 10 mins   at 180°

Staff is friendly

We got table immediately and food served before we finished washing our hands, it was during Saturday lunch time. Very impressive!

Chicken was great and we barely could finish our medium portions! We had tried Monchi and Seoul versions, both were perfectly flavoured.

We loved this place and this chicken is addictive!

The food was amazing but the waiting time was really long.
We had reservation but they didn’t seem to remember for that and we got a table next to the door facing with the cold weather. Also, we had to wait a lot for the waiter.
BUT
Apart from these, we had a really good time and they were so flexible in the kitchen helping to me with my insuline resistance!
So in total, I’d recommend this place but if you wanna go, definitely book a table!😋

After a month in Southeast Asia (Japan, Taiwan, Korea and Vietnam) both my husband and I are huge fans of any kind of food from this part of the world.

Hearing good things about Monster Chicken we spontaneously dropped in and were lucky to get seats for two (mid-July in Stockholm and the city is nearly empty due to summer holidays).

The atmosphere and vibe, both inside and outside, are funky, modern and cool. It’s a restaurant where you wanna “be seen”. The seating outside offers great people-spotting since the big and vibrant street of Birger Jarl runs just outside the restaurant.

Perhaps it was the high temperature (30 degrees Celsius) that made the service slow this evening. It took unreasonable time waiting for service since the amout of guests was modest. Being located in a hotspot area, one assumes the tempo is flawless.

The food was good. Nothing more. Not bad, not amazing, nothing new, but still crispy and ok taste. The half-half plate is perfect sharing (2 persons).

This is a great lunch place with a a great vibe.

Great looking restaurant with a decent vibe.

Food was good however the chicken could have been warmer. We were told we would have to wait for the rice cake - but I believe the chicken could have been served sooner...

Service needs improving a lot. Always had to get someone's attention for additional drinks and the bill.

Next time I'm in Stockholm, I'll give them another try. Chicken looked and tasted good - just an issue with the temperature.

Potential to be a great place.

name: Morfar Ginko

googleReviews.text: I hosted a Cocktail Contest in the barber shop downstairs for a birthday. It was a great location for a small group (10 people), the bartender was super helpful and we had a great time!

Nice atmosphere and cool bar with some nice drinks. The bar becomes a dance-floor/club after some time. There is some food available including 2 pizzas that can be made vegan (but are not vegan to begin with). Didn’t have food there so not sure what it’s worth, but the food seemed a bit pricey. Nicely located and staff was slightly slow but very kind.

It’s such a popular place. You could sit outside, drink or eat, it’s always so full. And have their delicious pizzas. Even the pita and hummus in starters was one of the best. It’s a  super crowded place in summers to sit outside and in winters too I remember it’s a nice drinking and dancing place

Love the decoration and atmosphere, gives an alternative feeling that is often
missing in Stockholm. Pizza tastes really good and the coctails were also well made. Would def visit again.

I did visit again and had a delicious grilled auberigine with lentils an chickpeas. Must try when there!
Pretty nice atmosphere, especially during Friday nights. Interesting concept with restaurant/bar on ground floor and dance club downstairs.

googleEditorialSummary: Lively bar & restaurant with an industrial-chic vibe & DJ sets, plus a classic Swedish menu.

name: Mosebacketerrassen

googleReviews.text:
We recently discovered Mosebacketerrassen, and the place was simply fantastic. It has a laid-back, open-air charm that really impressed us. While some views of the water are slightly obstructed, it's still a true waterfront space with a large capacity—easily seating 150 to 200 people.

The setting is casual, with relaxed bench-style seating and smaller tables and chairs scattered around. The ground is gravel and dirt, so expect it to get a bit dusty, especially on dry days—but that’s part of the atmosphere. On sunny summer afternoons, the vibe is great. We recommend arriving around 3 PM to get a spot; later than that, it tends to get very crowded.

However, prices are on the high side. A basic plate of fries cost around 125 SEK, which felt steep, and the beer was about 75 SEK for what was essentially a standard lager. The menu is quite limited—there aren’t many food options, and even the limited items come with a high price tag. So, it’s not a place for a full meal unless you're willing to pay quite a bit.

There are washrooms available, but, as expected, be ready to stand in queues—for food, drinks, restrooms, and just getting around. It gets busy, especially in good weather.

Getting There:
If you're taking the Tunnelbana, get off at Slussen. Once you exit the station, you can either take a staircase up (about 5 minutes of uphill walking) or opt for the newly built elevator (Hissen) that takes you up more comfortably. From there, it’s a short, beautiful walk—around 300 meters—across several small bridges with lovely views of the city. The journey itself adds to the overall experience.

Didn't have anything to eat so that's why there is no stars.
Really a nice place with a fabulous view for a drink or two with friends.

One of my favorite places in Stockholm. It's an outdoor beer garden with picknick benches and small tables scattered across a gravel lot overlooking the water and parts of the city from the edge of Södermalm. The vibe makes it the perfect place for a summer day.

Food is ordered with a QR code mounted on the tables and next to the bar. For drinks you gotta get up to the bars. Everything unfortunately served in single-use plastic. The drinks menu has beers, ciders and the normal mix of expected summer cocktails (and also served in jugs).

Great place to enjoy a beer with a view of Stockholm!
But the price of beer???!!!😱
A 33 cl. bottle 10 euro. That's much even in Sweden. 💰💰💰
A simple watery draft 8 euro...
Please, don't let your greed be on the menu... 😥
It is a really nice spot to have a beer and chill. The beer is good, the view of the city is amazing. The place is also child friendly and you can come with a stroller if you are with your family.

googleEditorialSummary: Bustling gathering place for drinks & quick bites with an outdoor beer garden & live music.

googleReviews.text:
Great coffee shop, with tasty pastries!

The location is very privileged with nice views of the nearby church. There is also a lot of available street parking if you come by car.

Definitely try the red velvet croissant!

This bakery offers a wide variety of pastries. It has glass windows so customers can watch the bakers at work. I tried the matcha latte but wasn't very impressed, although the red velvet croissant was delicious. I'll definitely come back.

Mr Cake is hands down my favorite place in Stockholm for cakes and desserts. They also have a great bakery selection, but the cakes are what truly won me over. As someone from Argentina who loves sweets, it was really hard to find a place in Stockholm that lived up to my expectations—until I found Mr Cake. This place exceeded them in every way. I’ve been going almost every Friday morning for breakfast, and every single time the staff is incredibly kind and welcoming. Every cake I’ve tried has been absolutely delicious. It’s a place I truly love and one that became really special to me.

Great place for everyone who likes a decadent cake. They are big, delicious and it’s perfect to share a few as it’s hard to choose! You can be seated both indoor and outdoors.
And there is blankets if you are cold. Do try their red velvet croissant, it’s so good! But they all are..
They have several locations, my favourite is the one at Rådmansgatan.

Ordered online. I was gifted a 3 pack chocolate bars and a Love Letter pralineask. I have to say I loved the Love Letter but hated the chocolate bars! I'm not over exaggerating when I say they are the worst tasting bars I can ever remember. The pralines were delicious and flavorful you understand why the high price tag but the bars, I took two bites (and that's cause I was hoping the taste might grow on me the second time around...it did not) and had to throw them away. The crunchy peanut was like eating dust and the poppy popcorn tastes like old popcorn that just sticks to your teeth. It's hard when you get a gift and you're like "mmmm thanks so much" with a big smile just to claim you'll eat it later and then just hide it in the bin. Pralines yes, bars NO

Really Good muffin. Was a little thick at first and was apprehensive when they atuck it in the microwave, but it was really good. The staff were really friendly and helpful; going out of their way to clean and answer qs.

With that name for a bakery, I expected to get some really good muffins. However, I was badly disappointed. Their muffins were not very exciting, they were very dry and not worth the money at all. The service in the shop was also a bit funny. Even though we spoke English to the person behind the counter, he continued to speak Swedish to us. If she had said she didn't speak English, we would have solved it with a translator app. But the whole thing seemed really awkward.
I had the strawberry cheesecake muffin. Really good! Fresh and mildly sweet.

We ordered caffe latte, hot chocolate, calloni, muffins of many kinds, shrimp sandwich and bagel. Service was passive aggressive. The drinks were great. The calloni and the rye bread tasted old and dusty. Bagel was ok. Shrimps are fresh, eggs are old. It's not worth your money. Don't come here.

I was on search for good cannoli and came across Muffin Bakery. The pistachio cream is amazing, mouthwatering , creamy, not too sweetand the shell is extraordinary, made with crushed nuts. + very nice staff :)

name: Mummus hummus & more

googleReviews.text:
A small, cozy restaurant.
Seriously the best hummus I've had in Stockholm.
The portions were huge, so I washed it down with some free-bread.
A must-visit for hummus lovers.
I'll definitely go back to try the other flavors.

Jordanian style Hummus with bread served by hospitable hosts in a cosy place in the city center.
I liked the food quality very much the chicken breast was jucy and delicious the portions seem small at first but digging in you realize that it is a lot of food! You're offered nice food and extra bread.

Excellent food and service, with exceptionally warm and welcoming staff. The owner made sure we felt at ease and took time to explain the menu. Complimentary sweets at the end added a perfect touch to a wonderful experience.

We have a lot of hummus places back home but this one was better than all of the them. I got the pesto with chicken, the ingredients were so fresh and tasty, and the chicken was very tender.

The owner is so nice and the atmosphere of the place is really good as well.

Would definitely recommend!

Generous portions
Tasty but I was missing some more herbs as well as something acidic breaking off the taste (pomegranate or similar would be nice)
Would give a 4 if I didn’t find bread a bit bland and the table dirty

googleEditorialSummary: Made-to-order hummus is served alongside typical Middle Eastern bites at this informal eatery.

googleReviews.text:
Really nice Bangladeshian food!
Disclaimer: I love Indian food, that’s very similar, but I still haven’t been to both countries myself.

Compared to the most Indian restaurants in Europe, the menu was surprising! Finally something else than butter chicken (although I love it). And I’ve never seen Duck on an Indian menu before so maybe I prefer the Bangladeshian kitchen 😊

The hasher josh was really good, reminded me a bit of indonesian duck, very tender and great spices.
The nihari gost was very tender too, no wonder, if it’s cooked for 5 hrs 😆
I had the mixed thali, and at first I thought it looked a bit small as a portion, but it was enough. The shrimp sauce was very nice, but the shrimps were just ok. The lamb was awesome, but the menu said it were 3 stews and it was ‘just’ a lamb chop.
The Balti chicken was nice too. The naan was good, pretty fluffy, not the best we know.

The desserts: brownie was above average, the icecream sizzlar was very disappointing.
(A bad Belgian waffle on a sizzling plate, which burned the waffle after a while, with vanilla and mango icecream on it with some pomegranate on it)

As drinks we had a bottle of good Merlot and an apple juice, and that came with a straw (happens a lot, I AM NOT A CHILD! 😖).
Other remarks: service was fine, fast enough, but not really friendly/happy. Very hard to understand when they mentioned the dishes.

For the main courses you should definitely go there, even though it’s a bit pricey!
I ordered the Nihari Gost. This consisted of lamb shank and curry with naan. It tasted really good. I ordered takeout and the packaging was not good. It had leaked everywhere.

Very nice Bengali place with authentic dishes, friendly service and nice interiors. Plus for also making an effort with presentation, which isn't very common for this type of food - made it feel extra special. This place is literally next door from us so will definitely visit again. Thank you!

This is a really friendly place, which earns it the warm recommendations! Cosy interior, and we liked the ambition of the restaurant to introduce Bangladeshi food to a mainstream audience, including dishes like "lotpoti" with chicken heart. Reasonably priced and several vegetarian options.

My husband and I stumbled upon this place and we are so glad we did! Fresh, tasty food. Pictured is the Tikka masala and the palak paneer kofta. It was very good! FYI, the Tikka masala was a little spicy, which I loved, but heads up to anyone who might want something else :) service was excellent and a cozy, modern atmosphere. We will keep this place in mind the next time we are in town!

Went there with high expectations, hoping that I would take some samples to my friends back home, but we left disappointed.
The chocolate was so watery, like it was made with water, and it tasted awful. The V60 was okay, but I expected more based on reviews, it tasted just like some regular batch brew, it seemed that it had more potential, but wasn't prepared right. The pastry was great.
I appreciate that they offer Sibarist filters, you won't get that in many places. I think it has great potential, and the barista probably didn't have her day, but everything from the service to the beverage was disappointing.

The very best of the micro-roasters. The cup we had was truly outstanding, with crisp, clean, and citric notes that shone through exactly as described on the bag. The clarity and brightness in the cup reflected the hallmark of Nordic roast. It left a refreshing finish that made us want to linger over every sip. We enjoyed it so much that we brought back a bag, and we’re already planning to subscribe once we return home.
If you love high quality coffee, this is the place! Excellent speciality coffees, cozy atmosphere and really friendly staff. Highly recommend!

I had very high expectations for this place given the reviews. I had even walked an hour to get here specifically to come, however I was highly disappointed. The cinnamon bun looked great yet upon having the first bite, I could taste how stale it was. Along with the bun, I got a cappuccino which wasn’t bad but I cannot say I was impressed. Overall, I left still needing another fika stop.
Loved it here! Jack serves amazing sandwiches and matcha fresco 👌 will be back at now one of my favorite cafes in Stockholm.

Amazing restaurant located in one of the nicest parts of Stockholm! We had nice drinks at the bar and enjoyed the unbelievable view before we got our table and ordered the food. Simply an amazing food experience and great to see that the food and drink culture in Stockholm is finally progressing! Also very affordable when comparing nearby restaurants with same standard

1h for the food to arrive, half of it cold once it arrived so had to send it back.

A normal kebab place has better skewers.

Waitress was kind and offered a discount.

Wonderful place! The food is amazing and very tasteful, we had grilled fish and meat and it was one of the best meals we've ever had. The staff is super friendly and accommodating, they were looking after us the whole evening to make sure we had a great experience. The view is magical with the sunset over Stockholm. Can highly recommend this place!
Amazing Culture and food. A great outdoor venue that has an excellent night life.

My sister and I went here the other day for my birthday and were unfortunately quite disappointed with our experience as a whole. Some of these things we would not have cared about but the treatment of the restaurant manager (Felix) and the owner (Rima) were ultimately below all standards.

Firstly, we had booked a table, we arrived on time and were told to wait in the bar because they had not reserved our table for us. We waited for about 10 minutes to be seated.
Secondly, we were seated right next to the grill, resulting in smoke from the grill coming directly on to us. To be fair, we asked for a different table and were moved to a better spot shortly after.
Thirdly, our wait time for food was long (1.5 hrs for any food), the food itself was OK, some dishes were balanced well, while others were way too salty (the kebab especially).

As we ordered a fair amount of food we were unable to finish all of it, so we asked one of the waitresses for a take-away container. Instead of seeing what she could do for us she started making excuses about how they just recently opened and didn't have many things in stock, including a take-away container. She told us to "keep at it" in regards to our food. We later asked Felix for a take-away container and he was able to quickly source one for us. So the first waitress was just making up excuses for no reason.

We paid our bill (which seemed larger than expected, but we did not question it at the time) and headed home. At home we received a text message from the restaurant host saying that we paid the wrong bill (we paid more than 3x the amount of what we should have paid, roughly 3300 kronor, including a 5% tip). They told us to come back to the restaurant in order to settle a refund, however as we were already home this was not an option for us. The host was generally not helpful in trying to find an alternative to us returning to the restaurant but insisted we should come the following day. As we asked them to send our actual bill - we discovered they had charged us for a dish that we never received (roughly 100 kronor). We ordered 10 dishes but received only 9 (luckily we had taken a photo of all our dishes and spotted the discrepancy). In summary, they owed us roughly 2470 kronor and were not helpful in finding a solution nor apologetic about their mistake.

The following day they offered to transfer the money to our bank account (finally) but did not transfer the full amount as they thought they did not owe us the tip, mind you we paid a 5% tip on a bill more than 3x larger than what we should have paid. At no point prior to this had they offered to transfer us the money. After we asked for the full amount of what they owed to be refunded (including tip), the owner of the restaurant (Rima) transferred us the remainder of the money and sent us a text message stating how "restaurants make mistakes and you don't have to be so rude". At no point did they ask for feedback on our experience or take proper responsibility for their F up.

1/5, I will never go there again and hope you don´t either! Spend your money somewhere else.

name: Mälarpaviljongen

googleReviews.text: This is one of my favorite places to go relax, eat and have some drinks with friends all while enjoying a good view and the occasional breeze on a hot summer day in Stockholm. Highly recommend it to everyone! 😍

I love this place so much! In my opinion, it is the best place to get kanelbulle in Stockholm. It is basically a nice restaurant next to the sea. Some parts of it are even floating on the water! They have many different items including breakfast and lunch, pastries, and they also have wine. The bathrooms are behind the restaurant towards the path. Also, the prices are quite high, but it is so worth it given the really cool experience you will have. There is a bus stop nearby, but expect to walk a bit.
If you are ever in the area, I would highly recommend coming here and trying the fresh kanelbulle!

Scenic location, charming environment and good drinks in normal prices for central Stockholm.
However, the food is pricey and average and the service is almost non-existent.
So yeay for drinks, nay for food.

Beautiful outdoor space right on the water with a modern vibe and good bites to eat and wine. Perfect place to go to get some sunshine and enjoy the lovely view after work in the summer. I recommend the halloumi burger which they can also make gluten-free 🥰

An absolutely wonderful place with chic and chill ambiance whether you come for dinner, drinks or a cup of tea on the water. Very cool vibe, relaxing music, people of all ages and unbeatable views. Some guests even jumped into the water for a quick swim and then went back to their table.

googleEditorialSummary: Renowned summer-only cafe/bar on a floating pontoon, with seasonal, creative menus & lush garden.

name: Mäster Anders

googleReviews.text:
Great dishes, great environment. But the staff has been quite rude. We just sat down the staff kept hurrying us up to order wine and first dish (we didn't even finish reading the menu).

During the meal, without consent, the staff took away the food that we haven't finished, didn't bother to ask, and the staff just flew away immediately.

Everything is in the rush, seemed that they want us to finish the food quickly. Very unpleasant and unfriendly.

Despite they have nice food, with such vintage trattoria kind of vibe, they do not trattano bene(treat well) the customers. Won't dine here again.
Delicious meatballs and VERY nice and friendly people who did their best for my New Year's Eve dinner even though I had no booking and it was pretty busy.

Booked at table at 17:15 for me, my wife and our 1 year old. Left a comment in the booking that we were bringing a 1 year old and needed a kids chair. When we arrived the waitress said that they do not have any kids chairs and was rather rude. She also said that we should have left a comment about brining a kid, which we did. She checked our booking again and saw the comment.
She gave no apology for missing our comment, told us there was nothing she could do and asked us to leave.
Horrible service and they obviously don’t want kids there.

Came for lunch and I liked it. The place has a cosy atmosphere and the food is nice. We had great service but we also came a bit later than the lunch rush hour. I think this is a good place if you want to have a more intimate lunch were you can discuss important matters.

Pretty solid.
The entrecôte was delicious and so were the fries 👌🏻
Service very warm and accommodating

googleEditorialSummary: French cuisine prepared in an open-to-view kitchen in a classic brasserie with mirrors on the walls.

googleReviews.text: Great location with waterside tables. Recommend to order 2-3 tapas per person, which were very tasty!
The lack of reviews must mean it’s new bc it’s amazing— the best!

Good and fun place. The food was fantastic and the layout was fun. Small dishes like "tapas" with a sea-inspired tone.
My party and I ordered four dishes and were full.

The staff were super nice and accommodating. Strange that there weren't more people when we were there. Lovely music in the background and a bar you could go up and order from.

The fact that the rocking boat feeling was constantly with us didn't make things worse. Very peaceful!

The place could have been a little better cleaned as spiders and cobwebs tend to occur at sea. It would have been good if the staff swept the place before they opened every day.

The menu from the street was a little hidden, here they could have made it a little more visible. As well as a more welcoming entrance.

But I really recommend this place!

I was scammed for two glasses of wine because they claimed that the first payment in Apple Pay did not go through. After five minutes I had to pay again. That is, double billing.
Also, there is no phone number for them to correct.
Lovely location at sunset, wonderful guys in the service and good quality

Wanted to try since we live in the neighborhood.

Lunch menu lacked vegetarian food so we substituted.   Unfortunately we will probably not come back even though we live a couple blocks away.

Service was nice and place was clean.   Food for us was so so.

Incredibly friendly staff who spoke great English for us tourists. Authentic Vietnamese food and simple decor and seating to give it a comfortable edge. Trust them with what they advise according to your likes.
Best Banh Xeo (Vietnamese pancake) in Stockholm 🥰 The dishes are cooked with South Vietnam style, really love it! Will try Pho next time.

Came here, because the place is relatively highly rated on HappyCow in Stockholm and I live nearby. Taste- and service-wise I really can't complain. I had the vegan chicken with glass noodles and it was a big portion and tasted good. I think that the vegan options could be better labelled and there could be more of them, but seeing that this place is close by, I'll certainly come here again.

Decent food, spacious restaurant with 2 levels of seating. The restaurant was rather empty on a weekend afternoon. We didn't have to wait long for our orders.

Food :

- Vietnamisiska buffalovingar (Buffalo Chicken Wings) 👍👍👍👍
These fried chicken wings were the most enjoyable. They csme hot, are medium sized and seasoned with a light oyster sauce and served on a bed of lettuce.

- Vietnamese ice coffee 👍👍👍👍
They do this well here. This is served in a tall glass filled with ice and milky Vietnamese brewed coffee.

- Combo. Bún Thịt Nướng    👍👍👍
This is a plate of rice noodles topped with a prawn skewer, fried spring roll and grilled pork fillet. A light seasoning of fish sauce is provided.

- Coconut water 👍👍👍

- Cà ri 👍👍
This is a dish of red coconut curry with pineapple pieces and red bell peppers accompanied with whiterice. I ordered the tofu version.

The ingredients are fresh and comes with  many tofu pieces. Rice was good but found the curry too sweet and tasted more like a Thai red curry dish.

Non-solo bar had sweet and savory options for breakfast and me and my five girlfriends loved eating here. We also sat at a cute little booth with a view of the outdoors. It was awesome. Five stars for me. Goodbye.

Our morning at Non Solo Bar was a true treat.  We came here by recommendation from one local person. As we indulged in our breakfast, we couldn't help but appreciate the delicious and flavorful offerings, making the slightly higher prices understandable given the quality. The ambiance was enhanced by the option to enjoy our meal outdoors, soaking in the atmosphere of Stockholm. What truly stood out, however, was the warmth and hospitality of the staff. From the moment we arrived, we were greeted with genuine friendliness and attentiveness, which made our dining experience even more enjoyable.

Very nice brunch place! The food was amazing (everyone’s ingredients were super flavorful, especially the hummus and the salmon) and the coffee as well. The service was really good and fast..its just a great place to start your day :)

Worst food!! Honesty what is the hype of this place and it's always full? We ordered a sallad, carbonara, pizza, club sandwich and a schnitzel. Out of the 5 dishes only the pizza was good. The meat was as stiff as a rock and the pasta and sallad were super salty you could hardly eat it. So disappointed.
Definitely not worth the money and I am not coming back again.

Me and my friend decided to have lunch in this place. We ordered two salads - Cesar and Rakor. The order came quite quick, but Rakor salad leaves were not fresh at all (you can see it in the picture), so my friend didn’t eat them, almost half salad she left uneaten. We paid before ordering, so we decided to ask to return money for salad as it was not good quality. Waitress made us wait at the table, but after 5 mins we decided to go inside and check at the cash desk where is she. She was talking on the phone with administration. They told we can’t get money, since we ate SOME salad already and didn’t let them know about our issue immediately. So we felt like it was our fault. Though they gave us two pastries instead, the behaviour was absolutely unacceptable and they even didn’t say sorry for the waiting and overall poor experience. Never go to this place again!!!

We have ordered and enjoyed sushi from Nonni last 1 year but last couple of visits there is a significant drop of the quality( rice is falling apart) and sizes of sushi, prices are increased and somehow always when you order you get a feeling from a girl in cashier that you do something wrong (service in that way didn’t change …) sad that one good place with sushi been “destroyed”
An excellent Japanese joint. Very flavoursome food, excellent free miso and green tea for diners. The meal deal with a main dish and a side of 5 sushi pieces is a bargain!

UPDATE....WAS My 1ST place to go when I want great sushi or Bowl, but several times I was served dishes way different (guess it was some other chef then usually), ordered bowl with mix of fish - rice was overcooked, this time there were way smaller fish bites then the day before, the fish itself tested not that good as the day before, added too much mayonnaise..soup sometimes really good but other days too spicy. Since then i prefer to eat in other Sushi restaurant where they have permanent dish quality and WAYYYYY LOWER prices... Sorry Nonni, it was once amazing, now it is lottery.

The beef bibimbap was overall an okay dish, although not very flavourful. The beef was served as small chunky bits rather than thinly sliced beef.

The restaurant has good interior, felt welcoming and nice to dine in.

The food is amazing. Really great value for money. I’m a big fan of this place but to get 5 stars I would suggest to sand the tables as they are really sticky. The menus stands get dropped regularly. Also raise the lights slightly as we had a head bang, and a sign for restroom oh and the playlist was not appropriate. But please don’t focus on the negatives. I’m just happy to help in improving things.

we had our first and last meals here while staying in Stockholm. it was absolutely satisfying and delicious. the sushi was beautifully served and staff there made us feel quite welcomed here during our first visit here. will definitely come here again if we are visiting the city in the future!

I do not know where to begin. I was visiting Stockholm with my church and the guys I were with wanted sushi. I hate sushi, I never have but there was no where else to go, as this was right by our hotel. I am here to tell you my life has changed. This was the greatest restaurant experience i have ever had. You wouldn’t think this is a 2 man operation. The most hospitable restaurant environment I have ever been in. There are too many good things to say about this place and I will leave with this: norio legitimately changed my life. I now like sushi, I now know how to use chop sticks, but more importantly, I made a friend. Do yourself a favor and make this your new local spot. Say hello to Norio, Boat, and even Andy if he’s hanging. Till next time!

The best sushi I have ever had. Norio is an EXCELLENT chef. Not only does he make the best sushi rolls, but he also curates an environment that’s warm and fun and peaceful. Grateful for this evening with my friends to celebrate what Jesus has done in our lives and the joy set before us!!

Amazing food, the sushi is so good ! I want to go again and again. It's as good as sushi I have eaten in a market in Japan.
The chef was so nice, they even gave us extra sushi 🍣🍣.
Thank you very much.

Newly opened Japanese restaurant in town! Absolutely incredible! If you’re looking for sushi in Stockholm look no further, try this place the you’ll not disappointed! Thanks a lot for all the incredible lunch filled with taste explosions in my mouth 🙏 🫶🏼🍣

Really tasty food, one of the highlights I've found in Stockholm so far. All sourced locally with mindfulness given to how each source cares for what they produce. (Fun note they gave, the only thing they could not source from Sweden was the pepper!) Really nice non-alcoholic alternatives in addition to the wines.

Very unassuming exterior. The place inside is beautiful. Really cozy decor. And the service is fantastic. Higher price, but worth it. Definitely worth a visit, a nice night out. At the moment I don't think reservations are required (and least not the weekday I was there), but with how this is that could change!

Depending on your hunger, price is likely between 300-600kr (without drinks) for one person.

On a random walk home yesterday, I saw this new restaurant which had posted the ingredients of the week outside. Booked dinner for tonight and had the best and most interesting restaurant experience in a long time. The chef did a great job of presenting the food - robustly and interestingly - and the waiters gave useful and truthful tips, including the amount of food to order. Everything was delicious but I particularly enjoyed the "pölsa" of lamb, a Swedish version of haggis! Strongly recommended.

One of the best dining experiences I’ve had😍

We discovered Restaurang Nuvära through a Boka Bord tasting list featuring standout restaurants offering food tastings dinners under 1000 SEK—and this place more than delivered.

The restaurant opened recently (November), already has well-deserved five-star reviews, and it’s easy to understand why. The tasting menu was 17 courses, entirely focused on local Swedish ingredients—with a beautiful emphasis on lamb, sheep, and fish. Every dish felt intentional, creative, and deeply connected to the land.

The flavors were layered and thoughtful, the presentation was stunning without feeling pretentious, and the pacing was perfect. We were there for nearly three hours, and it never once felt rushed. Each course built on the last, and we found ourselves genuinely excited for what would come next.

What truly elevated the experience, though, was the staff. There were a few women working that evening, and every single one of them was warm, attentive, and genuinely kind. You could feel how well they worked together—checking in on us, supporting one another, and creating a relaxed, welcoming atmosphere. One of them seemed to be the manager (possibly even part owner), and her presence really set the tone for the entire experience.

The chef experience was another highlight. The head chef came out twice during the evening to speak with us, alongside his assistant, and took the time to explain where some of the ingredients were sourced from. That personal touch—hearing directly from the person creating the food—made the entire meal feel even more special and grounded in intention.

Overall, the experience felt refined yet warm, creative yet deeply human. You can tell there’s real care behind every detail—from sourcing to service.

We left completely blown away by the food, the people, and the atmosphere. If you appreciate local ingredients, slow dining, and a truly memorable tasting menu, this place is an absolute must.

Highly, highly recommend.

Go, and go hungry. Probably the best food experience in a long time.

Courses just keep coming and you can feel the love from the chef and team in every bite. Amazing wines, too, and fair prices for stockholm.
Vasastan's new nose to tail -restaurant not to be missed by foodies.

I was absolutely starving and popped in for an evening meal. I ordered something different, I went for the schnitzel. I missed the fact the potatoes were covered in mustard. I am not a big fan. That is definitely on me. The schnitzel was deep fried and very oily. It also seemed heavily seasoned and salty. The beers were great and made locally. I think I just had a did meal. The vibe here is good. My partner's food was good..albeit a little over salted too.

I went here in the evening with friends. We sat inside at a high table which was perfect. Drank pints and shared plates. The beer was delicious and the veda-like malt bread on the sharing plates was to die for! (Is this made with left over yeast from the brewing process?) All the sharing plates were delicious really.
The location is great. I arrived in a taxi but there were both cars and bikes parked outside.
The staff were fun, knowledgable, friendly and helpful. I would go back with more friends again.
Thank you for a memorable evening.

Very cool microbrewery with many delicious beers. I did the tour in English (which they give on Tuesdays) and tasted 4 beers. All were spectacular. Tour guide was super friendly. Afterwards, I stayed to have another pint on the patio. Location is right by a ferry stop and super convenient.

Nice place to enjoy good crafted beer and looking over the nice scenery in Hammarby Sjöstad. The beer is a bit pricey for 40 cl, around SEK 85-95. I only had a beer so my review is only about the outdoor area and beer. Recommend this place for a beer.
First of all: thank you for the nice afternoon @carnegiebryggeriet! We just had some drinks - sadly no food - which really looked great. The staff is super super friendly - we really had a nice time!

This place was packed out at lunchtime and we had the christmas tasting ‘open’ sandwich special. We had never tried herring before and it was delicious. Everything on the plate had a good balance and was very fresh. Would highly recommend here for lunch.

We were captivated by the display cases of shrimp dishes at this restaurant stall inside Östermalm Food Hall. We paid around $35 CAD for a toast skagen, which is essentially a piece of brown bread topped with a ton of small shrimps, some coated in a lovely creamy sauce.
We were extremely delighted by the taste of the shrimps; they were incredibly tender and sweet. The bread on the bottom had a hint of sweetness as well, and adding the lemon almost made it taste like a dessert. My mom and I shared one dish as an appetizer for our later meals, and we would absolutely recommend this to anyone looking for a nice Swedish delicacy.
Very great food, big portions. Although very expensive (76€ for 4 breads)

From the inside Östermalms Saluhall, Nybroe Smörrebröd is an unassuming place packed with a lot of flavor! The open faced pickled herring sandwich (Smörrebröd) was topped with the tiniest cut chive toonds, whipped butter, diced red onions, dill, and fresh radish slices. It was a work of art! The pickled herring was so good too. And, the side of potatoes were perfectly buttery and a bit crispy on the outside and soft and warm on the inside. It really was the perfect little meal (or snack, depending on how hungry you are). The music was a bit loud and the staff/waitress didn’t seem super interested in making small talk, but they were nice enough.

Nybroe was a real disappointment. The service was extremely unfriendly, with a noticeably bad attitude that made us feel unwelcome from the start. We then had to wait 40 minutes for a smørrebrød that was already sitting on the counter in the glass display — so not even freshly prepared, just taken out and served. No explanation, no apology, nothing. For the prices and the reputation, this level of service is simply unacceptable. I will definitely not recommend this place and won’t be coming back.

Their potato gratang is to die for.
I know people come here for burgers, fish and chips or steak but his potato gratang is so creamy and cheesy I cant have anything else here.
Can recommend ceasar sallad too.

The place is located in the centre and has several rooms, with many tables and screens.
Several sport events are streamed but not many.

The food is expensive, quantities are small, and the quality is not great.
I had a sirloin steak because the tenderloin was not available.
I asked for rare and came medium rare.
Not very rich in flavour, very small.
Indeed the knife was bigger than the steak (look at the photo).

Wifi was slow and unstable.

The service is almost inexistent, you order with an app and they bring you food, sometimes not even knowing which table the order belongs to.
No explanation of the menu, no welcome etc.
What's the point of going out if no interaction occurs with the staff?
My view.

However, the staff was helpful when asked.

But still, it was a waste of money.

I would not recommend, at least for dinner.
Maybe for some (expensive) pints.

We had lunch in this restaurant Stockholm Central on July 11th, 2024.
When paying, we were told by a restaurant employee that the payment apparently hadn't worked. Unfortunately, we didn't receive any receipt and no information why the payment didn't work. One of the employee asked us to pay again. We did that and got a receipt during the second payment process.
Now I noticed that I have two debits from O'Learys Stockholm Central. Therefore we contacted the restaurant in order to ask to immediately refund the amount. But nothing happened. We cannot reach anybody.  No phone and no e-mail go through.

Personally had a really good experience here. The beer was nice and clean lines. Service was really friendly I ordered at the bar twice and had quick service, with a smile, in english. There are screen throughout so no blind spots for football. Overall really enjoyed here. I can't comment on the food but a limited menu is a good sign. Will be back.
