In addition, the manager initiated a disagreement with me in front of my guests regarding the closing time. All official information stated they close at 23 p.m. However, the manager insisted it says that they close at 22 p.m.
Waterfront location is very nice, drinks menu is extensive, and the orange basil daiquiri (pic) is amazing. Staff seems overworked though.
The view is nice otherwise not all staff is polite, the food is ok,but not so good, more overpriced,especially deserts.

name: Portofino

googleReviews.text: Got only a table outside. But they have heaters and blankets. Turned out to be good. Inside was packed and loud. Quality of the food was outstanding. Had the carpaccio for appetizer and penne for my main. Great choice. Our waiter Olivia delivered great service with a smile. She made that evening truly special. Not the cheapest restaurant in Stockholm but very much worth it.

«First time» in Stockholm, and I’m very happy that me and my friend choose Portofino as our dinner spot.

➕ Very welcoming and friendly staff/waitresses. And the male waitress we had was such a sweetheart.
➕ Super cozy and authentic place.
➕A good variety of options on the menu - something for everyone.
➕ The food was very good - especially the steak. 🥩

➖ Nothing special negative to comment. Overall very happy with our visit at Portofino.

With kind regards Jannicke and Ella.

Food was awesome! We were a group of 4 but was sat by a dead end corner when the restaurant isnt full house. We struggle to get services to order extra drinks as there isn't any service available. Waited 1 hour for our main course after starter, we shared our concerns to the waiter (a tall silver grey hair man) and we was very angry challenged back that he is not late to serve and we just have to wait for it. Due to such unwelcome experience we rank their service as 1 star. Meanwhile two of our guess got a nasty belly after having tuna / tonno (in Swedish) as their main meal. I had Bigoli Frutti de Mer / seafood pasta which taste 5/5 . I can tell the pasta are freshly made with perfect al dente cook texture. As for the carpacio starter, it is nothing to shout out so I give this as 2/5 stars.
What a wonderful surprise. Serving and food was top! Maybe not so Italian for a starter but the Oysters were sublime. Will come back next time in Stockholm

Amazing find in Gamla Stan! Super friendly and fun staff, quick service and delicious food!

And cocktails! I was expecting a shot sized drinks, but look at the size! 😍

googleEditorialSummary: Informal Italian restaurant with a terrace serving pasta, grills & pizzas, plus desserts.

googleReviews.text: Amazing Italian food at a great price with awesome service. I had the melanzane alla parmigiana and it was excellent—generous, comforting, and full of flavor without being heavy. It comes with a green salad perfectly dressed with lemon and olive oil, finished with a delicate touch of Parmigiano. Everything feels simple, well balanced, and made with care. True homemade Italian cooking.

the sitting area and tables are small. its best suited for couples. food was fresh and tasty and came fast. i was a bit disappointed that the spaghetti and meatballs came with 2 small meatballs😭. i expected more / bigger for the price. my friend enjoyed the tangetelli and chicken (essentially a chicken schnitzel)
staff was attentive but it would have been good to put the machine in the center of the table if she wasn't sure who was paying instead of automatically putting it in front of the man 🙄

overall good experience😊
All the pastas we tried were a little too al dente for our liking. The sauces were ok. Decent place to get a semi quick meal.
Really enjoyed the lunch here. The prices were very reasonable for the amount of food you get. I really enjoyed the vodka pasta. The service was good and attentive. Would really recommend

This food quality with this price at this location with a not bad environment, I was very satisfied.
The pasta was cooked perfectly to my liking, not too soft nor hard. The flavors were a good combo of creamy and spicy, with a balance of cheesy and meaty. The tiramisu was not too heavy nor sweet, it almost felt refreshing with its light texture, and it was needed after a heavy pasta.

After helping a friend move for an entire day and evening we were starving. Prego was just about to close around 21.00 but made us a few pizzas to-go. Very nice of them even though they were about to close. Pizza was a solid 4/5.
Thank you Prego!

Was GREAT in the beginning. Then food got progressively worse, I stopped ordering real food after getting spoiled meat twice in a row that I sent back (an inedible entrecôte that was grey inside and smelled like five day old meat, and then a bifteki the week after that was even worse). I only eat vegetarian pizza here now, even the meat on the pizzas is bad unless it's canned capricciosa ham.
Pretty decent food with friendly staff. Good option after football or Training. Price is okay and location near Metro
The service was super nice and friendly. Pizza was very nice. Definitely will go again.
Sadly one of the worst meat things I ever got served.😭 Maybe other dishes are better.

Prime Burgers & Smokehouse is an absolute masterclass in meat and fire right in the heart of Stockholm. From the second you walk in, the aroma of the smokehouse hits you like a warm hug from a pitmaster. This place serves up burgers that are juicy, perfectly seasoned, and stacked with ingredients that actually make sense. But the real secret weapon here is the smokehouse menu; the brisket is so tender it practically melts before it even hits your tongue.

The atmosphere is laid back and effortlessly cool, making it the perfect spot for a weekend feast or a high energy dinner with friends. The staff are incredibly friendly and clearly take pride in the craft of great barbecue. Whether you are a burger purist or a serious meat lover, this place is a total five star winner that will leave you planning your next visit before you have even finished your meal.

🙏🏻🕯👑☘️🇸🇪🕊✝️🇻🇦⭐️

Tried this place for Burgers just recently and left really impressed. The burger was tasty, juicy and full of flavour, with a nice balance between the patty, cheese and toppings. The bun was good and held together well, and the fries were a great compliment. Friendly service and quick preparation made the visit even better
will definitely come back.

Great burger and also super fun.

We got there early before dinner and spend 1 hour playing shuffleboard and having a drink. Nice different plan to do in the city!

AMAZING burgers! I went to New York burger place for lunch and then these guys for dinner and they blew the other guys out of the roof it’s not even a competition. I had the more basic original prime burger and it’s one of the best burgers I’ve ever had.
The best Burger in my whole life. Exellent staff members. Very polite and funny. Great ambience. I will come back for sure! Greetings from Poland

Stockholm’s best Italian restaurant! It’s ALWAYS a pleasure to visit. We are regulars, and the staff are always so welcoming. The food is delicious, and the atmosphere is amazing! I can’t recommend it enough, you have to visit!
Had an amazing waiter who took great care of us. I had gluten free pasta and my partner had a regular pizza. We both loved our meals and our waiter. He was extremely professional and fun.

We had a lovely evening celebrating our honeymoon at this restaurant. It is cozy, beautifully decorated, and the staff were friendly. They do take reservations but we walked in that evening. It is reasonably priced but the quality of the food was 10/10! The pasta was so fresh and the pizza was hot and fresh from their woodfire oven.
Delicious food, professional, attentive and personal service in a charming atmosphere.  Must be one of the best Italian restaurants in Stockholm. Go there.

Visiting from Canada and absolutely loved this small Italian restaurant. Delicious pasta and pizza would highly recommend!

Fantastic staff as well, they are so thoughtful and helpful. Definitely worth stopping by!

Been here a couple of times and no complaints.
Nice atmosphere and friendly service. Good selection of food, pizza was great as were the artichokes. Chips were a bit too oily and salty for me but there is a vast selection of dishes to pick from to accompany your drinks.
Their apple cider is incredible!

All at a decent price point, especially for Stockholm.

Would recommend.

I don’t understand why this place doesn’t have even better reviews. The staff is amazingly friendly and attentive, and remembers you even if you haven’t been here for six months. They make you feel special, the service is great, the prices are decent by Stockholm standards, and the food is good. It’s an authentic Italian place, which is quite rare here… There are a lot of copycats, but in the kitchen here they all speak Italian. If you’re a regular here they might even bring you limoncello just like in Italy. Highly recommend!
Great atmosphere, perfect for afterwork. The staff provides excellent service. Great food, love the mortadella pizza with buratta 👍😋

Friendly staff,
Oysters that smell like the ocean breeze,
Relaxing vibe,
Good wine!

We walked in without any expectations but the service was exemplary! I think I found my new favorite AW venue! A place that treats their customers REALLY well is somehow challenging to find in Stockholm. This place is a gem! Would gladly come back.

We wanted to order a meat and cheese board but the waiter let us know we can combine the two which was so great! We also got the arancini.

Absolutely no complaints, everything was delicious!
Coming here not the first time and each is lovely experience. One of the best pizzas in Stockholm for me. Decent price and amazing taste

Good location, and I can only judge the drink we had here, so no food review.

The Hugo drink we ordered did not taste good at all and was quite the disappointment. The tap beer was fine, nothing special. The cakes looked very generic and it seems they put more emphasis on decoration, but can not judge the food and others seem to like it. Just dont get a Hugo drink

Asked if the texture of the cheesecake should taste like it was hard and she sent someone else and told her to say that it is fresh.
I could squeeze the cheesecake and not even have anything on my finger.
It looked very icy.not worth to try

The music is better than the cake

Café Ragazzi is one of those Stockholm spots that mixes Italian charm with a relaxed, urban vibe. The location on Drottninggatan makes it easy to drop in whether you’re out shopping or meeting friends. The atmosphere is lively without feeling stressful, and the staff usually keeps things moving even when it’s busy.

Good cakes, If you want to have a coffee after 19:00 this is the place to go they have open to 00:00, we had three  coffees  Americanos the coffee tasted ok, two cakes  and one ice cream. very nice atmosphere you can order italian food and ice cream here as well.
I tried pasta and a pizza with mushrooms and tryffel it was delicious 😋

We dropped in for dinner on a weekday and there was a decent crowd.

We started with Samosa & paneer sizzler which were both disappointing. You get a single Samosa with a sweet yogurt based sauce.
Paneer sizzler was just few pieces of panner with sliced veggies in a sizzler plate. Season with some salt & pepper (nothing else)

We asked the server to make the main course a little more flavourful (more Indianize it). He understood and agreed.

We opted for Grilled chicken karahi with paneer naan. Both of them were flavourful & tasted good. Paneer naan was also good change to regular garlic naan.

I recommend the main course.

Tip: Ask them to make it more Indian version of the dishes and you’ll enjoy it more :) (if you can handle it ofcourse)

We tried paneer balti and veg biryani with garlic naan. The only thing that I think can be improved is the garlic naan and make it more softer and fluffier. Staff was very humble and accommodating. The taste of food was good too.
I ordered a delivery two nights in a row and the food, especially the mains were excellent - night one was a jalfrezi and night two a bhalti - I would definately recommend

This place is a joke. Tried several dishes that tastes not too good a very long time ago, and after a while decieded to give it a new try, went ther for dinner and even though service was lame the food was ok, so decieded to take away dinner today, and what a horrible experience, the service is just terrible, I ordered at the phone and the person who took the order says that I have ordered one dish that I have definitely not ordered because it contains Spinachs and i hate them, I know exactly what I ordered because I have a screen shot with the name of the dish in my phone, which I just read over the phone, which i showed the guy. The guy was super rude and told me that I did not ordered what I said I ordered ,that he is 100% sure of it. I would never dare to treat customers the way he treated me. He first didnt want to actually provide me with what I ordered, but at the end accepted. No need to say I am never going back, because you know, mistakes can happen and if he would have said " excuse me I thought you ordered X, I'd fix the right order for you" I would have give him a big tip to cover for the expenses of the wrong dish, but seriously, his actitude was so rude and out of place.
I strongly recommend you to not eat here. Very bad service, the food was okay but the waiter was very rude, gets irritated whenever we asked for something.

name: Ramblas

googleReviews.text: While many of the dishes aren’t entirely traditional Spanish style, the garlic shrimp were truly delicious. The paella, however, was just okay and didn’t particularly stand out. The ambience was pleasant, warm and inviting

Pretty decent tapas restaurant. My favourite dishes were the potatoes (even though I wish they had the classic batatas bravas sauce and not aioli), and the Gambas. Normal service. Very nice to sit outside, we made reservations the same day.

Not a good feeling leaving the place with average food and to me a very rude approach toward my partner, suggesting by the waiter that he was the underdog when he took the bill.

Wine bottle put on our table without agreeing for a full bottle… our fault not saying anything as we had a pleasant date night so far with a show and good night and was caught in our moment. Not understanding the waiter wanting to get rid of wine for full price and taking us for money bags.

Felt ashamed leaving as the waiter treated my partner like I was bought goods :(

Very tasty dishes varying between different cuisines. The flank steak, chorizo, and garlic bread were all terrific. Drinks were also quite nice. Some of the vegetarian dishes were slightly underwhelming. Perhaps slightly pricey, and service was not as attentive as one might like. Still, recommend trying it out.

I enjoyed the Gambas during our dinner here, the paella was just ok, i can eat it but will not order again kind of dish.
The service was slow even with a scoop of vanilla ice cream for our child took 15mins to get served.
The restaurant has several tables but I wished they had coat hangers or maybe they did but nobody showed it to us.

googleEditorialSummary: Polished bistro turning out traditional tapas & paella mains, plus beer & drinks.

googleReviews.text:
This review is based on the Korean dishes of this place.
Even though this is a ramen house, it serves authentic Korean dishes. My Korean friend took us there and the food in general was above average. The beef short rib stew was very flavorful, the seasonings went all the way through the meat, and the cabbage soaked in the fond properly. The pork kimchi wok was very well cooked, that the pork meat was tender and the veggies were crunchy. If you happen to be in the neighborhood, this is a good to go place for Korean food.
The waitress who helped our table that night was very not helpful unfortunately. First it was really hard to get hold of her when we needed to add extra dish to the table, and when she came she brought her poker face. Then she messed our order with the other table’s guest, put our dish on their bill and their dish on our bill, and gave us the other table’s bill to pay. We didn’t realize it until we were home, and when we called the next day, we were told to wait until next week when this waitress comes to her shift and call again. Luckily the next week a very helpful waitress took my call, and she was very understanding and apologetical on her colleague’s behalf. However we still had to travel there in person to get the refund fixed.
Very nice place. The food was really good - we ordered spicy and mild ramen - both were very tasty, and the spicy one was actually hot, which is not a rule in Sweden.

Not recommended: Dirty, terrible service, and inconsistent portions!

My recent visit to Esuki was extremely disappointing. The place was dirty, with sticky floors and menus and tables covered in leftover food. The chairs were uncomfortable, and the portions were inconsistent—some dishes were slightly more, others less, and nothing matched the tempting pictures on the menu. My companions received their food, but my meal never arrived. When I asked, the staff showed no concern, denied forgetting, and offered no apology. Overall, a terrible experience, and if there were minus stars, I’d give them one.

The interiors are really nicely done , but the cleanliness looked a bit off .
Food is hit & miss . Felt tad higher side in salt . It is priced on higher end as well .
Robots serving was cool . Though , i couldnt get to see them in action .
To summarise, I’ve had better Ramen in town .

We tried Ramen and other dishes overall food taste compared to price is not that great else would have rated 5star! Staff delivered ok service as well the noodles was not baked right and literally there were 4 shrimps 2 small clams in the seafood noodles:) Ramen was good and nice portion too! Give it a try for Ramen 🍜 you will like it!

Stockholm is blessed with great sushi and ramen spots, including Michelin starred ones. Raw is a great spot where the sushi is made fresh and the menu has something for everyone. We had a mix of the Mr and Mrs Sumo platter, spicy tuna, Iberico pork. Tuna Tiradito etc and it was pretty good. Also the yuzu drink is lovely.

The food left me not much impression except the beef entrecôte skewers and the side dish green beans. For this price range I expected more impressive dishes. The entrecôte skewer was juicy and tender, and the green beans were flavorful with ginger. The Yakitori chicken skewer tasted like something you could find in the frozen zone of the supermarket. The maki, crispy rice with tartar and salmon croquettes were nothing special. This was the first time I tried nigiri with parmigiano, personally I felt the flavors of the cheese were overpowering the beef and sushi rice.
The cocktail was better than I expected, the pairing of whiskey and sweet sesame cracker enhanced the layers of the flavors.
The environment light was way too red, and the music was too loud, we unfortunately couldn’t stay long.

This place has a fantastic location! We dined here twice, both times on the outdoor patio which, while not as fancy as the interior, has its own charm. The first time we were served quickly, but the second time we had to wait longer than we would have preferred. Nevertheless, food was consistently delicious! Prices are average for a mid-range sushi restaurant with an a la carte menu in this area.

I've been here multiple times, the service always good and nice.
The sushi is soo good if you like e more modern touch. The meat sishes is not the best dish served so stick to sushi, it is very good👌

My father and I wanted a nice dinner on a Thursday evening. We came in at 18:25 on 10/11/2022.

We order a few minutes later. I wanted Karage, basically fried chicken. Apparently they don’t serve it during evenings which was odd since they served Karage Serada which was shredded Karage. I don’t get why they couldn’t just not shred some Karage, but I digress. I ordered Yakitori, which is five chicken skewers with a glaze, I got it without the glaze due to a food allergy. My father ordered the Chef’s Choice which was ten sushi pieces.

The first issue was with how long it took to get the food. We ordered at roughly 18:30 and we got my skewers and five out of the ten pieces of my father’s sushi at 19:05 which I think is fine. Then my father had to remind the waiter that he was supposed to get more food at the same time I ordered a cheesecake. At 19:20 my father got three more pieces and at 19:22 I got my cheesecake. Then we had to yet again remind the server that my father had two more pieces which arrived at 19:34. We finished our food and asked to pay. We were there almost one hour and ten minutes on the dot.

My skewers were tasty, good quality and quite well portioned. I was asked if I wanted rice which I said yes to. My father liked his sushi, though it was a slightly small portion at 10 pieces, we knew that from the start and tried to order a twelve piece version, but apparently that wasn’t possible. My cheesecake was great, nothing remarkable, but tasty none the less.

My father getting half of his and then another three pieces fifteen minutes later wasn’t a big deal, but him having to wait another fifteen for the last two pieces was absurd. I want to remind you that it took thirty minutes after my food, and the first five pieces of his food, to actually get the whole meal. The waiter took my fathers chopsticks after half of the meal which was yet another annoyance. Luckily I had a pair by me.

The sound levels in the restaurant was quite cacophonous throughout our meal from 18:25, it sharply ramped up at 19:00. I don’t exactly know the ins and outs of how a restaurant should handle sound issues, but since they recently remodelled I would hope that it was a bit less loud. Some music was pumping in the background which wasn’t a problem, but it felt kind of superfluous.

The remodel was admittedly pretty. It gave me ghost in the shell vibes.

The service was fine. I don’t know if the waitstaff was grossly understaffed or if there was another cause for their slowness at waiting tables. We had to keep reminding them to get my father his food. They also kept checking in on the kitchen staff, seemingly, though I am no expert, to make sure that orders were getting through.

I requested to and was able to pay at 19:40. The full meal was 690 SEK (64 EUR, 65 USD) we tipped roughly 15% which was 100 SEK (9 EUR, 9 USD). Do note that tipping culture in Sweden is not mandatory or necessarily expected like other countries.

The Chef’s Choice, ten piece sushi, was 340 SEK (31 EUR, 32 USD)

The Yakitori, chicken skewers with rice, was 265 SEK (24 EUR, 25 USD)

The Cheesecake was 105 SEK (10 EUR, 10 USD)

All numbers rounded to nearest full number.

A beautiful Indian restaurant, a friendly, polite host and incredible fresh and tasty Indian food. We loved the place and food so much that we came back again in the same week. We were in Stockholm (from the Netherlands). Really recommend everyone who visit Stockholm to go to this place!
We come back next year when we go back to Stockholm again.

A nice little Indian restaurant with very generous lunch prices. I chose chicken and lamb stew. The price for a daily lunch includes salad, soft drink, soup of the day, and coffee. The portion was larger than I expected. Seating is available both indoors and outdoors.

Amazing flavors, great service and relaxed ambiance. It is a delight to get a taste for original Indian flavors again, the dishes are exquisite. I am enjoying the Indian chai one spoon at a time to make it last longer. I have tried many Indian restaurants throughout Europe, yet the taste of this restaurant’s cuisine blew me away.

Razmoni's enchanting Indian charm swept us away as we indulged in a culinary symphony! The cozy ambience embraced our family, the artistic interior added flair, and the Chicken Biryani, Naan, and Chicken Grill were pure delight. Every bite resonated with authentic flavors, and the manager's friendliness crowned our dining experience. Razmoni is a treasure trove of delectable delights and warm hospitality!

Really good Indian restaurant😃!
The food is good and the service is fast. I have been here two times and I will definitely come back. Come here if you want good Indian food here in Stockholm!

name: Reimersholme Hotel Matbaren

googleReviews.text:
This hostel, is good only if you are a group of young people wanting to party. The room and the common showers/toilets were very dirty, never cleaned in the month we were there. They did change the towels but the bathrooms were dirty, like you have to wear shoes to the shower, don’t want to touch anything.
We got badly treated from the team, one person shouted at us, twice.
Sauna is not worth it, it’s way too expensive for what it is.
I would not recommend.

Unique place and location. Fun atmosphere but still calm and quiet enough. Room and shared bathrooms at annex building as expected. Basic but comfortable beds. Very good breakfast! Guests from all ages. Not very accessible with stairs and heavy doors. Nice views!

Not a hotel at all more like a low end hostel without any clean or working facilities ! No power outlets in the room that worked unless u unplug the TV and lights did not work in the room plus the two toilets for guests was not clean or in working order! Shame the location was nice but nothing much around !

Would not recommend this to anyone. The stairwell was full of dust bunnies, the room wasn't properly cleaned and the interior was... No. Just no. We got disgusted by the bathroom and just decided to not get breakfast there in the morning. Not worth the risk.

The service at the reception gave a lot to wish for also. Don't ignore people just because you're eating behind the desk.

We stayed here for 3 nights with our children in the middle of summer and overall it was a good experience with ups and downs.

The location is fantastic with good access to both the tranquility of the island, a Coop for essentials and the rest of Stockholm by public transport. The nearby playpark was good and having access to a kitchen was great with kids. The breakfast was very good with something for everyone and the beds were very comfy. The bar with food and entertainment was great and we hardly heard it in our room because the windows were so thick.

The room was very run down though, the shower didn't drain and there was a broken lamp in our room. The room itself was pretty cramped with limited space for storage and overall the cleanliness of the bathroom and the public kitchen wasn't great.

This is a good budget hotel option which was popular with families and younger independent travellers. Like a hostel with private rooms, a better breakfast and a relaxed atmosphere.

googleEditorialSummary: Unfussy property offering simple rooms with shared or en suite bathrooms, plus lake views.

name: Restaurang Hasselbacken

googleReviews.text:
Hotel Hasselbacken is the legendary grand dame of Djurgården, standing proudly in the heart of Stockholm’s most beautiful park island since 1853! It is a world-class sanctuary where royal history, lush greenery, and modern elegance collide.

Famous for its magnificent dining halls and its iconic terrace, Hasselbacken is more than just a hotel—it’s a cultural landmark where generations of Stockholmers and global travelers have come to celebrate life’s most beautiful moments.

Hotel Hasselbacken is the undisputed royalty of Djurgården and a total masterclass in 'timeless glam!' Walking through the doors feels like being cast in a high-budget historical drama, but with much better Wi-Fi and a top-tier spa. It’s located in that perfect 'sweet spot' where the excitement of the ABBA Museum and Gröna Lund is just steps away, yet the hotel itself feels like a peaceful, aristocratic retreat.

The restaurant is a total showstopper—dining under those crystal chandeliers makes a simple lunch feel like a coronation. And let’s talk about the vibe; it manages to be incredibly grand without ever being stuffy. The staff are true pros who make you feel like the guest of honor every time you walk by. Whether you’re staying the night in one of their chic rooms or just stopping by for a 'fika' on the legendary terrace, Hasselbacken is the gold standard of Stockholm hospitality. Five stars for the place that invented the Hasselback potato and continues to reinvent luxury every single day!"

🙏🏻🕯👑☘️🇸🇪🕊✝️🇻🇦⭐️

This review is about the restaurant :
The restaurant is located in a beautiful and charming old building. The space is chic and refined, spacious and comfortable. The waiters are attentive and polite. The food is good, but I didn't know that the famous Hasselback potatoes came with tuna, salmon and caviar as a topping and I don't eat seafood. I ordered a dish of dumplings with mushrooms; it was very good but could have had less butter. I found it a little oily. The dessert was good but nothing exceptional. The drinks were good.

We recently stayed for 5 nights in a family suite on the ground floor (2 teens shared the pullout couch) and it was perfect for us. Kids had a bit of their own space at a distance from us, including patio doors that opened to a little courtyard. Room was across the hall from a lovely sauna and cold plunge room and we used it every day and there was never anyone there but us. Spacious clean bathroom with a great shower. Everything felt very clean. Helpful and kind front desk. You can borrow hotel bikes and ride around Djurgarden and feel like you're in the countryside or hop on the ferry and be downtown in minutes.  Our favourite cafe Bla Porten was also across the road for easy food when we were too tired to go out. Morning buffet was excellent.

We had a wonderful stay! The hotel is in a great location, perfect for attending the annual dinner from the Dutch chamber of commerce at the Vasa Museum. The room was comfortable, we slept very well, and the breakfast was absolutely delicious.

This review is for the restaurant. We had our daughter’s baptism lunch there and we were a fairly small party of 22. The staff were amazing and the food was really good. There was a jazz band playing at the restaurant that day which added to the atmosphere.

We were really really pleased with everything and I wanted to give them a 5 star review but unfortunately the communication with the restaurant was a disaster on the weeks leading up to the event - they didn’t answer the phone nor did they answer emails. Hence the 4 stars.

googleEditorialSummary: Upmarket hotel featuring a spa area & a gym, plus a bar & a restaurant with water views.

googleReviews.text:
I enjoy a place that cooks normal ingredients into fabulous dishes. Combined with a dine in the kitchen environment, the whole dinner was a great experience.
The beef tartar, which I’m referring to their own creation 50/50 tartar, was a harmony of different savory bites. The artichokes’ crunchiness matched the softness of the beef, and the egg yolk added the creaminess without extra grease. The grilled perch absorbed in the charcoal scent was very tender, the seafood butter sauce was not so salty, and the carrots balanced the whole dish well. My personal favorite was the leek and potato dish, such plain ingredients can do miracle when cooked right. The potatoes were served both fried and deep fried, and the leek was served both fried and into a cream sauce. That created multi layers of texture, and the seasonings were used just right.
I would definitely come back for new dishes in the future.

Our dinner was wonderful and the staff were so kind and friendly. The cocktails were excellent and the selection of dishes was refreshing and felt well suited for for the summer. Dishes were fresh and vibrant, and the cocktails were well suited for the meal. Standout dishes were the tartlets, artichoke, tartar, and cucumber and white chocolate dessert. We also enjoyed the overall atmosphere and interior of the restaurant.

Food was great, set menu has nice connections. Correct use of salt, fat, sweet, sour. Also nice use of soft and crunchy in the dishes. I was a bit disappointed at the end as they didn't serve elderflower tea as it would complete the circle back to the first dish. Staff was friendly but I had mixed feelings.

We dined at Restaurang Oxenstiernan while visiting Stockholm. We were excited to dine there because of the sustainability mention in the Michelin Guide. Unfortunately my husband’s oyster arrived to the table with a worm wiggling in it. The server apologized, but it did taint our experience.

Impeccable. Food was excellent; the sauces and dips are to die for giving a completely different character to the dishes. The airy dining room filled with contemporary art objects creates a relaxed vibe to further enjoy the tasty food.

Professorn is a cozy sports bar located inside the Lappis student housing area. The food is great, the staff are friendly, and it’s a perfect spot to grab a beer or drink with friends. Fridays feature a fun karaoke night, making it a lively place to hang out and relax. Overall, a great spot for students to chill and enjoy good vibes.
Very good restaurant and very good vibe during Fridays and weekends

Sorry for not taking a picture of pizza, it was too good to wait. I like how you can chose different pizza in various price categories, how fast it is being prepared and how they have coleslaw standing for everybody to grab as much as they want. Cheap beer and always same taste, the one you expect from being there long time ago. Definitely go there if you are in the neighborhood.
Super chill place for pizzas and drinks. Located just next to the student housing. Very budget friendly and the staff is really great!

Really nice and cozy bar in Lappis. Prices are from 40-100 SEK for most meals and beers from 35 (and they give complementary salads for all meals!). The waiters are super friendly and the ambience is pretty nice. There's usually sports playing on the TV screens and music plays outside. They let you use the bathroom for free too so yay. Super recommend

We visited the Retro on two nights during our stay in Sweden and both times we were welcomed lovely by everyone and especially “Bella” both nights her service was exceptional and always catered to us. The food was 10/10 i definitely recommend trying a burger, very tasty and probably the best burger i have ever had! The selection of Sports to watch was amazing and we couldn’t have found a better place. Thank you so much and hope to see you again in the future!

Real ahols, I didn't have ONE beer before I got there. The girls in the bar refused to serve me because I according to them was drunk! Haha. I've had two beer with a beautiful smile next door.
I recommend hirschenkeller.

Great place to eat, a lovely atmosphere with all of the sports and great people. Food was 10/10 and the staff were attentive and helpful.

We definitely will be back!

Tack så mycket

Lively, but oh my gosh, service is atrocious 😅 Waitresses making eye contact and simply turning away and ignoring you for 15-20 minutes. The bartenders are lovely though, and all in all, it’s a decent place.
Three stars just because the food is very good, but it would be one star for the service and the behavior of the maids

We were there quite early in the day to watch the Olympics, so it’s hard to judge their general vibe as a sports bar. We had a nice time, the halloumi burger was really nice for a «bar and restaurant»-kind of place. Generous servering of fries! Lots of screens to watch sports on. Nice staff.

After spending the late morning in a bookshop, I found myself hungry and visited right as they were opening at 3pm. They were accommodating to the fact that I can at an early time when people don’t typically eat lunch. The burger was solid, the place was clean, and the staff were polite and friendly. It had a chill vibe, with a sports theme and English quotes around the whole place. The food came fairly quickly, and I was able to get in and out without much delay.

We watched EuroBasket Semi Final there. There are more than 6-7 screen and each of them could be seen for different events. We requested them to open EuroBasket. Staff was helpful. Thanks ! Cocktails were ok; I would prefer beer next time.

Barmaid refused to fill a beer to the 400ml mark

Apparently “had a staff meeting this morning and was told 3/4 full is the correct method”
Nice sport bar with good beer for good price near Stockholm centre. They served the beer to freezed glasses ;-)

name: Riche

googleReviews.text:
We passed from the restaurant and we decided to take a seat outside. They informed us that they only accept dogs inside, so we sat on the tables inside.
As an overall experience, was good, nothing wow.

We were shocked by the toilets, as they were nasty and dirty and not suitable at all for a restaurant like this.

Nice atmosphere, good Swedish restaurant. Every dish we ordered is good. The steak, chicken, fish( artic char), and the meat balls. Even salad and potatoes are quite delicious. Highly recommended. And make a reservation if you can as it seems to be popular and busy during peak times.
One of my most favorite Swedish restaurants in stockholm. Very lucky enough to get the seats without reservation. Good place to try Swedish meatballs.
Pleasant Restaurant. Service was very good. The Meatballs were excellent. Came back a second time . Fun ,friendly staff. Lively around Happy hour.

This was a bit of special treat as it came with a premium price tag. However, I would say it wasn't worth it. I ordered a lobster which sadly was not cooked fresh, it was still cold as if it came straight out of the fridge. I also ordered meatballs which were okay but I had better in Södermalm only a couple days earlier at half the price. Having said all of this, the worst part was wine and service around it. Admittedly, I ordered a house red with my meatballs and it was overly sweet and spicy almost like drink a strange Christmas drink. I asked a waiter to talk me through some of the options on the menu and he knew absolutely nothing about any of the wines they had or how to make a suggestion for a decent pairing. Slightly disappointing at this price I have to say. The rest of services felt quite evasive almost like I had to keep calling the waiter to the table especially when I was done eating and ready to pay. It would have been nice if they asked me how my food was and if I was finished eating so they could collect the plates and glasses from the table.

googleEditorialSummary: Hip bistro with a Swedish/French menu & a quirky decor of bold artworks & chandeliers.

googleReviews.text: Delicious food. Great service (the waitresses are so cool!!!). And cozy atmosphere. Nothing to complain about. Oh and best nduja pizza I've had!
This is my go to from now on in Stockholm. Been here two nights, the food is amazing! The baked char is a must have! The staff is very nice and helpful.

We visited this Italian restaurant last night as a group of five.

Unfortunately, we were not satisfied with the behavior of one of the female staff members. She seemed irritated and unhappy, slammed items on the table, looked annoyed, and was not warm or welcoming at all. This really affected the overall experience.

We ordered a lemon and peach sparkling mocktail, but the taste was extremely sharp. The peach flavor was barely noticeable, and it honestly felt like bottled lemon juice rather than fresh lemon.

All five of us left a significant portion of our meals unfinished. While the food quality itself wasn’t bad, it was extremely heavy. All of us felt uncomfortably full, and even the next morning our stomachs still felt heavy.

The restaurant itself is beautiful, but based on this experience, none of us felt interested in giving it another chance. This isn’t just my personal opinion — all five of us reached the same conclusion.

Food good, the staff careless and the waiter (Woman) kind of bad mood and rude, to not say another word ugly. The girl that bring the food was very friendly, for her I will like to give 5 start. To be fair probably the restaurant full. They need to fix a few tables, the restaurant is pretty by the way. The restaurant was more friendly before. It was a poor welcome and despised goodbye. The presentation of the menu was poor too. It was more like choose something and then she left. What i can say.

We had nice dinner here, food was good, nothing special. Pizza was bit dry and tasteless. Steak was cooked perfectly. It was quite busy, but didn’t have to wait for service. Good value for the money 👍
Great food and great host Oskar. Bartender had funny jokes. Would recommend for all! Beer is good price

It's a good place to hangout after work but food is nothing special while price is quite expensive. I ordered Steak Minute (Ryggbiff). Taste was ok but sauce was not WOW for me. I expected more than this if I need to pay more than 350 KR with steak.   Cocktail is more expensive than other bars in the same area too so I didn't order it even I'm a cocktail lover. Beer and other drinks are better option.
One of my favorite restaurants in downtown Stockholm. The food and drinks are great and the interior is classy. Their outdoor seatings are excellent for people watching during the summer.

This is one of my regular places to go. But lately, the quality has gone down drastically. Me and my partner were there yesterday, and the waiter said that the menu was new from that day and that the staff in the kitchen wasn't used to it. But the menu looked exactly the same and nothing special with it at all. We ordered the burgers because we were in a hurry, and they were probably the most boring burger I've ever had in a proper restaurant.
We then ordered dessert and chose the chocolate fondant. It didn't taste ANYTHING at all (!). Not chocolate and not even sugar. We've also tried the macaroons before and it seems like they buy ready made dessert from somewhere else?! This cannot possibly be made by a chef.
During the night we both got food poisoning.

The food was delicious and the drinks were great. The outside seating was amazing when the sun comes out. Toilets were clean and private. Outside seating is perfect around 1500 when the sun comes out around the building across the way.

Really great bakery! I tried the strawberry cream croissant, salted vanilla bun, and cinnamon bun.

For the strawberry, I wish there was a way for the cream to not just be in the middle so you get it with every bite. The vanilla bun was very flavorful, but a bit too sweet in one sitting, and the cinnamon bun was absolutely perfect.

The employees were super helpful, patient, and friendly. You could tell they enjoy working there and getting to know their customers. They had both indoor and outdoor seating, both of which were well kept.

First time here and got lucky with semla (which is usually only made during Easter), but they're doing it especially for Cinnamon Bun Day (October 4th) tomorrow. Divine. SEK142 for a semla + punschrulle aka dammsugare + black coffee.

The service was great as well. Super friendly and patient, with great recommendations. Would definitely come again 🥰

A bakery that will be missed. Came twice on a 3 night trip. Options might be limited but everything I ate absolutely banged. Not tooo sweet, literally the perfect amount of sweetness. I’d recommend everything. As a tourist I’d definitely recommend visiting.
10/10 on the alimeter.

I went here for sandwiches for three days last week after my morning jog with my son.
I still miss the sandwiches I had for breakfast.
The sweetbreads are the best in Stockholm, and the staff's friendly greetings and smiles are a warm memory.

A lil cozy bakery with a 4 small tables.
We passed by in the evening and were impressed by the view of the baker working the dough. We went to try the buns the next morning - I think the dough could be fluffier and the coffee has nothing special but the place is so cozy. We would go back :)
Came at 2pm but not much choices except semla. Just a really normal semla. There are many other places offer better fika and coffee.

Excellent café. Service is quick and effective. Pastries are top-notch. Soup of the day is pretty good. The only slight concern is that it may be a noisy as it is very popular place. It is close to a subway station very difficult to park so taking a car is not recommended.
It is very calm and cosy! The perfect place to enjoy a cup of coffee and a  tasty pastry!  It is a bit more expensive than the other coffee shops but it is worth the try👌
Super cozy cafe with good selection of food and sweets. tried their soup lunch a couple of times which is really good. Recommend a visit!

It's smells so good when you go in!
Very cozy and calm

Nice staff and cozy authentic italian vibe. There was only a few other customers when we visited on a Sunday lunchtime. Whilst the garlic bread was amazing, and service was fast, the mains were not so good. The pizza dough didn’t seem very fresh and wasn’t very tasty. And the tomato pasta was unusual, and not what we expected, we think there was perhaps a cheese in the sauce, giving it a strange texture and unusual taste, which we didn’t think worked very well.

Amazing carbonara! The atmosphere of the place is beautiful and service was quite good. I thought the prices were okay for the location.

I overall recommend it if you feel like eating good Italian food while in Sweden.

Ordered the lasagna and I’m so happy I did. The sauce was delicious and pasta is made fresh daily. Ingredients are all fresh, food was fantastic, service was quick and friendly. Garlic bread was a nice touch too. Highly recommend.

If you’re looking for a true taste of Italy, this restaurant is the place to be! From the moment you step inside, you’re transported to Italy with its beautifully designed décor—rustic wooden tables, warm lighting, and charming details that make you feel like you’re dining in a cozy trattoria in Italy.

The highlight, of course, is the pizza. Perfectly thin and crispy crust, fresh, high-quality ingredients, and just the right balance of flavors.

The service is warm and welcoming, making the whole experience even better. Whether you’re on a romantic date or out with friends, this place delivers both in atmosphere and taste. Highly recommend!

Comment #1 => A very AVERAGE Food (Pasta 🍝 were OVERCOOKED). Kitchen is definitely NOT from Italy 🇮🇹 ?! It’s definitely TOO EXPENSIVE for what it is + the Staff is ABSOLUTELY DESPICABLE 😱 => Pass on immediately !

Comment #2 [In reply to your response] => Being "happy on the spot" does NOT invalidate a VERY DISAPPOINTING experience. Like many customers, WE chose Politeness + Discretion during service rather than confrontation in front of staff and other guests. That does not remove our right to give honest feedback afterward.

Saying that Pasta cannot be OVERCOOKED because you decided so is NOT an argument. The fact that a dish was eaten does NOT mean it was good ; especially when dining with children and no alternative was reasonably offered (let me also point out that we waited over 30 minutes for our main course, with fewer than 15 guests in the restaurant..).

Warning parents is one thing ; doing so in a judgmental and dismissive tone is another. Regarding our child : not only was he quiet, he caused no disturbance (he briefly walked between the tables for a few seconds, taking up virtually no space at all..) and interacted with other guests with smiles that were clearly well received (adding a touch of good mood, something that could hardly be considered inappropriate in a restaurant..). Mentioning safety is understandable, but the tone used was judgmental and lacked empathy. Parents do not need lessons or to be lectured on "being adults". Respect and understanding toward families are basic expectations in hospitality. Your comment just proved entirely my point !

This review was not emotional or exaggerated ; it reflects a REAL experience : UNFRIENDLY Attitude, POOR Understanding of Parents with young children, and food that was (at best..) LESS than AVERAGE. Your response only reinforces that criticism rather than addressing it.

We went in the evening when it was a bit less crowded - and happily so! ☺️

Really cozy atmosphere and plenty of choices, whether you’re just after a snack or looking to fill your belly to the max.

The shrimp sandwich (aka räkmacka) was hands-down one of the best I’ve had in Stockholm. Also, very reasonable in price.

Bonus points: they’ve got a happy hour deal to save a few bucks on beer, while the sparkling wine is always shine (..in price) 🥂

It looks so beautiful and cosy from the outside, we loved our food and our lunch was good value for money.

We had the fish special, arctic char and the saffron fish and prawn soup. Very tasty.

The interior inside could be better and the service could be slightly better too, however I'm happy with our visit.

A cozy place to have a lunch/dinner! 🙂

The service is pleasant, the staff suggests plates basing on your wishes and tastes.
The food is cooked of high-quality products, main course was served within 15 minutes.
Free bottle of water was provided immediately.

By the end of the dinner, when paying by card, you can choose the amount of tips to be left.

Adding some photos of my evening including recent January’2025 menu ☺️

Recommend this place to anyone willing to enjoy food in the city center.

We ate at this restaurant and I can say that the staff is very nice, cozy atmosphere where you can sit inside or outside, great food, they give you a bottle of water for free. We tasted fish and chips and kotbullar. Both dishes are perfectly prepared. If you like more traditional home food, I suggest you try kotbullar. These are traditional Swedish meet balls with potatoes, sauce and a little pickled cucumbers. Really very tasty and extremely filling. Fish and Chips, perfectly made and delicious. I really liked the sauce with them.

Delicious food, friendly, helpful staff and a lovely vibe. We had a selection of Swedish starters (shrimps, herring, roe and blinis) and then the meatballs. All were delicious and the wine wasn’t too expensive. Would definitely go back.
Very nice bar nd great selection of tapped beer. Different menu  fun nd tasty. Did not know thst goatcheese icecream could taste so good. Brilliant place with great staff.

A cool hidden bar off one of the main streets in the middle of Stockholm.

We stumbled across this place for a wee drink before eating dinner and thought that the atmosphere was just right for us; intimate, casual, yet quirky.

The beer selection was fab, including at least 8 craft beers on tap. The service was friendly with some thrown in humour.

Definitely coming back again and it looked like they had a DJ set for potential beats in the evening!

This bar despite being hidden on a side street stands out as my favourite in Östermalm. The cool quirky vibe is complemented by great food, and a quality range of beers curated by knowledgeable and friendly staff. Can’t recommend enough!
Great selection of beers and food. Define place for a beer lover

👋😃 Welcome to Stockholms best bar! They beat "Crum Heaven" for the 1st place since they have a legendary chef.

This is a cozy smaller place where your after-work transitions into a luxurious dinner 🍽🍒🧀🫛🥩
Right now they rival "Tehran Grill" in the saffran culinary experience -league. (⚠️ Menu may change quickly)

Expect to pay accordingly with the regional high prices 🎀
Rosa Bar makes up for it by delivering an S-tier experience & beer 🍻
Your non-beer friends may enjoy wine, ginger mule or non-alcoholic alternatives 🍷🥂

Godlike staff ❤️‍🔥

😊🎀 Welcome! ✨️🙏
Its not my best pizza but the place is nice and clean.
Very friendly owner, nice and clean place, and love the chicken kebab here. Definitely worth coming.
It was the most delicious pizza I have ever eaten.  The staff was very friendly, the design and comfort of the place was very nice.
Not only meals are perfect but also employees are so kind to their customers.  I will come there again. Everyone should try this place.
Absolutely happy about family size pizza. Thanks for friendly and cozy atmosphere!

A lovely cafe in a lovely setting just a short walk from the tram and the major museums.
Food is simple, and the menu quite short but it’s all super fresh and very tasty.
We had the soup and bread, and then raided the table adorned with pastries and cakes.
Order at the counter, choose a table and then wait for the buzzer to go off.
When you’re done, work off the calories with a short walk back through the gardens and trees to the tram, the museums, or a longer walk back along the waterfront to town.
A great Stockholm lunch venue.

Rosendals appears to be part farm and part cafe, it was very crowded when I was there and the staff seemed somewhat overwhelmed. It was so hectic, in fact, that I did not get pictures of meal.

It was, however, delicious! I ended up getting a vegetarian meal that came with delicious bread. You could tell how fresh the food was and I could have easily tried everything on the menu. I will definitely be back at some point in the future. Do not miss this place!

Rodendals Cafe was one of the best experiences in Stockholm. After long day of museums and Skansen walking, it was peaceful and nice to sit and enjoy delicious food in romantic atmoshpere.

The place looks really fairy and keep in mind that there were lot of people and had to wait for some time. There is self service, so you take whatever you like and pay at the desk. Outdoor seating was almost full, but you can find a spot inside.

I tried famous carrot cake and cheese sandwich. Carrot cake was one of the best, soft and moisture. The cream cheese frosting also tasted very balanced.

Please know that you have to clean after you and respect the place.
Delicious sandwiches, cakes and pastry served in a  beautiful garden. Lunch is also available, it looked super fresh and tasty but we didn't try it.

Delightful and delicious experience, an escape that feels out of time and away from the city.

Made sure to support the project by splurging on a few items from the garden’s shop.

Also loved the recycling policy.
What a pleasure to discover spots like these!

name: Rosetta's
openingHours: Mo closed; Tu-Th 17:00-23:00; Fr-Sa 16:00-01:00; Su closed

googleReviews.text: Fantastic food and atmosphere! Nearly everything on the menu is vegan, except for the mussels. We tried the burger, tacos and death by chocolate they were delicious . The fries were a standout! perfectly crispy with a spice mix that’s absolutely addictive. The service was warm and attentive. Can’t wait to come back!

Lovely place with a very cozy atmosphere. Nice and friendly staff.

The vegetarian Burger was my first ever mushroom based Burger and it was phenomenal!

The Garlic bread was crunchy and fluffy, the Cauliflower Mac and cheese was nice.

I very much recommend trying the shroomburger, especially if you're interested in trying vegetarian foods. One of the best vegetarian burgers I ever tried.

The mushroom burgers were an absolute dream, highly recommended; especially if you're not a fan of mushrooms but want to try something new. Easily the best burger I've had for years.

Nice selection of drinks and smaller dishes was well. I'm going back soon to try some other items on the menu and will update this review accordingly. The first impression though is VERY GOOD.
One of the best places in Stockholm. All vegan and pescitarian. Food is great. Atmosphere and the patrons are all up my alley. And the prices are reasonable.

Perfect place for a chill night out as well as for partying with friends.
Food is great, creative and cooked with love.
We love this place.

Nice place for a good coffee and breakfast. Lots of space inside and outside. A lot of the table have outlets to charge laptops. Friendly service; nice selection of drinks and food. The coffee was great and the breakfast sandwiches delicious. Really nice sweets and snacks.

Espresso was good. Service poor. I was ignored at the cashier literally for first 3 min by the barista, who was sitting behind me at the table with other people. The coffee shop was empty and when he took my ordered he was not very subtle to show me that making coffee was not what he wanted to do at the moment. That made the espresso bitter, but not the way it is supposed to be bitter.

Excellent coffee,  modern and cosy environment,  nice, big windows,  kind service.
Thank you!

I came in around 11 and there were 2-3 people in a pretty large space. After I ordered the barista told me I can't work there between the hours of 11-1. I would understand that if they were at all busy, but the place was nearly empty. It felt unnecessarily cold and rude.
The food is really nicecsnd the coffee also. The chefs are really nice and the atmosphere is very good. The place is a bit small but very cozy. I highly recommend eating at that cafe/restauraunt.
Decent lunch place with pretty good pasta, but maybe tone down the garlic a little bit, as my dish was close to inedible due to the massive amount of raw garlic and I normally like garlic.

I had lunch here recently, and I must say the service was excellent – the staff was very friendly and attentive. I ordered pasta with chicken and sun-dried tomatoes, but unfortunately, the dish didn’t meet my expectations. The pasta was so hard that it felt undercooked. I know what al dente should taste like, but this was borderline undercooked. Additionally, there were only two small pieces of chicken and two sun-dried tomatoes, so the dish was mostly just pasta. For the price I paid, I expected much more. The price felt far too high for what I got on the plate.

We had one of best experiences with my colleagues at this restaurant. Special thanks for the warm service goes to Cicero, whom made our visit special! Food was delicious, restaurant was cozy and beautiful!
We can recommend this place 100%!

The seafood pasta was pretty good, the tomatoes enhanced the flavors of seafood, and the pasta was boiled in a good way, not too soft not too hard.
However the service was a bit off. We received bread basket without being told that it costed extra charge. We didn’t know that we needed to pay for the bread until we got the bill.
Rustico Ristorante offers an authentic Italian dining experience. The pasta was exceptional, and the service was spot on. Cozy atmosphere I’ve had. Also friendly with kid. Highly recommend!

We went here for a spontanous late lunch (2 persons/no reservation) and had a wunderful time.
Romantic table with candlelight and flowers, friendly stuff.
The side salads were not that good, but the kotbullar and the entrecot (medium rare) were absolutely fantastic! also the french fries were great.
Fully recommend this place.

Restaurant Review: Rydberg – Stockholm
Visited this Swedish restaurant with high hopes. The interior is charming and cozy, but sadly, the experience didn’t live up to expectations.

We had Plankstek with mashed potatoes and red wine sauce. The mashed potatoes were the best part—smooth and tasty. However, the meat portion was tiny, overcooked, and the red wine sauce was barely there.

Service was a mixed bag: the waiter who showed us to the table was friendly and professional, but the one who served our food was careless and inattentive.

At 350 SEK per person, I expected more. I've had much better food for less.

Rating: 2/5 – Not worth the price.
The weekday lunch menu is insanely delicious and for an unbelievable price. Amazing experience! I am still dreaming about the souce and the salad bar. Thank you!

We made a reservation here for a New Year's dinner. We based the reservation and the place on good reviews and we were excited to try their signature dish "Biff Rydberg". The service started off really shaky and it really looked like the staff was not ready to take on customers. After a change of waiter it got better and the drinks came out as well as the food, on time, warm and well-cooked. The Biff Rydberg was a little bit of a disappointment based on that this is their signature dish.... The meat was tender but it felt simple and the presentation was poor. The fish and the pasta was good. Overall an okay experience.

I came whilst visiting Stockholm. I admit it was quiet but saw they had meatballs. They were excellent the staff really looked after me and made me feel welcome. The food was excellent and i would definatly come back again

The restaurant has an authentic vibe, with an atmosphere that’s vibrant and energetic. The schnitzel was cooked perfectly and without any unpleasant smell, but the warm wine sauce didn’t feel like a good match for the dish.

The live rock blues music was on another level it truly made the evening special. We left with full stomachs, happy hearts, and a little rhythm in our steps!

We wanted to grab a quick bite to eat before a concert and spontaneously ended up at St:a Clara. Our food was served quickly and it was delicious! I had the breaded cod and a non-alcoholic Eriksberg beer. The place looks like an English pub and has German and Swedish cuisine. I would love to go there again.

Came for dinner, had to wait a little for a table but we’re seated in the bar area. Service was fine but felt slightly pressured. The potato pancake and schnitzel were lovely and tasty. Good if what a quick casual meal but if you want an experience go elsewhere.

Easy five star review!
Cozy little pub with good atmosphere and great food. Massive servings, well worth the price.
I had the pork shank which fell off the bone with delicious crackling. Wife had rolled schnitzel and it did not disappoint.

Dropped into this place for Friday Blues jam. There is a basement downstairs where they have live music. It's very small and gets pretty crowded but the atmosphere and music is top notch. Good friendly bar service.

I had a great experience at Saqi Persian Restaurant. I ordered the Chenjeh kebab, and they actually asked how I wanted it cooked, which I really appreciated. I chose medium, and it was cooked exactly as requested. The meat was incredibly tender and juicy — it literally melted in my mouth.

The staff member who took our order was extremely polite, warm, and friendly, which made the experience even better. If you’re looking for truly delicious kebab, I highly recommend ordering Barg or Chenjeh.

We also tried the Joojeh Kebab, which was made with thigh fillet meat, and it was very flavorful and well-cooked. On top of all that, the atmosphere was beautiful and pleasant.
One of best quality (and presentation) Persian foods I’ve had combined with amazing service and friendly owners. Highly recommend.

The food was perfectly cooked. A lot of variety, hard to choose from, but everything we got was tasty, the meat was cooked to perfection. Loved it. Will definitely come back.
Friendly stuff and very helpful.
Nice interior decoration, very friendly staff. Food was delicious and high quality. Doogh was top notch ;). One of the best Iranian restaurants in Stockholm so far.

We had a fantastic meal at Saaghi Grill! The kebab-e-barg was exceptionally juicy and tender—rich in flavor and grilled to perfection. Paired with aromatic saffron rice and a side of mast-o-doogh (yogurt drink), the experience truly brought the taste of Persia to the table. Highly recommend for anyone craving authentic Persian cuisine!

Really nice Thai restaurant in the town. Small, cozy, with decorated indoors. The service is good and the menu is rich with quite a few vegetarian Tofu options..

The food was really flavorful, with good quality and nice presentation. I love the fact that they make it mild and you get the chilly and nuts seperate so you can adjust the amount yourself.

4 stars only because it's slightly expensive by 15 to 20%.. But everything else is 5 stars.. For some dishes, portion sizes were small.. But we liked the fact that some came with Rice bowl separately..

Really nice thai restaurant. Small, cozy, with beautiful decoration. The service is good and the menu is rich.
The food was really tasty, with good quality and nice presentation. I love the fact that they make it mild and you get the chilly and nuts seperate so you can adjust the amount yourself.
I would give it 5 stars if it was either a bit cheaper or a bit bigger portions and if every curry dish came with each own rice bowl.
Baqer, the waiter, served us so good. The food was incredible and the place so chill. Definitely recommended. We asked for pad thai, spring rolls and chicken with peanut sauce

Great food, very tasty, a little spicy and delicious!
Quick and nice service!
Good food, friendly staff and fast service. Recommended👌

Saffran is a cozy little gem that delivers big on flavor. The food is fragrant, well-seasoned, and comforting — every dish feels thoughtfully prepared with fresh ingredients. The portions are generous without being overwhelming, and the balance of spices is spot on. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, making it a great spot for both a casual meal and a relaxed evening out. The staff are friendly and attentive, which really adds to the overall experience. A place you’ll want to come back to again and again.

The place is very welcoming & we were offered the menu in English which helped us a lot... 😆
The food was excellent & obviously prepared with love - it was so tasty and fresh. Really cosy & comfortable atmosphere. Highly recommend & would come back anytime we are back over this side of the world! 🌎

Good food and nice ambiance overall :)
We had some vegetarian and non vegetarian options, both of which were nice.

I wouldn’t say it’s the best Indian restaurant in town, but it could also be that our orders weren’t a specialty.

Not bad not good place, suitable for a casual dinner if you are in the neighborhood.
The chicken bites were too dry in the curry sauce. The Paratha was a bit lack of texture to my taste, the dough didn’t separate in enough layers. But the Chai was to my liking, very milky and in combination of many spices, drink it without sugar can bring up the aroma even more.
Great food and very friendly staff. Highly recommended!

I had an absolutely fantastic experience at this restaurant in Stockholm! From the moment we arrived, the atmosphere was warm, cozy, and incredibly welcoming. The staff greeted us with genuine smiles and made us feel right at home.

The food was simply outstanding. Each dish was beautifully presented and bursting with flavor—clearly prepared with care and top-quality ingredients. The balance of traditional Swedish flavors with modern twists was impressive, and every bite was a delight.

The service was attentive without being intrusive, and the team went above and beyond to make our evening special. The overall ambiance was charming, perfect for a relaxed dinner or a special occasion.

I can honestly say this is one of the best dining experiences I’ve had in Stockholm. I’ll definitely be coming back and recommending it to everyone I know. Highly, highly recommended! 🍽️✨

A beautiful and cozy Italian restaurant with a lovely atmosphere. The service was friendly, polite, and very welcoming. The food was delicious and clearly made with care. It’s not cheap, but the portions are generous and very filling, so it definitely feels worth it. A great place to enjoy a satisfying meal.

I had a fantastic experience here! From the moment I walked in, the staff (Michael and Remo) were welcoming, attentive, and professional. The service was quick but never rushed, and everyone was genuinely friendly, which made the atmosphere even more enjoyable.

The food was equally impressive – fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. Every dish we ordered was cooked perfectly and tasted amazing. It’s clear that they put care and effort into both their ingredients and their preparation.

Highly recommend this place for anyone looking for great food paired with top-notch service. I’ll definitely be coming back!

Great pizza, loved the cafona I had and the rest of my company seemed to enjoy their carbonara and gamberetti as well.

Beverage menu is a disappointment in the non-alcoholic department; water, Pepsi and Pepsi Max is the extent of it.

The waiter we had didn't seem like he enjoyed his job.

Had a fantastic dinner at the Sallys.
Not only all the meals were scrumptious, but also the waiter Michel was absolutely fantastic,  even we were at the basement to ourselves, he kept coming back and we had a great hospitality from him.
I would 200% recommend.
Lovely warm yummy restaurant in Stockholm.

The best soup I’ve ever had before!!!!! I’ve been Thailand for couple times and this is exactly the flavor I’m expecting. Also, for the basil pork is good as well💗💗💗 the service is pretty good, thanks for providing a good night😚😚
Very few items on the menu, which I see as a sign that they know what they're doing. Great no-frills Thai classics, love it!

Seriously good food. The Thai curry is perfectly balanced in spice and heat, everything is perfectly cooked.

The beef also has no business being this good for this price.
Nice and cosy restaurant in kungs… the food and service was really good!
The food missed then fragrance I would expect in Thai dishes
one of my favorite place for a good espresso coffe and Sam really is good in what he does! He gives water near the coffe ( no one does it anymore these days)
Great coffee and great conversation with the owner, Sam and his friend Peter. It’s a small, hole-in-the-wall coffee shop near Skinnarviksberget. Worth your time and money.
Maybe the best espresso in Stockholm? In any case, the best I have drunk here. The owner is super friendly and you feel welcome.

A very local espresso bar with a very friendly owner. Everyone is welcome and Sam makes you feel like you're part of the family.
The focus is on coffee but Sam serves lunches aswell, cooked by himself in the little kitchen in the back.
This is the kind of place where most people are on a first name basis with each other, well at least with Sam.
Little gem on Hornsgatan! Cosy atmosphere, lovely owner, beautiful coffee. Highly recommend :)
Best pizza I've ever had! A fantastic Negroni and great friendly service.  Don't miss it if your in Stockhom. Right by a metro station so no excuse!

We found our way here after arriving in Stockholm an hour before. Really good food, the diavola was exceptional. Great dough that both gave a crunch and a chew, as it should be. I enjoyed the addition of parmagiano, it gave the pizza an added dimension.

The highlight were the staff. Warm, attentive without being in your face and so friendly. Would absolutely return again.

Had a great dinner at Savoca. Apparently, they just opened since a few weeks, and quality is already on point. Fettuccine Savoca was delicious, tiramisu was the same, very very good. Staff taking their time to talk to their customers, very attentive and friendly. Recommend this place! 10/10
Newly open spot in prime location, we will definitely be back because of the food, vibe and SERVICE! Lucas brought our night to the next level #maskin
This a review of the Merluzzo….fantastic!  What a treat. The freshest cod prepared so as to allow it to shine.  So good!

We went to Savoj because of the Kebab Pizza on Sundays and where not disappointed! Really good neapolitan pizza and as someone from the Kebab capital Berlin I approve of the kebab part aswell, no diet food tho :D

The outdoor seating area is lovely, especially during the summer evenings. Eating a fresh Margherita pizza under the fairy lights with a cold beer in hand was the highlight of my week. I’ll definitely be coming back soon.

The portions are very generous for the price. We ordered a large thinking it would be enough for two, but we ended up having enough leftovers for lunch the next day. It’s great value for such artisanal quality.

I love that they offer a gluten-free crust that actually tastes like real bread. Usually, I feel left out at pizza parties, but their GF option is so good that my friends couldn't even tell the difference. Thank you for being so inclusive!

You can really taste the quality of the wood-fired oven in every bite. That slight char on the edges of the crust gives it a smoky flavor that you just can't get from a standard electric oven. It’s pure perfection.

The food is fantastic!! The vegan one is amazingggggg, slightly spicy for those who hate spice, but it was perfect for me.

Huge long tables for big groups and also small tables for two, nice ambiance and kind of student-like but enjoyable and clean!

Service was lovely, I was the only one there so it was quiet and they brought my food to me :) Water is already on the table filled up and the staff was kind.

Came here with my wife late in the evening. We were really starving and looking for a good pace to have our dinner and we stumbled on this place.

This is a small place with very cozy setup. The staffs are super friendly. They helped us explain about the menu and what to expect from the dish were were going to order. We ordered a chicken and veg sandwich and a portion of chips.

Hands down this was one of the most amazing sandwiches we have tasted. We it had just one piece of bread with loaded stuffing and sauces. The bread was crispy and chewy at the same time. And the fillings had an amazing taste to it.

If you are in Stockholm, I would definitely suggest this place for you. Please do check it out.

Sandwiches here are like an amusement park! full of layers, textures, and flavors. I’ve tried the chicken and Miso min miso so far and preferred the chicken, though I wished the fried chicken was less greasy. The vegetarian option has a large piece of grilled cabbage, which was also greasy and not to my taste, but if you love cabbage, go for it.

The atmosphere is lively, and despite Stockholm’s usually cozy and calm restaurants, Scandwich makes you feel young and energetic! The staff are wonderful, and this will definitely be our go-to spot.

I think it's suppose to be backpackers/students place, cheap food and cheap beer and cheap wines. The food is good but its a lot of oil/butter in it and you will feel it from the first taste. It would be better if tables are more clean.

Went for a drink and ended up having a very tasty diner : the meat sandwich. Top notch pork sandwich- hardly recognizable as such. Very friendly staff and also beverage prices. Busy and popular it seams-no wonder!

name: Scarpetta by Ingrosso

googleReviews.text:
I had quite high expectations of this restaurant. The menu was limited, but the choices were very appealing. And they really delivered!!

As a vegetarian I was happy to see that there were two meat-free options. They were delicious and well-presented. I left the restaurant fully satisfied.

It’s too expensive to eat there every day, however for the quality of the food the prices could be even higher. So yes, good value for money!

I wish I could add an extra star to my ratings.

Me and my sister have celiaki and we hade two different glutenfree pasta meals (CARBONARA DI LUSSO and SEDANI DEL VECCHIO CONTE (oxfilé) ) and they were delicious. We also hade two different glutenfree desserts and they were also  very nice. We sat outside in october but the area was within glass and with heating so it was really nice. Friendly and polite staff.

A truly beautiful Italian restaurant, just as delightful to look at as it is to taste. We were a party of six and everyone thoroughly enjoyed their meal. The dishes were generous, full of flavor, and beautifully presented.
A wonderful surprise from start to finish, both in terms of the quality of the food and the atmosphere.
We will definitely be back!

We were in Stockholm recently and made a reservation here for our first dinner! And it didn't disappoint!!
Everything was absolutely amazing, from the food, ambience, lovely and friendly staff, just nothing to fault!
We felt the courses were coming out perfectly timed giving us time to digest the previous but no rush!
5 start restaurant!
Good Italian restaurant in Stockholm city centre. Warm atmosphere, very good pasta.

googleEditorialSummary: Traditional Italian & Mediterranean dishes, plus cocktails, in an elegant, plant-filled setting.

googleReviews.text: Its been a while since I have been to such an amazing place. The vibes are cozy. The location is kinda small so limited seating areas but it is worth the wait. We got the famous sandwich with chicken soup and the steak pie. I highly recommend to try the lemonade with olives.

Me and two friends had dinner here in September, sat outside, nice weather and atmosphere.
The guy that served us was highly knowlagble on sandwiches, specially the Ruben, so the service was on point.
The foo was great to, probably the best ruben i have had in sweden.  they had alot of other food as well.
Highly recomended

The food was very good. We ordered the Reuben sandwich and really enjoyed the pastrami, which was flavorful and well-prepared. The place is small but cozy, with a warm atmosphere, and the service was attentive and friendly. I would recommend booking in advance, as the restaurant was full and we only had our table inside for 50 minutes.

The food was absolutely delicious – the flavors were spot on! On the downside, the service felt a bit cold and impersonal. The waiter handled the salami that felt of the playe with his hand, we didn’t get a small dish for the finished olives, and nobody checked in to ask how we liked the food or offered any recommendations.

Was looking for a cozy spot for lunch by myself but was instantly turned off, I nearly left before even ordering. Quite possibly the worst service I've had of four years living in Stockholm. Arrogance and uninterested attitude from staff throughout the whole visit. Even when I left to pay, he said nothing, not even "how was your food" "thanks for coming", nothing, like my visit was a massive inconvenience to his day. The whole energy and vibe in the place or lack thereof was a complete and utter downer to the entire visit.

Must say the quality of the food was way above expectations. We had burgers and the fries were heavenly, might be the best fries I've had - very light and crispy! Fast and pleasant service, can really recommend going here.
Very good food. Nice atmosphere. Nice staff and good selection of drinks
Cozy, at-home-livingroom feeling with  an accompanying menu of well chosen items of classic husmanskost. Nice beer assortment and very accommodating staff.
Beer and drinks, seemed to be most local people visiting,  but a nice spot for any  visiting dogs, look at the sign :)
Super nice service - dogs are very welcome inside - and the food was really good - we had a Schnitzel!

This place honestly exceeded our expectations. From the moment the food arrived, you could tell real care had gone into both the flavors and presentation.

The lamb dishes were the real stars for me tender, rich and cooked with depth. Among everything we tried, Markus Indi Korai was hands down my favorite and a must-try. The lamb was beautifully cooked, full of flavor, with fresh, aromatic notes and a spice level that built up perfectly without overpowering the dish.

The creamy chicken dish was a pleasant contrast smooth, mildly sweet with a gentle tang and very comforting. It balanced the spicier dishes on the table really well.
