The sizzling platter was another highlight. It arrived hot and aromatic, with juicy grilled lamb and fresh sides that made the whole dish feel complete and satisfying.

Desserts ended the meal on a high note one rich and traditional, the other light, fruity and refreshing.

Overall, the food had excellent spice balance, the atmosphere was warm and service was top-notch.

If you’re visiting for the first time, Markus Indi Korai is an absolute must-try. I’d happily come back and recommend this place to anyone who enjoys well-cooked, flavorful food.

Really delicious indian food. The portion size could be a bit bigger but this might be just our preference.
A good adress for good food in Stockholm.

Delicious Indian cuisine, taste was very good, perfect blend of spices. We ordered chicken & lamb dishes & both meat was tender & chef customised the spice level for us. Staffs are very friendly from Bangladesh. Veg was also good. Only felt the portions were less compared to price. Atleast the take away boxes should have been full with sauce if not meat pieces. Salad & sauce was given complimentary & it was super good.
Highly recommend for tourists too.

We ordered take away from here On a whim as I love Indian food. All I can say is wow.

I got the korai chicken it was the perfect flavour, the chicken was perfect, the sauce was perfect and the rice was a big portion which I absolutely love.
If your thinking about ordering from here…do it!!!

We tried this place at end of lunch hours. The soup was cold and the food was not tasting good. The lowest quality of indian/bengali food experience I had in Stockholm.
In addition, the staff were not friendly.

A bit too spicy, especially compared to the other Shanti locations in stockholm. Service also was not as friendly and I’d say the other locations have a better ambience inside. Still overall quite tasty and a wide ranging menu.
I'm sorry, but way too much fatty food. It feels like the oil is not enough drained from the food. The people here are very kind and the restaurant had exotic, modern atmosphere that is nice though!

Very atmospheric place. Nice design and dishes look pretty.
Nice place for romantic time, family dinner, fast lunch with colleagues.
Prices are typical for that area.
Great place and delicious food. Perfect when in the mood for a curry lunch! I recommend the paneer with fresh spinach and a naan, because rice portion is small.
2nd time I've seen a Bangladeshi inspired restaurant (usually it's an Indian restaurant run by Bengladeshi's). Food is good but not London quality good. The ambience & decor get 5* though

It was such a perfect romantic night with delicious food! The waiter with the glasses was very nice and welcoming to us, we will come back for sure
I had the lime chicken and garlic nan, I highly recommend it.

Lots of option and deleciius veg options
Best indian food in stockholm
Service is 10/10
We be back

Last couple of years ago with my wife had a delicious lunch and now after 6 years agin here with my son .
Never forget the memory the food and the waiter we shared lot of Memories

Best and superb food & service in Stockholm
Love you as always

We be back again

Good quality and nice restaurant
Portion was really small.
Very nice restaurant, good ambience and very friendly staff! We came for dinner and the staff was very helpful and attentive! Definitely recommend!

Great ambience and quite busy. I had the Sloth, a spicy miso ramen. The broth tasted great, but there were only three tiny pieces of pork — felt like they were skimping on the chashu, which was disappointing. The service was also lacklustre; staff seemed to disappear into the restrooms together for about 15 minutes. I was here a few years ago and it was a lot better then.

Very dimly lit, red light ambience and with a  surprisingly quiet volume despite being lunchtime.

The ramen i ordered was ok, not as great as i was led to believe from other reviews unfortunately. That being said it wasn't bad at all, just missing some key details.

The meat was cold, not fridge cold but slightly colder than room temperature. As the ramens  noodles and broth are both hot this gave it an unpleasant contrast as well as the fat being less smooth than it could have been had it been temped up and matching the broth.

Noodles were great, good consistency and texture. The broth had a brilliant aroma but sadly lacked a bit in taste.

Overall a very small portion size of solids, although there was ample broth.

I have a feeling this was a bad day, or since i was there just after the rush maybe i was experiencing the kitchen catching up after a busy lunch. All in all i was a bit disappointed but I'll give it a new try next time and update.

Cool place for a quick lunch, cozy, small but not noisy, you can easily talk to each other. I took the gluttony ramen, strange combination between pork and fish broth at first, but just a savoury combination instead. I would take it again. Looking forward to try other ramen choices
Guusy place, Great food and personel. Fast service, good taste on all what we had as a group of four persons. Make sense to do a reservation

Very nice and authentic place - good food, nice staff and very nice drinks 🙌
If you want good ramen - this is the place to go!
(sorry for the bad pic)

Listen, you might think this place is only a gimmick, but truth be told, the food is also AMAZING.
While a little pricy, everything about it is great. The workers stay in character, the decoration is great and even the cutlery is nothing like you would expect.
As someone who was visiting Stockholm, I can say that this is the place to bring any visitors or tourists.
What a fantastic expericen

A truly special experience in Stockholm! The medieval ambience at Sjätte Tunnan is unique – vaulted cellars, candlelight and the atmosphere immediately transport you to another era.

However, you should be aware that the food and drinks are rather expensive for the portion sizes and what you get. Nevertheless, we had an exciting evening with a great atmosphere.

Be sure to make a reservation! Without one, you really need luck – we were lucky, but I wouldn't count on it. Highly recommended for anyone looking for an extraordinary restaurant experience.

One of the best restaurant experiences of my life! The medieval themed restaurant was from the moment we entered absolutely amazing.

Entering through an old door down to the old city base-cellar you will find a medieval ambiance which brings you straight to the old days. Candles in stead of light bulbs, medieval dresses from the staff and NO forks (largely absent from common eateries in the middle ages) transcended us way back in the past ages. Nothing felt touristic and they have put a lot of effort and love in their concept.

Regarding the food of course we were sceptical in the beginning but they overcame all our considerations. We had the pork leg and the venison which were unexpectedly on the gourmet side of gastronomy than a peasant dish. Well thought and delicious recipes based on the diet and ingredients of the middle ages.

The staff were absolutely friendly and generous and made the whole experience better.

If you visit the old city of Stockholm do yourself a favor and visit them and believe me you will be rewarded and amazed by this restaurant!

Great medieval restaurant in the center of Stockholm. Ordered two dishes, one with lemon lasagna and the other one I cant remember unfortunately. Both were super tasty. Go there, order one main dish with Mead and then just enjoy the atmosphere ✨

Ps. Careful eyes can notice they dont give you a fork :)

Great experience. Medieval themed restaurant with great food, many variants of mead and staff dressed in medieval garments. The staff was very attentive. We ate beaver, lamb and deer. Their house wine was excellent value for money. Situated in an old celler vault.

A very nice restaurant with a wonderful service, amazing personnel, good wine, we’ll definitely come back and of course it has a wonderful view to the water.  Would recommend to book ahead of time. And enjoy the evening.

wonderful place, amazing view on the sea and delicious food. the girl of the staff was kindly, she gave us a lot of infos about the city. but she forgot to give us her instagram. please tell her to find me😂
Great environment and service! The food choices are somewhat limited for lunch, but everything else makes up for it. Recommended!

Visited as a large group on a weekday evening. Our orders were pre-booked.

🔸️Steak tatare 👍👍👍
Decent quality, classic presentation with mustard, capers, beetroot, onions and raw egg yolk.

🔸️Sirloin steak 👍👍👍👍
A tender, juicy steak done medium. Generous cut of meat with a side of bearnaise sauce. Crispy, yummy lightly salted golden fries.

🔸️Red wine 👍👍👍👍

🔸️Brewed coffee 👍👍👍
Medium brew, nothing special.

We were at least 20 people and were seated at a long table with a window view of the beautiful marina.

Service was overall attentive. The waiters tried their best to keep up with requests but sometimes slip up and forgot some orders.

We came without a reservation and they were quick finding a table for us. The staff was attentive and nice.
We ordered the seafood spaghetti and the mushroom beef pasta. The seafood pasta was great! As for the beef pasta, the beef was cooked perfectly, very tender and not chewy at all. But the cream and the mushroom made it a heavy dish to digest.
The atmosphere was ok, a little too noisy at times, because we were seated right next to the open kitchen.

Next to the train and tram lines at Gullmarsplan. To get there easier, go on to the tram platform and use the exit at the far end, away from the station building. You have to cross the tracks and then turn right.

The food is really good. We went twice while we were in Stockholm. Once on a very busy Friday evening and then the following day when it was much quieter. There is a wide selection of bottled beers. The food arrives quickly and is hot and tasty. For us it was on the way home after a day sightseeing, so we were changing froma train to a tram at the local station. There was plenty of room and it was comfortable. On the Saturday evening it was great to sit and watch the sun go down over Stockholm. Well worth a visit.

It’s a good mix of a simple place (good prices, kind staff and regular good food) with an exotic location, due to the windmill architecture and the water view  with rail ways around. Definitely recommend, specially if you leave in the area. We had fish and chips (179 sek) and good beers with very affordable prices.
What a lovely place in the city!

Very good price for cava and a decent shrimp sandwich.
The crowd is very gray and on the elderly side. But the view is amazing 🤩

It's always nice sitting here in the summer and the staff is always great. It was nice with a DJ playing music. Only downside was that he raised the volume so loud you couldn't talk in the end without having to raise your voice to almost screaming which killed the atmosphere a bit as it's mostly seating area and not dancing place so one of the points with being there is to hang out with other people in a socialising way. And it wasn't even that late.
Music was nice to have around, the place was good, the staff was great, the location was awesome and i will happily recommend it but the volume on music should have kept to where people can talk and mostly hear each other.

Pretty awesome vibe, super yumm bowls. All bowls come with either sweet potato noodles or rice. It was a great bowl and some crunch stuff at the top. Vibe of the place is pretty cool, usually many youngsters.
You could give it a try to try a bowl

Great lunch spot! I had the miso glaze salmon salad and it was delicious and very filling will definitely be back to try more. My friend had the vegan one which was also really good. Nice atmosphere too and quick service

Well-priced, mostly tasty cold salad-style dishes. There is an option for vegans and vegetarians, and several for pescatarians and those who eat chicken. The vegan dish was tasty and filling, but the fish (miso glazed salmon) left something to be desired in terms of texture. Taking lunch in an enjoyable setting was, however, a positive, and the service was helpful in reimbursing an overcharge. All in all, I can recommend.

In the hart of Södermalm you find this gem. The ambience is something between Copenhagen street food and Berlin with cool crew making sure you're seen and food that's killing it with some asian inspiration. definitely worth a visit and its not even expensive and they serve beer and wine to :)

Nice vegetarian salad bowl with tofu and kale and cabbage base. Tasty flavours, but a lot of the same ingredients, so got slightly boring towards the end of the bowl. Service was very nice and friendly.

I visited Skörden restaurant for a simple dinner and was truly amazed by the delicious yet uncomplicated meals. The atmosphere was both cozy and stylish, creating a perfect setting to enjoy the evening. The staff was incredibly friendly and efficient, making the experience even more enjoyable. I had a fantastic time with my friends and family and will definitely be coming back! Thank you for the warm and wonderful welcome!   Desserts are a must try!♥️

The restaurant seemed a little disorganized, I wasn't sure what to do when I arrived.  All you need to do is find a table and sit down.

You order through your phone, scanning a QR on the table.  It was pretty easy.

The food was amazing.  I had a Schnitzel Sandwich that was prepared perfectly, with a delicious sauce.  It came with a nice salad that was also great.

I will go back often.
Wow, this was the best baked potato I have eaten in Sweden. The meat was also delicious. Lunch is a bit pricey, but it's really worth the money.

Best night in Stockholm by far! This restaurant is warm, welcoming and fun. The service is great and the food is beyond amazing (the meatballs... wow). They play live music on Thursday  and the band is awesome, everybody stood up eventually! I strongly recommend it.

It was truly impressive! Entering through a luxurious wine bar was an unexpected twist for a street food place, setting a special tone right from the start. Inside, there was a minimalist vibe, and the menu was simple yet thoughtful, with some great vegetarian options that pleased everyone in our group.

The ordering process was fast and convenient through an app, with cashless payment adding a modern touch. We were served by an incredibly kind waiter who surprised us with complimentary hot tea—a perfect gesture on a chilly day! This place blends an unusual atmosphere with the authenticity of street food, creating a memorable experience that’s sure to stick with us for a long time.

Solid tasty New York style pizza in the heart of Stockholm. Tonnes of cheese, great sauce and great topping options with zero flop! Especially their leek pizza - I think called the dabrowski - highly recommended. But also, their Brooklyn and the flat iron. In fact all were super good. I went 3 times and also got a pie while waiting to leave for the airport and ate it in the hotel lobby. The pizzas are big. The slices are big. And the staff and owners are great! Super chatty and always happy to serve. You also get a free drink if you order 2 or more slices which we did a plenty! This is a hidden gem in the city.

Disappointing

It’s just your typical Swedish pizza with double dough, same blend of cheese and ingredients as any pizzeria one would find in the stockholm suburbs.

Interesting idea and it’s no wonder I haven’t heard anyone talk about this place before

There’s tons of foot traffic and this place was very empty.

Edit: 2nd time was really good. Had the white slice w/ truffle mayo ,,, super good 😋

Great pizza for an American traveling abroad! They make it fresh, it’s got a great dough (thin and a little more of a crunch than typical NY but still great. Great sauce and cheese. Wonderful staff. Go here. You’ll be happy. It’s a quick and inexpensive lunch in Stockholm! Tack!
Wow, I was not expecting to get such amazing NY-style pizza in Stockholm! The space is elevated, with chic decor, great service, and just a fun vibe! Really good pizza 🍕 .

Had the supreme and Brooklyn slice enjoy both. Staff friendly and good service. I thought for this good New York style pizza for price. Nice atmosphere would recommend if fancy a pizza in central Stockholm.

I’ve been to Slingerbulten twice, and if there’s one dish that truly stands out, it’s the Swedish meatballs (köttbullar – 215 SEK). No matter how good the other dishes are, the meatballs are on another level. 🌟🌟 They have that homemade, comforting flavor — like grandma’s cooking. Juicy, full of flavor, served with proper mashed potatoes and lingonberries… it’s the kind of meal that makes you want to slow down and just enjoy it. Cozy and authentic.
We also tried the Toast Skagen (165 SEK) 🍤 and the Stekt strömming (215 SEK) 🐟 from the traditional section of the menu. Both were good and very much in line with classic Swedish home-style food, especially the strömming if you enjoy traditional flavors. But honestly, I would go back and just order a single plate of meatballs again — they were the highlight of the night.
On a previous visit, we also had the salmon and the reindeer sirloin. While the reindeer was especially delicious, the meatballs still win. They simply outperform the rest of the menu.
If you’re looking for traditional Swedish comfort food in Gamla Stan, this place delivers — but don’t skip the köttbullar.

We went to this restaurant for our wedding anniversary and we are pleased with our choice. We tried the arctic char as main course which was great. We also tried toast skogen which is a Swedish classic and theirs was a great version. We also had lingon juice as drink. Overall we are happy that we chose this restaurant. It’s a plus that they are in gamla stan. I also want to add that the staff was quite friendly. Definitely recommend.

I found this place through Google ratings and reviews. Frankly, it's very small. It's perfect for a quick bite and a quick meal. You can't really sit and relax; the atmosphere, tables, and chairs aren't suitable for that anyway. I tried the famous Swedish meatballs. The price includes various salads (actually, there aren't just one type; you get a small plate of lettuce, boiled chickpeas, bulgur salad, olives, and boiled corn kernels as a self-serve meal) and filter coffee. Mashed potatoes and jam are also served on the same plate. As a Turk of Balkan origin, I've eaten the best İnegöl meatballs, ıslama meatballs, and Tire meatballs. I've tried all the meatball restaurants in Sarajevo and Skopje. Unfortunately, compared to those, the Swedish meatballs only become somewhat edible thanks to the sauce and jam on top. In my opinion, the meatballs themselves are very tasteless. Would I eat there again? Only when I go to Ikea :)

We had an excellent meal with Swedish meatballs. We ordered one dish to share because we weren’t very hungry. The portion with meatballs for 215 SEK and cheese was simply divine.

A real must-have if you’re in Sweden, especially during your first days here or if it’s your first time trying local cuisine. This is a great place to stop for a very tasty dinner.

Highly recommended.

A “lagom” place for Swedish cuisine, I’ve been here for both lunch and dinner and the food quality in general is above average.
The deer meat was very tender and juicy, the gamy taste was pleasant and not overwhelming. The cured salmon was a bit stinking fishy, but the fish soup was flavorful. The pricing was very moderate and the atmosphere was chill. However when we were there for dinner, our main course didn’t come until one hour after we finished the starters.

Living room vibes, good music, and perfect plates to go with your matcha, coffee or wine. It feels intentionally small, and personal, with a seasonal menu and limited items. Sometimes they host other (vegetarian) small food businesses and events. I always drop by if I'm in the neighbourhood and would highly recommend this spot.

Amazing atmosphere - very chilled, homely and pretty cool! It also just looks quite beautiful. Super convenient location right next to the t-banna station. The staff were very friendly and helpful and service was pretty quick. The food (all vegan) was delicious - my gnocchi in tomato sauce was super simple but very moreish. My partners filled pasta (agnolotti) was excellent too. Maybe on the slightly pricier side.

Chill vibe. Loved their choice of chill music that was not of typical mainstream music. Somewhat too loud volume for a good conversation.

Vegan place. The food was not as good as non-vegan food can be, but tasted pretty ok to be vegan food. Enjoyed the pasta especially. The brownie was not as good.

Not much fika & coffee options to choose from. They had more of light snacks, salty food and drinks. So I would recommend this place more for a chill weekend lunch hangout with pasta & wine rather than a fika place.

Good food and drinks in a cozy atmosphere. The cafe has a small selection of drinks and food, but the quality of the coffee and the sandwich I had was great (and the cakes looked amazing). The inside of the cafe is opens dem full of light - a perfect combination with the nice music, great food and friendly service.

Cozy and a warm atmosphere! Great place to have a simple lunch or fika. Everything is vegan and the prices are very reasonable. Had 2 baguettes, a semla and a banana chocolate cake for 200 sek. Everything tasted really good!
Excellent cocktails and good vibe! We had the La Mandorla and the Bounty Espresso Martini. The latter was great, the La Mandorla was beyond. And they played old school hip hop. Perfect!
Nice bar, cool music and good beer. 10/10
Paloma & rök and Bounty Espresso are to die for! The staff is extremely nice, good music and vibes in general.
Great place for drinks!

Riktig mysig bar. Med härlig inredning och fantastisk atmosfär. Goda drinkar och trevlig personal.

Vi var här igår och tog ett par drinkar innan vi skulle vidare på middag.

name: Snaps

googleReviews.text: I am surprised by the lower reviews. We were visiting Sweden (for 2 weeks) from the U.S. We stopped here by chance and figured we get a burger. All 3 of us were pleasantly surprised by the quality of food as well as the service. I would rank my burger as a 9.25 out of 10... and I know my wife and son felt the same way. When we return to the area, we will be going back. Pricing is very good compared to places like Max and others and the quality is better.
Of the restaurants in the area this seems to be the most popular place. It was quite busy on a sunny Sunday early evening. But food was good and service too.

Here you can witness that its a lie that just white people can be racists. Anyone can be racists and thats what I have experienced here. Coming from London a multicultural city, for the first time I was racially discriminated for entering this place. Specifically by this person. I believe conflicts of our nations should not create issues amongst people enjoying their normal lives.

Open air outdoor seating in the square with tasty quick food. Blankets provided on chairs in the springtime. Popular spot with local residents in Sodermalm neighborhood. Seat yourself outdoors and order at the bar for food. They take cards American Express.

Unfortunately, as a black man I don’t want to post a review like this. But here I was refused any kind of service and when I went to the bathroom, I was escorted outside of the building after.

Now I  don’t want to make this bigger than it really is, but I have an issue removing patrons from your establishment when nothing wrong (physically or verbally) has been committed. Of course it’s always hard to prove (he said vs she said) but this is the first time in Europe I’ve felt wrongly accused.

The bar tenders after a certain time refused to serve me and I was escorted outside of the building by security after I visited the restroom. I was not rude, and simply complied with all the instructions given to me. As someone visiting Stockholm from North America, I felt a bit hurt. From the many European countries I have visited this is the first time where I felt ostracized for being black.
I was not belligerent, aggressive, or obnoxious to the staff but I was made to feel as if I had somehow wronged them. Unfortunately now I am leaving this review.
So please visit during the day when it’s not very busy, but in the evening for people of colour (Asian, Black, Brown or those in between) find another place for a night cap.

googleEditorialSummary: Swedish & Mediterranean dining in a lively bar/bistro within a former 17th-century palace.

googleReviews.text: The taste of bakery is very exceptional! i've tried few of famous (from social media) bakery but dissapointed with the taste (and price also very expensive). then, i come to this place without any expectation, but trust me i was amazed by the taste of the bakery (even i take away some more to bring to my home country). I will surely visit this place again if i come to Stockholm in the future :)

Don't bother calling to make inquiries in English - they hung up on me without saying a word several times when I spoke in English. All I wanted to do was to place an order to pick up a birthday cake for my daughter who was in Stockholm nearby. At first I thought the lady could not hear me because it was an overseas call. But the 3rd time, a man picked up but when I said I'm calling from overseas and would like to order a cake for my girl, all he said was STOP CALLING PLEASE.
omg.
If you are busy or do not entertain phone orders, at least just say so. I've never come across such an unpleasant and unwelcome attitude! Truly awful.
Even if we came in speaking english (of course, we’re tourists), the ladies who served us were very kind 🫶🏻. We ate the kanelbullar (cinnamon rolls) and they were exquisite!
Very cozy ❤️

I loved it, I grabbed breakfast and lunch there every morning for my stay in Stockholm and the food (especially the pastries) was exceptional. Also they half price the sandwiches from the day before which worked perfectly for my budget. The lady that served me every time was exceptionally sweet and nice. A hidden gem for someone visiting stockholm on a budget.

I definitely recommend it!
I tried yakiniku, yakitori, dumplings and sushi and everything was really good! Fresh ingredients, light food, a lot of taste, generous portions and good prices! The sushi is one of the best in the city!
The staff was also very polite and helpful.

Perfect sushi Philadelphia.
I like this place because of always good food
This is my favorite lunch spot in Solna. Delicious food for a great price.
Its a good place for Dumplings you can try for sure.. had there couple of times

I like their dumplings with shrimps or pork, they are the best.  Dumpling with vegetable is not my style. Poke bowl was also very tasty but I found other cooked food such as Teriyaki chicken was a bit too salty. Sushi is nothing special, normal Swedish style of Sushi. So mix of sushi and dumplings can be a good option to try.

Best with this place is the outdoor seating when the weather is nice. Don't order the Gyros plate! Recommend to have their Räksmörgås and the Fish and chips. We waited over and hour for our food really lousy service!

Great place in a beautiful setting. Self service restaurant with rustique beach resto food, at good price/value point. The terrace on the cliffside has you sitting among the treetops overlooking the strait. Right in central Stockholm. Calm and nice park area in residential neighborhood. Great sunsets.

Very chill and relaxed joint by the water.  The decor is simple and lively. The food is average to good. It's not delicious but it isn't as bad as the reviews are saying...
We ordered 1 Halloumiburger, 3 chili and bacon burgers, 1 grilled fillet of pork and 1 Taco. I asked every person to rate their meals and they all gave 3,5/5 except for the grilled pork rated at 5/5 - which i must absolutely confirm: the grilled pork is absolutely amazing!!! Too bad it wasn't my meal.

The meat patties in the burgers were super small and stacked in a huge bun with very little sauce. The fries were okay. Nothing fancy. The potatoe wedges were big and a dry...

As others have pointed out, the table service is minimal. So you'll have to pick & pay by yourself your own drinks at the bar and food at the kitchen.
There's live music. And the atmosphere is hippie/ bohemian - except for the bill! The food isn't fancy but has the pricetag of a fancy restaurant.
To add insult to injury,  Gentlemen had their own toilets while Ladies had to share it with Gentlemen. No offense, but the stench of urine was nauseating in that mixed toilet. The washing sink and the mirror were both located outside the toilet in a hallway right next to the entrance and behind the kitchen. So you'll have NO privacy if you need to powder your nose or floss your teeth.

I would only recommend "Solstugan" for a quick drink or a coffee.
Very good food, very nice interior design and very good service. Highly recommend! One star minus because the plastic plants in the interior.

I was eating here with my parole officer, the weather was super nice so we thought it be a good idea. To my surprise i come to find out there are no waiters its just a big line to order the food, this was a huge problem for me since i have a prosthetic leg. I asked if i could be seated closer to the ordering counter but he just looked at me and said no. Well, wont be going here again. Fish and chips was cooked to perfection but still L service.
Good variety on the menu and relatively normal prices for Stockholm. Helpful staff and nice atmosphere inside. It can be a bit noisy on the outside dinning area tho!

Sweden’s best brasserie! During my stay in Stockholm, I came here almost daily to eat. This is the perfect place for a combination of delicious dishes and warm, welcoming people. It was always a pleasure to dine here. In addition to their mouthwatering meals, they also offer a varied beer menu (with Westmalle Tripel and Rochefort as the cherry on top).
Thank you for the cozy evenings at one of your tables!

At a Burger once there and it was okey.

Second time I enter I was told they are not letting people have a booth except If they are 4 people.
Right then I saw only 2 occupied tables and one booth.

I left immediately then and will never come back with that attitude.

Would not recommend.
Good, fast service. Colorite staff. Everything is very good.

Quite a good surprise this place. We went there just by chance. Really friendly and nice staff. The grilled meat and the grilled tuna were delightful. The sallad also with a super nice dressing.
There's a long list of options for homemade beer.
We will definitely come back

Sospeso feels less like a café and more like being adopted into a fiercely proud Italian family, espresso first, questions later. The owner himself served up the shot, chatted about the beans, and when I commented on the music, the gleaming espresso machine, and the cannoli lineup, he grinned and said, “Everything is Italian.” And he meant it. Delightfully, his dog, who lounged like a café guardian deity, fresh from winning a trophy at a dog show, blessed the space with quiet majesty and just a touch of diva energy. If you want espresso with heart, heritage, and a side of canine excellence, this is your stop.

an old bike by a small door in Östermalm discloses one of the coziest places for a coffee: an excellent espresso machine, awesome Italian pastries and some other snacks make it a perfect spot for a fika break - or a quick take-out.
pet-friendly with some tables outside, and a nice, personal service from the staff - including the friendliest dog in the neighbourhood…

Have not tried their food/pastries yet.

Typical authentic Italian espresso bar with the atmosphere, the perfection of an espresso and the selection of pastries 👌

Recently moved in nearby, new to the neighbourhood and already feel at home.

Amazing Italian owner, coffee, pastries, and atmosphere. Best coffee I found during a 3 week stay in Stockholm. The owner’s smile welcomes you in and the taste/quality of the coffee makes you want to return daily.
Perfect place for an Italian coffee taste..the cappuccino was really good, the Cornetto too, and the company of the nicest dog ever while you are having an Italian "pausa caffè" is the real Plus!
The best espresso bar in Stockholm (and the best hot chocolate in town too). Very attentive personal and owners, creating a special atmosphere for the place.
My favorite cafe in Stockholm so far. Must try their gelato! I took pistachio and coffee tastes, the coffee flavor was amazing. I ordered Americano which had a good balance.

Bright lit, elegant interior.

Thought it was just a café, but they also served pizza, wine and gelato.

Had a single shot cappuccino. Dark roasted espresso base. Wonder if it they use beans with high ratio of Robusta, which may be very italian but not of my preference. I also felt that the milk foam was also too thin for my preference.
Sosta Bar a morning espresso bar, delicious coffe. We tried the gelato based on the recommendations and loved the sorbets. You definitely should try it. Your little Italy stop in Stockholm.

Sosta = La dolce vita! It's a premium italian deli bar with the best espresso and the best gelato in Stockholm, served by their highly professional staff.
Also, their pizzas are the best I have ever experienced, better than most of the famous pizzerias in Italy!

Great food, disappointing service
We recently had dinner at Spago, an Italian restaurant in Slussen, and I have to say that the food was absolutely excellent. Every dish we tried was rich in flavor and clearly prepared with care. We ordered the Spago truffle spaghetti, the prawn risotto, and the tomato pasta, and all three exceeded expectations. The truffle spaghetti, in particular, was beautifully creamy with just the right amount of truffle aroma, a perfect balance of indulgence without being overpowering. The risotto was cooked to perfection, creamy yet firm, and the prawns were fresh and flavorful. Even the simpler tomato pasta had that authentic, homemade taste that makes you appreciate Italian cuisine at its best.

However, what could have been a perfect dining experience was unfortunately overshadowed by the service. The staff felt distant and somewhat unwelcoming from the moment we arrived. They seemed uninterested, barely smiled, and gave off a cold, almost irritated attitude throughout the evening. While we didn’t expect over-the-top friendliness, a bit of warmth or hospitality would have gone a long way. It’s a shame, because the quality of the food truly deserves better representation on the service side.

In short, Spago in Slussen delivers top-tier Italian food in terms of taste and presentation, but the atmosphere suffers from an unfriendly service experience. If you’re going for the food alone, it’s definitely worth a visit, just don’t expect much in terms of hospitality or engagement from the staff.

Food, wine, cocktails are all great! Not really anything to complain about, we were super happy.

Pasta is possible to get gluten free, which is a big plus.
Service was good, but nothing special.

Be prepared that the restaurant is clearly more expensive than similar restaurants in Stockholm with the same quality.
Had this deliciously rich and creamy pasta which was so comforting. The waiters were warm and made us feel at home. Would definitely come here again!
Obtain a reservation. We were fortunate our hotel obtained one.. intimate, but not crowded. Able to have a conversation. Food was delicious, risotto, steak all prepared nicely. Terrific Service

we walked up this large hill from our hotel to try this restaurant. I must say it was good. I had salmon and it came with vegetables. We also had a pasta dish they give you bread, complementary and water complementary I felt it was very pricey but good they did not have English menus, but they did help us with the menus and I also use my Google to figure it out

This clearly very popular restaurant was fully booked upon our arrival. Our table was situated in the middle aisle, resulting in constant foot traffic brushing past our shoulders & table throughout the meal. Under normal circumstances, we would not have accepted our reserved table & opted for one next to the window instead; however, there were no alternative tables available, as the restaurant was exceptionally crowded. Considering how busy the restaurant was, the service 'although no frills' was excellent, attentive, and with short waiting times. Overall, as a family, we all felt in the main, the food was well-presented, accompanied by a nice bread basket. That said, my chicken dish was overly dry, and under normal circumstances, I would have returned it, if not for the desire to avoid disrupting our family occasion.  The other dishes that we ordered, the lamb, the scampi & the Lobster soups were very good. The Mozzarella and tomato starter was nice, as was the pannacotta which was delicious but rather small, others had more in the glass, so I can only make the assumption that they were running out, as they already had with creme brulee. We particularly appreciated the set-price 3-course comprehensive menu. I'd rate a well considered 3.5/5☆ for the reasons I've explained.

I recently had the pleasure of dining at Spisa hos Helena on our first day in Stockholm, and I have to say, it was an incredible experience. The highlight of the evening was undoubtedly the salmon, which was cooked to perfection and bursting with flavor. The service was equally impressive—attentive, friendly, and made us feel right at home. It was a fantastic start to our time in Stockholm, and I can't wait to go back!

Amazing food, such lovely owners and so cosy and warm on a miserable November night. It was fully packed (seemed like loads of regulars and locals) on a Thursday night and we fully understand why. Highly recommended this lovely place!

Pleasant bistro with welcoming service.
We sat outside on a warm evening.
Thoroughly enjoyed the triple menu.
Everything perfectly cooked and well presented and very tasty.  Waiter very attentive.
We look forward to eating at Spisa hos Helena in the near future and will most certainly recommend it to friends and family.

A very cozy place to have a special gastronomic experience, the waiters and the owner are very friendly and helpful for anything one might need.
The food is cooked perfectly, the products are of very high quality and the service is top notch.

The atmosphere was so warm that I decided to have a coffee there too, before going out in the cold.

I am greatly thankful for falling upon this restaurant, the best possible start for my journey in Sweden!

Only been here twice, but both times the staff seemed completely disorganised

The first time, there was no clear queuing system, so people were walking in unsure where to order. I was served at the till, while others were served at the end of the pizza station. Pizzas were piling up with staff unsure which pizza belonged to which customer, and I even saw a couple walk in, wait a long time without having their order taken, and then leave.

The second time, I was told it would be a 30-minute wait for a pizza because they were busy, which I understand — but not everyone has time to wait that long. With no manager or senior staff member to delegate tasks, it was just 2–3 young staff members with little direction.

Each time I walk past, it also seems like there’s a constant rotation of new staff. That lack of consistency clearly contributes to the poor organisation, and it had a direct impact on the customer experience I had both times. Whoever owns the place should be more hands-on, rather than leaving young, inexperienced staff — who may have only started days ago — to run things on their own.

The pizza is good, to be fair, but the service really needs improvement on busier nights.

Gosh, so delicious pizzas! You can create your own pizza with any favourites vegetables, toppings and sauces! The bread dough is so soft and tasty! Don’t miss it when you are nearby! You must go and have it! So satisfied when eating a whole pizza! Spisa beats Domino, Pizza Hut, Pizza express! Spisa deserves the award!

Food here was great, plenty of options including lots of vegetarian options and a really reasonably priced meal (cheaper than the equivalent in the UK!). They have a couple of vegetarian meat alternatives too. When we arrived the staff were very busy with online orders and asked if we could wait 10 minutes to order, by the time they were ready there was a queue of new people but they made sure to come over and invite us to order first, which was very kind of them.

When we arrived, we got an explanation of how it works. It's one price (129kr at the time of writing) and you can build you own pizza however you like (like at subway). The pizza and service were great!

Rectangular pizza place finally done right? Tasty pizzas, quickly prepared, good price, beautiful unusual interior and styling. The main downside is the service, the staff often lacks proper experience, the delivery of pizzas for takeaway is inconsistent (cheese/sauces/koriander only being offered from time to time), and sometimes may be very weird, like they didn't find my takeaway order for several minutes until it was mentioned it was made on Foodora. This was especially stressful as they have another restaurant in the chain in Solna, and their Hammarby website order button takes you to place an order there, so be careful!

Coming from London, where you can usually sit or stand anywhere in a pub, I was a bit surprised by how organised things are in Stockholm. You won’t be let in unless there’s a seat available, which initially felt unusual. I was offered a seat at the bar and planned to just have a drink, but the atmosphere was so welcoming and lively that I ended up ordering food as well.
I chose the fish and chips, and it was absolutely fantastic. I’m not usually a big fan of fish, but this one was perfectly crispy, fresh, and really delicious. It was also great value for money, especially considering how expensive Sweden can be.
I noticed a previous one-star review mentioning poor treatment from a waitress. From my experience, the place was very busy, and the waitresses and bartender were clearly working hard and likely overwhelmed. Despite that, they were kind, polite, and friendly with me throughout my visit.
Overall, I highly recommend this pub. Great atmosphere, tasty food, and good value. I’ll definitely be back.
A nice city pub with lots if regulars. I haven't tried the food yet - but any pub that serves Fuller's on tap deserves a good grade!
Breathtaking experience, local beer, amazing service, tasty deserts. The best part is nice staff, especialy beautiful young lady. Definitelly recommend.

Very good service. It was totally full when we went there. Got more quiet during our stay. We were just for drinks. Heaters on the whole outside area. Big recommendation because of service and (propably) good drink selection and the popularity of the place. I actually do not know about the drink selection because we were served to table by our asks.
Nice place, good guiness, failed to split G.

If you are going to eat in this kind of place, you should not expect fancy, Michelin star food. You want fresh ingredients, solid portion, food which fits with after work beer or wine. And that's what you will get here. Tasty, decent food for decent price. Red wine sauce with my pork - even better than expected. Also After Work beer/wine deal is very nice, really like this tradition in Stockholm. If you want to eat here, don't be shy and try, you will not be disappointed if you come with the proper mindset 😃

What a bad place i have been in Sweden so far, the worst costumer service, the bartender/waiter, he was so rude to us :/ and the food was overcooked, the halloumi was way too fried and the club sanwich was everything, but that.

We stopped here by chance. We had a great time, the service is great, and the ambiance is also.  We were with a small child and it didn't bother anyone, even she had a good time.  🤣
We will definitely come again.
Pretty good food. Very fast service and a great outdoor seating!
didn't have high expectations, was very pleasantly surprised.  wonderful place nice decor and good service.

name: Stampen

googleReviews.text: Stampen was one of the highlights of my trip to Stockholm. It had such an incredible vibe and atmosphere, with the decor and live music being fantastic!

Stampen is hands down one of the best bars I’ve ever been to. Amazing live music, jazz vibes, and a cozy, historic atmosphere that makes every night feel special. Perfect spot to dance, sing along, and soak in Stockholm’s nightlife energy—unforgettable!

It's a really lovely place. Like a little New Orleans bar.. please remember the local Swedish do not say thank you when you move or open a door for them. It's expected they are not being rude. We English just say please and thank you more that's all. So don't get offended
Great live music venue for folks of all ages.  Leo's jazz trio on Sundays is the very highest level of the genre, and you never know who will sit it.  The bar staff gives great service.

Amazing venue with great, cosy jazz and blues vibes. Easily on the top 5 list of livemusic bars in Sweden.
Completely full with people so make sure to be in early on Saturdays.

googleEditorialSummary: Nightly live jazz & blues acts in an intimate, antique-filled tavern with a 17th-century cellar bar.

googleReviews.text: We visited Stavros Taverna today and were really happy with our choice. We sat outside, really nice atmosphere and crowd,  quiet, looking in the park. We got soutzoukakia, mpriam, kolokithokeftedes and Greek salad. The chef had his own twist in traditional Greek dishes and made them special. Everything tasted excellent and with fresh ingredients. Friendly staff who checked on us if we like the food or want to order sthng. We will certainly come back!
Very good restaurant, me and my fiance tried the lamb platter and it was delicious, a bit too little potatoes but the staff gave us more for free. Wonderful experience!

I have a hard time finding genuinely good restaurants here in Stockholm so trust me when I say this food is banging! Contender for the best pommes in the entire city. Fresh ingredients, fast service, amaaaazing cocktails. The lamb shanks are great, but I got the chicken skewer my second time coming and it was a tiiiny bit dry. The baklava was also not the best, but still good. Regardless, the best Greek food I’ve had here in Sweden.

Delicious authentic greek food in Södermalm!
Friendly service and nice atmosphere!
Great tasting, perfectly cooked food. Outstanding and polite table service. Recommended.

My wife and I enjoyed a fantastic dinner here during our holiday stay in Stockholm. An intimate / romantic setting. The food was delicious and the staff was so courteous and kind. We highly recommend!
The restaurant was a no-frills true Swedish place, which was what we wanted. I had a delicious fish dish with au gratin potatoes and the kids had meatballs. Everyone was happy.

A cozy place located on Söder with a very calm avenue outside. A very good menu whit a great soul. This is a place for a romantic dinner, birthday partys and business dinner. Amazing food and very friendly staff.Its a small place so if you haven't book dont expect to get a table.

Brilliant local pub! Food was very well priced with very large portions. I had the veal schnitzel which was one of the nicest things I had eaten in a long time. Our waitress was extremely kind and attentive.
Really great restaurant, I was with my colleagues and we were all happy with the food. Delicious char fish and the portions were huge.

Strong *3,5 stars*. We had the truffle & mushroom, and nduja pizza.

Nduja was nicely cooked, had a good amount of nduja and stracciatella.

Mushroom pizza had a nice taste as well. The dough was a but underbaked for my liking.

Prices are premium. Place is nice and clean, atmosphere is a bit silent.

A needed high quality pizza in this part of town. The pizzas are tasty, the tomato sauce is rich in flavour  and the dough is crispy.

The service was super friendly and we got our pizzas relatively fast.

We'll be coming back, very often.

Good pizzas, but less friendly people.
We went there to have a pizza on a "late" Friday night. I was there around 8:40pm. The staff told us we could not eat on site as they would be closing soon. Although we promised to eat quickly, they refused our request to eat at their place.
So we ordered two dishes to go. It took us quite a long time to get our order, leaving finally the place after 9pm. Fortunately, it was summer and we were able to eat in a park near the restaurant.
If you want to eat there, go at 6pm, not at the southern European dinner time.
Great pizza! The crust was perfect and the quality was good. Customer service was ok.
While visiting Sundbyberg, I went to Stella. I ordered a Margarita pizza with beer. Good service provided by the staff. We sat at the outdoor seating.

Been to SUS four times now in the summer. It’s amazing vibe wise, and always have great events. Love the movie nights and EUROS. Drinks are eh, food is pretty good for finger food and this vibe. But always a great time.

Went and watched the euros final, lovely place. Very busy so the line was a bit long sometimes. Only 3 unisex bathrooms, and one mens with a urinal.

As a whole would say a lovely place for most gatherings, great view
Chilled rooftop bar with views across Stockholm and a very friendly feel. There was space for our large group when we turned up at 9pm on a Tuesday night. Service at the bar was very efficient.

We ordered the local cocktail. And beer!
Price were as compared to Stockholm it felt.
A bit on the pricey side, but true to its name, this rooftop bar delivers. You get a stunning 180° view of Stockholm and its surroundings. It’s the perfect spot for summer, with a golden hour vibe that’s hard to beat. The lighting and big screen add a touch of party energy, and the elegantly designed amphitheater seating makes it all the more special.

Great selection of rooftop bars with stunning views over Stockholm. There are quieter corners to relax, but also spots with music and a livelier vibe. Definitely on the pricier side, but absolutely worth it for the view. Both indoor and outdoor seating available – probably even more enjoyable on warm summer evenings than in early May.

We stopped by Stora Bageriet to try their semla (62 SEK) and cappuccino, and it was a great experience overall. 🌟 The semla was fresh and well balanced — soft bun, generous almond filling and light whipped cream. The cappuccino was also very good and paired perfectly for a proper Swedish fika.
The atmosphere is cozy and lively, with a really nice vibe. The staff were very friendly and welcoming. One of the highlights is the open bakery area — you can actually see the bakers working. We were lucky enough to watch them making semla, which made the visit even more special.
The only downside is that it gets quite busy. In winter, without the terrace, it can be difficult to find a seat.
Overall, highly recommended if you’re looking for traditional semla, good coffee and an authentic bakery experience in Stockholm. ☕️

The staff was very friendly and helpful.
The bread and pastries are freshly made, and are super delicious. One of the best croissants I have eaten in Stockholm (also the cinnamon bun).
Their concept (logo) is very original.
The space is small. If you are lucky, you find direct a table otherwise you need to wait a few minutes. There is a bit of noice due to the small dining area.
Overall, I recommend to visit the bakery.

Went early, close to opening. Great atmosphere and good selection of bakes. Went for Dansk breakfast plate and had the most amazing homemade marmalade ever (possibly blood orange marmalade). Wish I tried the cardamom or cinnamon bun, but next time!

Cute bakery with amazing selections. They serve a mix of sweet and savoury. I had the egg mayo sando and it was so good. I love a thick Swedish sourdough and these guys defiantly deliver. The coffee is also really good. The customer service is also really good and they are lovely

All the pastries and food were excellent although very expensive. The service was quick and accommodating as well as nice. The place was very busy as this is a popular bakery spot so it was difficult to find a sea and a bit crowded but once we were seated and had her food it was all right. Would recommend to pick up a quick pastry along the way or sit down at a quieter time to enjoy. We got a salmon sandwich as well as pesto mozzarella, tomato one and a cinnamon fika and they were all delicious. Would recommend the salmon one

Loved it ! Super cozy, peaceful and yummy! Also staff is so friendly! Location is so good , close to central but in nature. You can swim down there and come to cafe to grab something! And price is very good if we compare with others .
Nice place to have light lunch and bulles. Only coffee served, no cappuccino or other varieties. Nice pleasant ambience surrounded by gardens and overlooking the lake/water body.

Stora Henriksvik is a charming spot with a great ambiance and friendly staff. The indoor seating has a traditional feel, but there's also plenty of space outside, where you can relax, enjoy the sun, and take in the beautiful views of the water.
We ordered the spinach and feta pie and pancakes, and everything was very tasty!
Overall, it's a lovely place to sit back and have a delicious meal.

The coffee and the desert are amazing.

Definitely a really cosy place to go all year around.

Not in the summer you can go to the beach and enjoy a good coffee from this place.

Definitely will be back next time!

Super cute and cozy setting and location. But food was oddly greasy. Side salad were saturated in oil and had little to no other flavor… panini grilled sandwich was tasty, but the grilled bread was also saturated in oil and very greasy.

Otherwise service was friendly and reasonably speedy.

We were a group of 6 and had dinner at Röde Orm. The food was traditional Swedish cuisine and it tasted alright. We were all satisfied with what we got. Nothing special though.
Our waitress was very nice!

Just strolling by yesterday and we decided to eat an early dinner and have a drink. Fish and chips good especially the chips! My friend’s typical Swedish dish was too heavy for me but he enjoyed it.
Complimentary salad bar - small but had arugula so I loved it. Bread too. Good prices. Nice sidewalk dining. Large indoors too.
Love the liver very much, they gave out a very thick and good taste, very nice to goes with beer,5/5 recommended if u eat organs
We came here with a group of 9 and met Zeki Bey, who was very helpful. The food and service were fantastic, and the atmosphere was wonderful.
Given that it is not a very expensive place, it is very good. The food is fish and chips, Wiener Schnitzel or Pizza-type, and the interior matches it name. I liked it.

Storstugan Hellasgården is such a cozy gem! The small menu focuses on quality, and everything we tried was solid and satisfying. The atmosphere is warm and inviting — especially with the real wood fire pit crackling away inside. Perfect spot to relax, enjoy good food, and soak up that charming countryside vibe just outside the city. 🔥❄️☕
Nice place. Open chimney piece warming up after nice walk in nature. Good food quality.

It's the proper restaurant side in the gård. I got a soup for 145 kr, it tasted fine, with just enough portion. My friend got a fish with good portion for 195kr. Plenty sitting place inside. It is a decent restaurant.
We live at Hammarby Sjöstad and come here all the time. Love the seasonal produce-driven dishes and ambience!
A cozy restaurant that is surrounded by nature. I really wanted to like this place but the food was subpar. Everything we ordered was extremely salty in an unpleasant way.

Outside. On the water. h
Having great food; how does it get better?
I had their Swedish meatballs with cream sauce, lingonberries and pickled cucumber. Is there a rule in Sweden about licking the plate?

We went here for a drink and to relax by the seafront. I cannot comment on the food here but the drink I had was only okay. It was not well blended and quite bitter. The view of the seafront is beautiful and the location is central for several museums, the old town etc. The main drawback here is that the music is LOUD. Not just a little, but it felt like sitting in a nightclub and we were shouting at each other over the music despite being sat at the same table. The music is also the type of bland beach party style and extremely repetitive, so if that is not your style I recommend you go elsewhere.

We sat down in this café to have a coffee and relax, and in the end we ended up being the waiters ourselves.
This rating isn’t about the quality of the coffee, but about the service.
So, we came to the café and were greeted by a man with a hat whose one and only job is to walk you to your seat. Then you tell him what you want, and he directs you to the bar so you can place the order yourself and—wait for it—bring it to your own table.
The best part is that they even ask for a tip for the service you provided for yourself.
I’m curious what the economic logic behind this place is if they pay people to just stand around and do nothing?
Do you have any open positions so I can apply, do nothing, and still get paid?

We had a nice lunch close to the sea in the open air under the sun.
Shrimps toast very tasty and meatballs very good
I wouldn’t suggest the charcuterie, cheese, olives plate named as “tapas” because it is really small and it looks like a snack to eat in the afternoon with a good beer. For sure for around 16€ it was extremely small and not enough for lunch.

Delicious food in front of the Harbour.
The place is nice and clean. Prices are fair and as expected when you eat on front restaurants. The food came fast and looks quite good as you can tell from the picture. The waiter was nice and helpful.
Definitely recommended.

Wonderful spot for great lunches, after work, pre party or watching the sunset. The staff is always lovely. This wonderful central Bohemic place is a must visit if you’re in Stockholm between April-Oktober ⭐️

Stopped here for a serendipitous early lunch yesterday after a ride around Djurgården. A light cocktail, fantastic seafood pasta, and the best Caesar ever. good music, lovely decor, friendly fast service and so much to see along the waterfront. It was a Monday and early so wasn’t busy at all. We will definitely go back for a long lunch next time. Too deck bar looked like it will be fun in summer too.

We loved this place. Weather was perfect, food and service was great and the overall vibe was good. Although there are no weather guarantees in Stockholm I would recommend. Tuna was great. A bit pricey overall. Lots of beautiful people as well. Will come back.

The cocktails were fantastic, and we loved the pleasant atmosphere, especially with the sun shining. Pontus, the bartender, made excellent cocktails and provided great service. Although we didn't try the food, the experience was still wonderful!

I highly recommend this place!

Perfect place to chilled out, take a drink or dance on the Weekend. Perfect food and settings but 4 out of 5 as no table service like in most place in Sweeden u have to queue and order at the bar even the food!
Took hotdogs. Nice view and big tables 🌭

Visited Fågelöudde the other day when it was so hot. New owner of the Cafe. Upgrade! Interested staff, plenty of snacks, ice cream and simple dishes. Perfect if you either just want ice cream, or something more filling. Also at reasonable prices! You don't have to stand in line by ordering and getting a text message when everything was ready. Good and nice place whether you are a pensioner, family with children. Highly recommended!
Excellent service, especially now with the new owners! They are so nice and do their best to make sure you have a good time.
The food took 1 hour to arrive and prioritized people who had ordered after me before me
Very good service, soooo good food and the best location!

This restaurant serves amazing sandwiches that are sure to leave you satisfied.

As soon as you enter, you’re greeted by a delicious cheese aroma from all the grilled cheese sandwiches. The menu is on the smaller side, but it covers all the essentials you’d expect.

It was really nice sitting outside and enjoying the sandwich in good weather. The scenery is calm and relaxing, and you’re bound to enjoy the experience.

It was a bit tricky to find the right path to the restaurant at first, but once we spotted the first sign, the rest was easy.

Wow!  Shared the Chicken Bun and Nashville Chicken Bun with family!  Absolutely delicious.  Perfect crispy chicken thighs and flavors are excellent.  Fries are a side order, but perfect crisp!!  Highly recommend.  Not sure what these 2 and 3 stars reviews are about - - worth a try for sure!

We have been seating under the straight sun, without the umbrella to protect from the heat, waiting for the food for about 40 minutes.

It was dirty, food was not worth for the money at all. Portions are small. The fish and chips we got were mostly paneer and the fries were cold.

Croque monsieur was just a slice of bread with mayonnaise.

Do not recommend at all.

Great location adjacent to the Nordisk Museum and along the lake. The sandwiches are tasty and the preparation quick. Order, grab a table and your meal is ready in several minutes. Perfect for outdoor dining.

Overhyped and overpriced.
Ordered the Nashville Hot Chicken burger. It was neither spicy hot as expected or hot (temperature), cheese was still cold and chicken was barely lukewarm.
The chicken was crispy however and juicy and the dressing was ok.

Overall, very dissatisfying and disappointing. I would recommend eating a KFC zinger burger which is spicier than what I ate and at a 4th of the price.

Would recommend ppl to wait for the summer to end and then grab a sandwich at Sandhäxan instead.

We visited Sundbergs Konditori, Stockholm’s oldest café (1785), and really enjoyed our stop here. We had a sweet cinnamon-and-walnut bun (similar to a kanelbulle) with coffee, and water was provided for free. The atmosphere is full of history with chandeliers and an old-world charm, right in the heart of Gamla Stan.

A practical note: in Stockholm, public toilets are usually paid unless you are a customer. Here, non-customers can also use the restroom for 20 SEK, which is helpful to know. We also loved how fika is such a strong tradition in Stockholm—this café is the perfect place to experience it like the locals. Highly recommended!

A charming cafe with a vintage atmosphere in the Old City. You order your food at the counter and it is brought to your table. We arrived early, right at opening time, so the place was relatively empty and quiet. We ordered an omelette that came with a side salad and a bagel. The portions were large and to our liking. You can buy a cup of coffee and refill it at the coffee stand.
the oldest cafe says a lot - great refill coffee and cinnamon bun. Space is a bit cramped near the counter but overall great vibes.

Great people-watching spot. Served from the counter, good and filling salads and sandwiches, cakes looked amazing. A little dear but worth it for the vintage charm of interior, friendly staff and great location!

The oldest Cafe in Gamla Stan.
Staff is very warm and cheerful, the ambience is every more warm with an old world charm.

We had Cinnamon bun, coffee and Pastry.

Absolute must visit .
Good value for money.

One of the best services! I ordered 16 bitar sashimi but got a free extra portion of rice with my sashimi, a free swap from avocado to fish, a complimentary miso soup, and to top it all off — great prices for the city. The only downside is that the place is relatively small, but it’s perfect for one or two people dining in.

One of- if not THE best service I’ve ever seen/experienced. I ordered the fried noodles with chicken (115 SEK) and it was great. The food was good but the chicken in the chicken noodles were a bit dry, the dryness did however work well with the dish so I didn’t mind it much at all. It was perfectly spicy but they added some rice too which worked amazingly as well. The free miso soup was also surprisingly very tasty. I’d give it a higher score if I could because of the service but here we are.
This cozy place is a wonderful place to enjoy a meal. The sushi was fresh and delicious, the service was great, the staff was extremely friendly, and the cost was reasonable!

Looked for a quick bite and found this place among otherwise very busy hamburger joints in the neighborhood.
Sushi was good and included miso soup. Quick and friendly service.
Tried the 10 piece june 25 and it is still good. 119SEK

Really good sushi. Generous amount of food and perfectly thick slices of fish. This is the family sushi, suitable for 3-4 people. The rolls have the best fish and veg to rice ratio. Amazing service on top of that! Very friendly staff and talented sushi chef. Top tier sushi!

Sushi Kultur - A Swedish sushi fusion

As a Swede, we love sauce. We want sauce on everything. This is also included but not limited to sushi.

On a first glance, the presentation is pretty nice, but on a first bite you understand that this sushi was made to please the Swedish tongue. It was a bit too much sauce for me, which distance itself from how original sushi tastes like.

Don’t get me wrong, it wasn’t bad - But if you are looking for traditional sushi, you’re at the wrong place

We had a biiiiig takeaway order : 50 piece set and 2 sets of maki deluxe and 1 rice dish which were done fairly fast ! Everything was nicely packed for easy transport. We were super delighted by the freshness, the creativity and how delicious it was !! Definitely one of my favorite sushi place in Stockholm 🥰

The best sushi I Stockholm by far. Surprisingly the part that impressed me the most was actually the miso soup It had a much more prevalent dashi tast to it. The presentation was also stunning with physialis on the plate. 10/10 I really recommend that you eat here!!

Clean place, but rude staff. The women’s behind the counter barely consider us. Could help us with the menu.
Recommend for take out because the food is good but my god do something regarding welcoming properly the guest.
Overly decorated and with too many sauces and extras. Drowns out the taste of the fish and the sauces go on the simple pieces also. Outside of that the nigiri is weirdly shaped.

Great food, you order from screens and the wait wasn’t to long for the food. The only issue I had was that they forgot my drink and when I asked they said they would come out with it but never did. After we were finish with our meal I asked again and they made it in a to-go cup.
The atmosphere is what you imagine from a restaurant in the middle of the walk area but you can se that they made effort into making it nice. Would recommend especially for lunch
Great to have lunch here. Best california maki in Solna🍣

Me and my boyfriend stepped in restaurant outside the mall. The waiter, today May 30th at 20:05 told us to go away and find another sushi because he was “busy” preparing sushi. Who would ever possibly guessed that at a sushi place someone would have to prepare sushi with such an arrogance.
By the way, there were two people in the shop.
My coupon didn’t work in the app but I emailed customer care and they sent me a code.
For some reason they have good prices and yet nice sushi at the same time 😀 I am a regular customer.

Such friendly staff! Possibly the owners themselves. The space is very inviting with many options for fika, light meals and even a few wines & beers.

I had some lovely soup of the day, a carrot and sweet potato blend with delicious buttered bread. And treated myself to their homemade carrot cake and glass 🍷 so a well-rounded evening to power me through my deadlines.

The real indulgence is nabbing the royal seats in the window throne 😄
One of our favorite places in Stockholm! We go there for family breakfast or lunch! Staff is very hospitable . Excellent selection of menus! Highly recommended! Wish you all the best! ❤️

One of the best cafès in Stockholm and  they certainly have the best poached egg! Cosy, bohemian atmosphere with great breakfast! During spring and summer one can also sit outside.

Uppdate, based on the last two visits:

As usual we got Egg Florentine. The sauce was salty.

The other time poached egg was undercooked.

I guess there is a change in the kitchen. We will give a break going there.

Pity, cause their standard was high in the past.

It’s a small cafe on a corner between two streets. The host was so welcoming and polite. She explained that this place is 95 years old started by two sisters that’s why the name Anderson sisters. There’s an old coffee maker at the entrance which is more symbolic than used. But a true summer siesta cafe with great coffee and great service.

🥐 Bakery is nice, and cinnamon bun was great. Not too sweet, and it was not dried.
☕️I had Chai latte and it was good. Served in a glass so it was a bit hot to grab but not a complain! I liked their Brew cafe as it was not too sour or bitter. It smelled great too.

👩‍💻Good environment for work a bit, or study. White noisy is just perfect level at this place. But I wouldn’t work more than 2-4 hours max as the seats are not for long time sitting. Just writing a blog post or journal could be perfect. :)

Small & cozy place with vegetarian set lunch menue, costing 145 kr. Food was tasty but everything was cold (by design).
Food is served with machatea shot and bred + spread.

Westernized Chai late costs 45 kr, and were made from real ingredients rather than concentrate substance.
Popular with takeaways.

My most reliable and regular lunch order on a working day. I love that there's something new to look forward to everyday with the meal of the day; The quantities are very generous, ingredients are super fresh and preparations are unique and flavourful; choose the meal of the day and you won't be disappointed. cute decor and excellent service too.

One gem of a place. I have been avoiding eating out ever since pandemic hit, but recently tried coffee and veg takeaway lunch. Highly recommend it.

Would love to sit there because the place feels very cozy and warm but wait for pandemic to be over.
A small little gem on a side street in Stockholm Great Great food mostly vegetarian and vegan friendly personal service @
Great selection of lunch dishes. Delicious food and drinks in a beautiful and calm environment.
Nice little pub. Cheap beer and good food.

The best thing about södra hörnan is that they don't send Swedish football, and the beer is slightly more expensive than the cheapest pubs. As a result, they avoid the drunks and football fans that unfortunately roam these parts of Stockholm. Still cheap beer and food that is will over average for this kind of pub.

Went to this restaurant because they had a kids menu, we could watch soccer (the world cup), and I could get a beer. I had BBQ ribs and got way more food then I needed. The ribs tasted more than OK and the beer was not that pricey. My kid wanted to go back the day after because of the pan and that we could watch the world cup. Never did that though, maybe next trip to Stockholm as the place was quite cosy. Guess you have to expect one or two over intoxicated old person, however.

This is my local pub. Everyone I take here loves it.  Why? Well it has plenty of booth seating and it's a mellow place to get bar food and drinks. Nothing special but for me that's a good thing. It's never  loud or filled with annoying hipsters. At least not yet.
Excellent food, big portions. Social distancing was good, few people insede the place. They speak Swedish and English.

My family and I are Arab and we got the Mixed Platter,the shawarma plate and the shawarma warp which was incredibly authentic. We  food was top-notch, and the atmosphere was beautiful and very welcoming. I highly recommend it to everyone.
I ordered wrap with chicken and it was so delicious! Special sauce inside the wrap was light and tasty, quite original and dainty. Burger was nice as well, smth like kebab cutlets inside🙌👍☺️

Quite Place.
Delicious Food.
Sufficient portion.
A bit higher in pricing, maybe just because of the location or anything.
But good to go.

The food was really okayish. Things that needed to be served hot, were also served cold and after a while it was extremely difficult to digest them. The server was good, the location is good and interiors are spacious too. Some vegetarian and vegan options available as well. I would say there are better places in this area, but if you really want to try it, then you can.
Food mix Maze was great. A little bit cold so I reduced one star. Rice and meat could be served warmer. Staff very firendly.
Cool atmosphere with good musics. Friendly staff. Portions were more than enough. My favorite dishes pastel de choclo and yuca frita.

It's a great example of chilean food, both home made dishes and street food. As chileans we go there when are homesick.
I recommend the Goloza table, to try a bit of everything.
Pastel de Choclo is a rather unique and great chilean dish, and can be vegetarian if you call in advance.

Probably one of the best South American eateries in Stockholm. Even the chicken from the kids menu was amazing. We had a combo of the various dishes and none of them disappointed. The service was friendly and efficient as well.
Very nice Chilean restaurant with a good vibe and food. That's all u need.
Best place to feel the Chilean spirit, food and its culture in on place. Definitely a place to have one your "todo list" while in stockholm.

name: Tak

googleReviews.text: The taste of everything including cocktails, snack, starters, and mains were superb; kudos to the chef! The portion sizes and service were satisfactory. We had a lovely night, which of course I expected nothing less with such price range :)

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Tak Stockholm is an absolute dream and the ultimate high-end escape perched right above the city center with the most breathtaking 360 degree views of the Royal Palace and the stunning rooftops 🌇✨ The vibe is pure sophistication and I am completely obsessed with the creative Swedish Japanese fusion where every single dish is a masterpiece of Nordic ingredients and Japanese precision 🍣🥢 Sipping a signature cocktail on the rooftop terrace while the sun sets over the water is a total movie moment and the energy is just so chic and vibrant 🍹🎧

The interior is a sleek masterpiece of Scandinavian design and the staff are literal pros who make the whole experience feel so special and effortless 🥂💖 Whether you are hitting the Izakaya for casual small plates or indulging in a full fine dining feast this place is a total ten out of ten and the undisputed crown jewel of Stockholm’s nightlife 🌟🙌✨

☘️🕯🇸🇪✨️🇻🇦🙏🏻✝️💚👑🐎🕊🫂🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿

I wanted suprise my girlfriend with a nice dinner. I thought of many fancy resturants, and im so dissapointed. The steak was like chewing gum, even after we asked them to reamake it. It was the opposite of a enjoyable suprise. For the price (wich wasnt cheap) you should be able to be able to get a nice steak. The service was decent but the menu and food need to be seriously reconsidered. If you only have 5 main courses, and its an exclusive resurant you need to make them excelent. We have given the resturant more than 1 chance and they still havent lived up to the expectation. We will never revisit this resturant again.

We made a reservation for the julbord and were very excited to try the Japanese twist on the traditional julbord. We’ve dined in the restaurant before and loved the experience, which made us even more eager to come back for this special evening.

The buffet itself was excellent — the variety was impressive and everything was delicious. The sushi and sashimi were absolute highlights. The warm dishes and meats were good, though not as memorable as the seafood.

The cocktails were fantastic, and the sake tasting was a real treat. The information shared during the tasting added a lot to the experience.

Unfortunately, the service was noticeably low and quite disorganized. We arrived before the group seated next to us, yet their drink order was taken much earlier than ours. No one explained to us where to go to get food, leaving us sitting awkwardly for a while. Throughout the evening, no one came to check on us — surprising, especially since the area wasn’t busy.

Considering there is a 500 kr/person ($80 CAD per person) cancellation penalty, we expected a much more attentive and well-organized experience.

We were also seated at a communal table with another group — initially two people and then a third who joined. They were loud and discussing topics that didn’t fit the romantic date night we had planned. The group mentioned that they didn’t even have a reservation and just walked in, which made our seating situation even more disappointing given that we had booked in advance.

The note mentioned “Izakaya,” but that doesn’t automatically mean shared seating — even izakayas in Japan offer private tables — so we didn’t anticipate being placed with strangers.

We’ve been to many julbords over the years as we travel very frequently to Sweden, and not once have we been seated at a communal table. It was the first time, and unfortunately it negatively affected the experience.

Overall, while the food and drinks were very good, the disorganized service and unexpected communal seating significantly took away from what we hoped would be a special evening.

The food overall was pretty good, also like their fussion concept, but the sirloin even though cooked medium rare, tasted a bit burned and at some parts was a bit chewy. I would also recommend ordering 2x of each snack. For desert I had the cinnamon bun with amaretto ice cream was really good but it was a special thing since today was the cinnamon day.

googleEditorialSummary: Stylish restaurant featuring a Swedish-Japanese menu, plus sake, a robust wine list & a rooftop bar.

googleReviews.text: Second time here in the last two years (two visits) and just love it. The food and wine are decent prices ($7 USD for a glass of rose) for Sweden. And the views are awesome! Ample seating during lunchtime and after work it’s not too bad either. I recommend the salmon wrap, shrimp salad and the olives if you’re looking for some nibbles. Great spot to bring friends who haven’t been to Sweden before!

Very good view and amazing ambience. Nice seating, not many options to try food wise but the vibe is too good.
I asked the bar person to make a sweet cocktail for me and it was very good indeed. Had pickled tomatoes with mozarella and oyesters.

It’s a really nice location. The view from here over Stockholm is amazing. All drinks we’ve ordered tasted really good. Their prices are a bit higher for sure, but it’s worth the view. The bar is located on the 9th floor. I can recommend their Longdrink called “Garden Smash”.
Looking forward to my next visit there.
